  29. 29Everyone who has left houses, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, will receive one hundred times, and will inherit eternal life.
  30. 30But many will be last who are first; and first who are last.

Chapter 20

  1. 1“For the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who was the master of a household, who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
  2. 2When he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
  3. 3He went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace.
  4. 4He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.’ So they went their way.
  5. 5Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did likewise.
  6. 6About the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle. He said to them, ‘Why do you stand here all day idle?’
  7. 7“They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ “He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and you will receive whatever is right.’
  8. 8When evening had come, the lord of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning from the last to the first.’
  9. 9“When those who were hired at about the eleventh hour came, they each received a denarius.
  10. 10When the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise each received a denarius.
  11. 11When they received it, they murmured against the master of the household,
  12. 12saying, ‘These last have spent one hour, and you have made them equal to us, who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat!’
  13. 13“But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Didn’t you agree with me for a denarius?
  14. 14Take that which is yours, and go your way. It is my desire to give to this last just as much as to you.
  15. 15Isn’t it lawful for me to do what I want to with what I own? Or is your eye evil, because I am good?’
  16. 16So the last will be first, and the first last. For many are called, but few are chosen.”
  17. 17As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside, and on the way he said to them,
  18. 18“Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death,
  19. 19and will hand him over to the Gentiles to mock, to scourge, and to crucify; and the third day he will be raised up.”
  20. 20Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, kneeling and asking a certain thing of him.
  21. 21He said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Command that these, my two sons, may sit, one on your right hand, and one on your left hand, in your Kingdom.”
  22. 22But Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?” They said to him, “We are able.”
  23. 23He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with, but to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it is for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
  24. 24When the ten heard it, they were indignant with the two brothers.
  25. 25But Jesus summoned them, and said, “You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
  26. 26It shall not be so among you, but whoever desires to become great among you shall be your servant.
  27. 27Whoever desires to be first among you shall be your bondservant,
  28. 28even as the Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
  29. 29As they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.
  30. 30Behold, two blind men sitting by the road, when they heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”
  31. 31The multitude rebuked them, telling them that they should be quiet, but they cried out even more, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”
  32. 32Jesus stood still, and called them, and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
  33. 33They told him, “Lord, that our eyes may be opened.”
  34. 34Jesus, being moved with compassion, touched their eyes; and immediately their eyes received their sight, and they followed him.

Chapter 21

  1. 1When they came near to Jerusalem, and came to Bethsphage, to the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
  2. 2saying to them, “Go into the village that is opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Untie them, and bring them to me.
  3. 3If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and immediately he will send them.”
  4. 4All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying,
  5. 5“Tell the daughter of Zion, behold, your King comes to you, humble, and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.”
  6. 6The disciples went, and did just as Jesus commanded them,
  7. 7and brought the donkey and the colt, and laid their clothes on them; and he sat on them.
  8. 8A very great multitude spread their clothes on the road. Others cut branches from the trees, and spread them on the road.
  9. 9The multitudes who went in front of him, and those who followed, kept shouting, “Hosanna to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
  10. 10When he had come into Jerusalem, all the city was stirred up, saying, “Who is this?”
  11. 11The multitudes said, “This is the prophet, Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.”
  12. 12Jesus entered into the temple of God, and drove out all of those who sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables and the seats of those who sold the doves.
  13. 13He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you have made it a den of robbers!”
  14. 14The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
  15. 15But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children who were crying in the temple and saying, “Hosanna to the son of David!” they were indignant,
  16. 16and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes. Did you never read, ‘Out of the mouth of babes and nursing babies you have perfected praise?’ ”
  17. 17He left them, and went out of the city to Bethany, and camped there.
  18. 18Now in the morning, as he returned to the city, he was hungry.
  19. 19Seeing a fig tree by the road, he came to it, and found nothing on it but leaves. He said to it, “Let there be no fruit from you forever!” Immediately the fig tree withered away.
  20. 20When the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, “How did the fig tree immediately wither away?”
  21. 21Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, if you have faith, and don’t doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you told this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ it would be done.
  22. 22All things, whatever you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.”
  23. 23When he had come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority do you do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
  24. 24Jesus answered them, “I also will ask you one question, which if you tell me, I likewise will tell you by what authority I do these things.
  25. 25The baptism of John, where was it from? From heaven or from men?” They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
  26. 26But if we say, ‘From men,’ we fear the multitude, for all hold John as a prophet.”
  27. 27They answered Jesus, and said, “We don’t know.” He also said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
  28. 28But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first, and said, ‘Son, go work today in my vineyard.’
  29. 29He answered, ‘I will not,’ but afterward he changed his mind, and went.
  30. 30He came to the second, and said the same thing. He answered, ‘I’m going, sir,’ but he didn’t go.
  31. 31Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said to him, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering into God’s Kingdom before you.
  32. 32For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you didn’t believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him. When you saw it, you didn’t even repent afterward, that you might believe him.
  33. 33“Hear another parable. There was a man who was a master of a household, who planted a vineyard, set a hedge about it, dug a wine press in it, built a tower, leased it out to farmers, and went into another country.
  34. 34When the season for the fruit came near, he sent his servants to the farmers, to receive his fruit.
  35. 35The farmers took his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
  36. 36Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they treated them the same way.
  37. 37But afterward he sent to them his son, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
  38. 38But the farmers, when they saw the son, said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and seize his inheritance.’
  39. 39So they took him, and threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
  40. 40When therefore the lord of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those farmers?”
  41. 41They told him, “He will miserably destroy those miserable men, and will lease out the vineyard to other farmers, who will give him the fruit in its season.”
  42. 42Jesus said to them, “Did you never read in the Scriptures, ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the head of the corner. This was from the Lord. It is marvelous in our eyes?’
  43. 43“Therefore I tell you, God’s Kingdom will be taken away from you, and will be given to a nation producing its fruit.
  44. 44He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but on whomever it will fall, it will scatter him as dust.”
  45. 45When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they perceived that he spoke about them.
  46. 46When they sought to seize him, they feared the multitudes, because they considered him to be a prophet.

Chapter 22

  1. 1Jesus answered and spoke to them again in parables, saying,
  2. 2“The Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king, who made a wedding feast for his son,
  3. 3and sent out his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.
  4. 4Again he sent out other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “Behold, I have prepared my dinner. My cattle and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding feast!” ’
  5. 5But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his own farm, another to his merchandise,
  6. 6and the rest grabbed his servants, and treated them shamefully, and killed them.
  7. 7When the king heard that, he was angry, and sent his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
  8. 8“Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding is ready, but those who were invited weren’t worthy.
  9. 9Go therefore to the intersections of the highways, and as many as you may find, invite to the wedding feast.’
  10. 10Those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together as many as they found, both bad and good. The wedding was filled with guests.
  11. 11But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man who didn’t have on wedding clothing,
  12. 12and he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you come in here not wearing wedding clothing?’ He was speechless.
  13. 13Then the king said to the servants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, take him away, and throw him into the outer darkness. That is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.’
  14. 14For many are called, but few chosen.”
  15. 15Then the Pharisees went and took counsel how they might entrap him in his talk.
  16. 16They sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and teach the way of God in truth, no matter whom you teach, for you aren’t partial to anyone.
  17. 17Tell us therefore, what do you think? Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”
  18. 18But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, “Why do you test me, you hypocrites?
  19. 19Show me the tax money.” They brought to him a denarius.
  20. 20He asked them, “Whose is this image and inscription?”
  21. 21They said to him, “Caesar’s.” Then he said to them, “Give therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
  22. 22When they heard it, they marveled, and left him, and went away.
  23. 23On that day Sadducees (those who say that there is no resurrection) came to him. They asked him,
  24. 24saying, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.’
  25. 25Now there were with us seven brothers. The first married and died, and having no offspring left his wife to his brother.
  26. 26In the same way, the second also, and the third, to the seventh.
  27. 27After them all, the woman died.
  28. 28In the resurrection therefore, whose wife will she be of the seven? For they all had her.”
  29. 29But Jesus answered them, “You are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God.
  30. 30For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like God’s angels in heaven.
  31. 31But concerning the resurrection of the dead, haven’t you read that which was spoken to you by God, saying,
  32. 32‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?’ God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”
  33. 33When the multitudes heard it, they were astonished at his teaching.
  34. 34But the Pharisees, when they heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, gathered themselves together.
  35. 35One of them, a lawyer, asked him a question, testing him.
  36. 36“Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the law?”
  37. 37Jesus said to him, “ ‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’
  38. 38This is the first and great commandment.
  39. 39A second likewise is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
  40. 40The whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments.”
  41. 41Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question,
  42. 42saying, “What do you think of the Christ? Whose son is he?” They said to him, “Of David.”
  43. 43He said to them, “How then does David in the Spirit call him Lord, saying,
  44. 44‘The Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet?’
  45. 45“If then David calls him Lord, how is he his son?”
  46. 46No one was able to answer him a word, neither did any man dare ask him any more questions from that day forward.

Chapter 23

  1. 1Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples,
  2. 2saying, “The scribes and the Pharisees sat on Moses’ seat.
  3. 3All things therefore whatever they tell you to observe, observe and do, but don’t do their works; for they say, and don’t do.
  4. 4For they bind heavy burdens that are grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not lift a finger to help them.
  5. 5But they do all their works to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries broad, enlarge the fringes of their garments,
  6. 6and love the place of honor at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues,
  7. 7the salutations in the marketplaces, and to be called ‘Rabbi, Rabbi’ by men.
  8. 8But don’t you be called ‘Rabbi,’ for one is your teacher, the Christ, and all of you are brothers.
  9. 9Call no man on the earth your father, for one is your Father, he who is in heaven.
  10. 10Neither be called masters, for one is your master, the Christ.
  11. 11But he who is greatest among you will be your servant.
  12. 12Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
  13. 13“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows’ houses, and as a pretense you make long prayers. Therefore you will receive greater condemnation.
  14. 14“But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you shut up the Kingdom of Heaven against men; for you don’t enter in yourselves, neither do you allow those who are entering in to enter.
  15. 15Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel around by sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of Gehenna as yourselves.
  16. 16“Woe to you, you blind guides, who say, ‘Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple, he is obligated.’
  17. 17You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifies the gold?
  18. 18‘Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on it, he is obligated?’
  19. 19You blind fools! For which is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifies the gift?
  20. 20He therefore who swears by the altar, swears by it, and by everything on it.
  21. 21He who swears by the temple, swears by it, and by him who has been living in it.
  22. 22He who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God, and by him who sits on it.
  23. 23“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and have left undone the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faith. But you ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.
  24. 24You blind guides, who strain out a gnat, and swallow a camel!
  25. 25“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and unrighteousness.
  26. 26You blind Pharisee, first clean the inside of the cup and of the platter, that its outside may become clean also.
  27. 27“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitened tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but inwardly are full of dead men’s bones, and of all uncleanness.
  28. 28Even so you also outwardly appear righteous to men, but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.
  29. 29“Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and decorate the tombs of the righteous,
  30. 30and say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we wouldn’t have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.’
  31. 31Therefore you testify to yourselves that you are children of those who killed the prophets.
  32. 32Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.
  33. 33You serpents, you offspring of vipers, how will you escape the judgment of Gehenna?
  34. 34Therefore behold, I send to you prophets, wise men, and scribes. Some of them you will kill and crucify; and some of them you will scourge in your synagogues, and persecute from city to city;
  35. 35that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zachariah son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the sanctuary and the altar.
  36. 36Most certainly I tell you, all these things will come upon this generation.
  37. 37“Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets, and stones those who are sent to her! How often I would have gathered your children together, even as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, and you would not!
  38. 38Behold, your house is left to you desolate.
  39. 39For I tell you, you will not see me from now on, until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ”

Chapter 24

  1. 1Jesus went out from the temple, and was going on his way. His disciples came to him to show him the buildings of the temple.
  2. 2But he answered them, “You see all of these things, don’t you? Most certainly I tell you, there will not be left here one stone on another, that will not be thrown down.”
  3. 3As he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign of your coming, and of the end of the age?”
  4. 4Jesus answered them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.
  5. 5For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’ and will lead many astray.
  6. 6You will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you aren’t troubled, for all this must happen, but the end is not yet.
  7. 7For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there will be famines, plagues, and earthquakes in various places.
  8. 8But all these things are the beginning of birth pains.
  9. 9Then they will deliver you up to oppression, and will kill you. You will be hated by all of the nations for my name’s sake.
  10. 10Then many will stumble, and will deliver up one another, and will hate one another.
  11. 11Many false prophets will arise, and will lead many astray.
  12. 12Because iniquity will be multiplied, the love of many will grow cold.
  13. 13But he who endures to the end will be saved.
  14. 14This Good News of the Kingdom will be preached in the whole world for a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come.
  15. 15“When, therefore, you see the abomination of desolation, which was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
  16. 16then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
  17. 17Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take out the things that are in his house.
  18. 18Let him who is in the field not return back to get his clothes.
  19. 19But woe to those who are with child and to nursing mothers in those days!
  20. 20Pray that your flight will not be in the winter, nor on a Sabbath,
  21. 21for then there will be great suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, nor ever will be.
  22. 22Unless those days had been shortened, no flesh would have been saved. But for the sake of the chosen ones, those days will be shortened.
  23. 23“Then if any man tells you, ‘Behold, here is the Christ,’ or, ‘There,’ don’t believe it.
  24. 24For there will arise false christs, and false prophets, and they will show great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.
  25. 25“Behold, I have told you beforehand.
  26. 26If therefore they tell you, ‘Behold, he is in the wilderness,’ don’t go out; or ‘Behold, he is in the inner rooms,’ don’t believe it.
  27. 27For as the lightning flashes from the east, and is seen even to the west, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.
  28. 28For wherever the carcass is, that is where the vultures gather together.
  29. 29But immediately after the suffering of those days, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken;
  30. 30and then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky. Then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of the sky with power and great glory.
  31. 31He will send out his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from one end of the sky to the other.
  32. 32“Now from the fig tree learn this parable. When its branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near.
  33. 33Even so you also, when you see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.
  34. 34Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away, until all these things are accomplished.
  35. 35Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
  36. 36But no one knows of that day and hour, not even the angels of heaven, but my Father only.
  37. 37“As the days of Noah were, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.
  38. 38For as in those days which were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ship,
  39. 39and they didn’t know until the flood came, and took them all away, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.
  40. 40Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and one will be left.
  41. 41Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and one will be left.
  42. 42Watch therefore, for you don’t know in what hour your Lord comes.
  43. 43But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have watched, and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
  44. 44Therefore also be ready, for in an hour that you don’t expect, the Son of Man will come.
  45. 45“Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his lord has set over his household, to give them their food in due season?
  46. 46Blessed is that servant whom his lord finds doing so when he comes.
  47. 47Most certainly I tell you that he will set him over all that he has.
  48. 48But if that evil servant should say in his heart, ‘My lord is delaying his coming,’
  49. 49and begins to beat his fellow servants, and eat and drink with the drunkards,
  50. 50the lord of that servant will come in a day when he doesn’t expect it, and in an hour when he doesn’t know it,
  51. 51and will cut him in pieces, and appoint his portion with the hypocrites. That is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.

Chapter 25

  1. 1“Then the Kingdom of Heaven will be like ten virgins, who took their lamps, and went out to meet the bridegroom.
  2. 2Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
  3. 3Those who were foolish, when they took their lamps, took no oil with them,
  4. 4but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
  5. 5Now while the bridegroom delayed, they all slumbered and slept.
  6. 6But at midnight there was a cry, ‘Behold! The bridegroom is coming! Come out to meet him!’
  7. 7Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.
  8. 8The foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
  9. 9But the wise answered, saying, ‘What if there isn’t enough for us and you? You go rather to those who sell, and buy for yourselves.’
  10. 10While they went away to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was shut.
  11. 11Afterward the other virgins also came, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us.’
  12. 12But he answered, ‘Most certainly I tell you, I don’t know you.’
  13. 13Watch therefore, for you don’t know the day nor the hour in which the Son of Man is coming.
  14. 14“For it is like a man, going into another country, who called his own servants, and entrusted his goods to them.
  15. 15To one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one; to each according to his own ability. Then he went on his journey.
  16. 16Immediately he who received the five talents went and traded with them, and made another five talents.
  17. 17In the same way, he also who got the two gained another two.
  18. 18But he who received the one talent went away and dug in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.
  19. 19“Now after a long time the lord of those servants came, and reconciled accounts with them.
  20. 20He who received the five talents came and brought another five talents, saying, ‘Lord, you delivered to me five talents. Behold, I have gained another five talents in addition to them.’
  21. 21“His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a few things, I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’
  22. 22“He also who got the two talents came and said, ‘Lord, you delivered to me two talents. Behold, I have gained another two talents in addition to them.’
  23. 23“His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a few things, I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’
  24. 24“He also who had received the one talent came and said, ‘Lord, I knew you that you are a hard man, reaping where you didn’t sow, and gathering where you didn’t scatter.
  25. 25I was afraid, and went away and hid your talent in the earth. Behold, you have what is yours.’
  26. 26“But his lord answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant. You knew that I reap where I didn’t sow, and gather where I didn’t scatter.
  27. 27You ought therefore to have deposited my money with the bankers, and at my coming I should have received back my own with interest.
  28. 28Take away therefore the talent from him, and give it to him who has the ten talents.
  29. 29For to everyone who has will be given, and he will have abundance, but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away.
  30. 30Throw out the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
  31. 31“But when the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.
  32. 32Before him all the nations will be gathered, and he will separate them one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
  33. 33He will set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.
  34. 34Then the King will tell those on his right hand, ‘Come, blessed of my Father, inherit the Kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;
  35. 35for I was hungry, and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty, and you gave me drink. I was a stranger, and you took me in.
  36. 36I was naked, and you clothed me. I was sick, and you visited me. I was in prison, and you came to me.’
  37. 37“Then the righteous will answer him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, and feed you; or thirsty, and give you a drink?
  38. 38When did we see you as a stranger, and take you in; or naked, and clothe you?
  39. 39When did we see you sick, or in prison, and come to you?’
  40. 40“The King will answer them, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you did it to one of the least of these my brothers, you did it to me.’
  41. 41Then he will say also to those on the left hand, ‘Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire which is prepared for the devil and his angels;
  42. 42for I was hungry, and you didn’t give me food to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave me no drink;
  43. 43I was a stranger, and you didn’t take me in; naked, and you didn’t clothe me; sick, and in prison, and you didn’t visit me.’
  44. 44“Then they will also answer, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and didn’t help you?’
  45. 45“Then he will answer them, saying, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you didn’t do it to one of the least of these, you didn’t do it to me.’
  46. 46These will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”

Chapter 26

  1. 1When Jesus had finished all these words, he said to his disciples,
  2. 2“You know that after two days the Passover is coming, and the Son of Man will be delivered up to be crucified.”
  3. 3Then the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders of the people were gathered together in the court of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas.
  4. 4They took counsel together that they might take Jesus by deceit, and kill him.
  5. 5But they said, “Not during the feast, lest a riot occur among the people.”
  6. 6Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
  7. 7a woman came to him having an alabaster jar of very expensive ointment, and she poured it on his head as he sat at the table.
  8. 8But when his disciples saw this, they were indignant, saying, “Why this waste?
  9. 9For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.”
  10. 10However, knowing this, Jesus said to them, “Why do you trouble the woman? She has done a good work for me.
  11. 11For you always have the poor with you; but you don’t always have me.
  12. 12For in pouring this ointment on my body, she did it to prepare me for burial.
  13. 13Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be spoken of as a memorial of her.”
  14. 14Then one of the twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests,
  15. 15and said, “What are you willing to give me, that I should deliver him to you?” They weighed out for him thirty pieces of silver.
  16. 16From that time he sought opportunity to betray him.
  17. 17Now on the first day of unleavened bread, the disciples came to Jesus, saying to him, “Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover?”
  18. 18He said, “Go into the city to a certain person, and tell him, ‘The Teacher says, “My time is at hand. I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.” ’ ”
  19. 19The disciples did as Jesus commanded them, and they prepared the Passover.
  20. 20Now when evening had come, he was reclining at the table with the twelve disciples.
  21. 21As they were eating, he said, “Most certainly I tell you that one of you will betray me.”
  22. 22They were exceedingly sorrowful, and each began to ask him, “It isn’t me, is it, Lord?”
  23. 23He answered, “He who dipped his hand with me in the dish will betray me.
  24. 24The Son of Man goes, even as it is written of him, but woe to that man through whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”
  25. 25Judas, who betrayed him, answered, “It isn’t me, is it, Rabbi?” He said to him, “You said it.”
  26. 26As they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks for it, and broke it. He gave to the disciples, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body.”
  27. 27He took the cup, gave thanks, and gave to them, saying, “All of you drink it,
  28. 28for this is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many for the remission of sins.
  29. 29But I tell you that I will not drink of this fruit of the vine from now on, until that day when I drink it anew with you in my Father’s Kingdom.”
  30. 30When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
  31. 31Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’
  32. 32But after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”
  33. 33But Peter answered him, “Even if all will be made to stumble because of you, I will never be made to stumble.”
  34. 34Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you that tonight, before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.”
  35. 35Peter said to him, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you.” All of the disciples also said likewise.
  36. 36Then Jesus came with them to a place called Gethsemane, and said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I go there and pray.”
  37. 37He took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and severely troubled.
  38. 38Then he said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here, and watch with me.”
  39. 39He went forward a little, fell on his face, and prayed, saying, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass away from me; nevertheless, not what I desire, but what you desire.”
  40. 40He came to the disciples, and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “What, couldn’t you watch with me for one hour?
  41. 41Watch and pray, that you don’t enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
  42. 42Again, a second time he went away, and prayed, saying, “My Father, if this cup can’t pass away from me unless I drink it, your desire be done.”
  43. 43He came again and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.
  44. 44He left them again, went away, and prayed a third time, saying the same words.
  45. 45Then he came to his disciples, and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. Behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
  46. 46Arise, let’s be going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”
  47. 47While he was still speaking, behold, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and elders of the people.
  48. 48Now he who betrayed him gave them a sign, saying, “Whoever I kiss, he is the one. Seize him.”
  49. 49Immediately he came to Jesus, and said, “Hail, Rabbi!” and kissed him.
  50. 50Jesus said to him, “Friend, why are you here?” Then they came and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
  51. 51Behold, one of those who were with Jesus stretched out his hand, and drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and struck off his ear.
  52. 52Then Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its place, for all those who take the sword will die by the sword.
  53. 53Or do you think that I couldn’t ask my Father, and he would even now send me more than twelve legions of angels?
  54. 54How then would the Scriptures be fulfilled that it must be so?”
  55. 55In that hour Jesus said to the multitudes, “Have you come out as against a robber with swords and clubs to seize me? I sat daily in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me.
  56. 56But all this has happened that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.” Then all the disciples left him and fled.
  57. 57Those who had taken Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were gathered together.
  58. 58But Peter followed him from a distance, to the court of the high priest, and entered in and sat with the officers, to see the end.
  59. 59Now the chief priests, the elders, and the whole council sought false testimony against Jesus, that they might put him to death;
  60. 60and they found none. Even though many false witnesses came forward, they found none. But at last two false witnesses came forward,
  61. 61and said, “This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to build it in three days.’ ”
  62. 62The high priest stood up, and said to him, “Have you no answer? What is this that these testify against you?”
  63. 63But Jesus held his peace. The high priest answered him, “I adjure you by the living God, that you tell us whether you are the Christ, the Son of God.”
  64. 64Jesus said to him, “You have said it. Nevertheless, I tell you, after this you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming on the clouds of the sky.”
  65. 65Then the high priest tore his clothing, saying, “He has spoken blasphemy! Why do we need any more witnesses? Behold, now you have heard his blasphemy.
  66. 66What do you think?” They answered, “He is worthy of death!”
  67. 67Then they spat in his face and beat him with their fists, and some slapped him,
  68. 68saying, “Prophesy to us, you Christ! Who hit you?”
  69. 69Now Peter was sitting outside in the court, and a maid came to him, saying, “You were also with Jesus, the Galilean!”
  70. 70But he denied it before them all, saying, “I don’t know what you are talking about.”
  71. 71When he had gone out onto the porch, someone else saw him, and said to those who were there, “This man also was with Jesus of Nazareth.”
  72. 72Again he denied it with an oath, “I don’t know the man.”
  73. 73After a little while those who stood by came and said to Peter, “Surely you are also one of them, for your speech makes you known.”
  74. 74Then he began to curse and to swear, “I don’t know the man!” Immediately the rooster crowed.
  75. 75Peter remembered the word which Jesus had said to him, “Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.” Then he went out and wept bitterly.

Chapter 27

  1. 1Now when morning had come, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:
  2. 2and they bound him, and led him away, and delivered him up to Pontius Pilate, the governor.
  3. 3Then Judas, who betrayed him, when he saw that Jesus was condemned, felt remorse, and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
  4. 4saying, “I have sinned in that I betrayed innocent blood.” But they said, “What is that to us? You see to it.”
  5. 5He threw down the pieces of silver in the sanctuary, and departed. He went away and hanged himself.
  6. 6The chief priests took the pieces of silver, and said, “It’s not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is the price of blood.”
  7. 7They took counsel, and bought the potter’s field with them, to bury strangers in.
  8. 8Therefore that field was called “The Field of Blood” to this day.
  9. 9Then that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying, “They took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him upon whom a price had been set, whom some of the children of Israel priced,
  10. 10and they gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord commanded me.”
  11. 11Now Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, “Are you the King of the Jews?” Jesus said to him, “So you say.”
  12. 12When he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.
  13. 13Then Pilate said to him, “Don’t you hear how many things they testify against you?”
  14. 14He gave him no answer, not even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.
  15. 15Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release to the multitude one prisoner, whom they desired.
  16. 16They had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.
  17. 17When therefore they were gathered together, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, or Jesus, who is called Christ?”
  18. 18For he knew that because of envy they had delivered him up.
  19. 19While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, “Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered many things today in a dream because of him.”
  20. 20Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the multitudes to ask for Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.
  21. 21But the governor answered them, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” They said, “Barabbas!”
  22. 22Pilate said to them, “What then shall I do to Jesus, who is called Christ?” They all said to him, “Let him be crucified!”
  23. 23But the governor said, “Why? What evil has he done?” But they cried out exceedingly, saying, “Let him be crucified!”
  24. 24So when Pilate saw that nothing was being gained, but rather that a disturbance was starting, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, “I am innocent of the blood of this righteous person. You see to it.”
  25. 25All the people answered, “May his blood be on us, and on our children!”
  26. 26Then he released to them Barabbas, but Jesus he flogged and delivered to be crucified.
  27. 27Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium, and gathered the whole garrison together against him.
  28. 28They stripped him, and put a scarlet robe on him.
  29. 29They braided a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and a reed in his right hand; and they kneeled down before him, and mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
  30. 30They spat on him, and took the reed and struck him on the head.
  31. 31When they had mocked him, they took the robe off him, and put his clothes on him, and led him away to crucify him.
  32. 32As they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name, and they compelled him to go with them, that he might carry his cross.
  33. 33When they came to a place called “Golgotha”, that is to say, “The place of a skull,”
  34. 34they gave him sour wine to drink mixed with gall. When he had tasted it, he would not drink.
  35. 35When they had crucified him, they divided his clothing among them, casting lots,
  36. 36and they sat and watched him there.
  37. 37They set up over his head the accusation against him written, “THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
  38. 38Then there were two robbers crucified with him, one on his right hand and one on the left.
  39. 39Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads,
  40. 40and saying, “You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross!”
  41. 41Likewise the chief priests also mocking, with the scribes, the Pharisees, and the elders, said,
  42. 42“He saved others, but he can’t save himself. If he is the King of Israel, let him come down from the cross now, and we will believe in him.
  43. 43He trusts in God. Let God deliver him now, if he wants him; for he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’ ”
  44. 44The robbers also who were crucified with him cast on him the same reproach.
  45. 45Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land until the ninth hour.
  46. 46About the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, “Eli, Eli, lima sabachthani?” That is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
  47. 47Some of them who stood there, when they heard it, said, “This man is calling Elijah.”
  48. 48Immediately one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him a drink.
  49. 49The rest said, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to save him.”
  50. 50Jesus cried again with a loud voice, and yielded up his spirit.
  51. 51Behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom. The earth quaked and the rocks were split.
  52. 52The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
  53. 53and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection, they entered into the holy city and appeared to many.
  54. 54Now the centurion, and those who were with him watching Jesus, when they saw the earthquake, and the things that were done, feared exceedingly, saying, “Truly this was the Son of God.”
  55. 55Many women were there watching from afar, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, serving him.
  56. 56Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
  57. 57When evening had come, a rich man from Arimathaea, named Joseph, who himself was also Jesus’ disciple came.
  58. 58This man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given up.
  59. 59Joseph took the body, and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
  60. 60and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had cut out in the rock, and he rolled a great stone against the door of the tomb, and departed.
  61. 61Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb.
  62. 62Now on the next day, which was the day after the Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees were gathered together to Pilate,
  63. 63saying, “Sir, we remember what that deceiver said while he was still alive: ‘After three days I will rise again.’
  64. 64Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest perhaps his disciples come at night and steal him away, and tell the people, ‘He is risen from the dead;’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”
  65. 65Pilate said to them, “You have a guard. Go, make it as secure as you can.”
  66. 66So they went with the guard and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone.

Chapter 28

  1. 1Now after the Sabbath, as it began to dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb.
  2. 2Behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from the sky, and came and rolled away the stone from the door, and sat on it.
  3. 3His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
  4. 4For fear of him, the guards shook, and became like dead men.
  5. 5The angel answered the women, “Don’t be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus, who has been crucified.
  6. 6He is not here, for he has risen, just like he said. Come, see the place where the Lord was lying.
  7. 7Go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has risen from the dead, and behold, he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him.’ Behold, I have told you.”
  8. 8They departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to bring his disciples word.
  9. 9As they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, “Rejoice!” They came and took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.
  10. 10Then Jesus said to them, “Don’t be afraid. Go tell my brothers that they should go into Galilee, and there they will see me.”
  11. 11Now while they were going, behold, some of the guards came into the city, and told the chief priests all the things that had happened.
  12. 12When they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave a large amount of silver to the soldiers,
  13. 13saying, “Say that his disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
  14. 14If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him and make you free of worry.”
  15. 15So they took the money and did as they were told. This saying was spread abroad among the Jews, and continues until today.
  16. 16But the eleven disciples went into Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had sent them.
  17. 17When they saw him, they bowed down to him, but some doubted.
  18. 18Jesus came to them and spoke to them, saying, “All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth.
  19. 19Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
  20. 20teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.
2

Mark

Gospel

Chapter 1

  1. 1The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
  2. 2As it is written in the prophets, “Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you:
  3. 3the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord! Make his paths straight!’ ”
  4. 4John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching the baptism of repentance for forgiveness of sins.
  5. 5All the country of Judea and all those of Jerusalem went out to him. They were baptized by him in the Jordan river, confessing their sins.
  6. 6John was clothed with camel’s hair and a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
  7. 7He preached, saying, “After me comes he who is mightier than I, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and loosen.
  8. 8I baptized you in water, but he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.”
  9. 9In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
  10. 10Immediately coming up from the water, he saw the heavens parting, and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
  11. 11A voice came out of the sky, “You are my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”
  12. 12Immediately the Spirit drove him out into the wilderness.
  13. 13He was there in the wilderness forty days tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals; and the angels were serving him.
  14. 14Now after John was taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Good News of God’s Kingdom,
  15. 15and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and God’s Kingdom is at hand! Repent, and believe in the Good News.”
  16. 16Passing along by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew, the brother of Simon, casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
  17. 17Jesus said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you into fishers for men.”
  18. 18Immediately they left their nets, and followed him.
  19. 19Going on a little further from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.
  20. 20Immediately he called them, and they left their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired servants, and went after him.
  21. 21They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught.
  22. 22They were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as having authority, and not as the scribes.
  23. 23Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out,
  24. 24saying, “Ha! What do we have to do with you, Jesus, you Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”
  25. 25Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!”
  26. 26The unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.
  27. 27They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching? For with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!”
  28. 28The report of him went out immediately everywhere into all the region of Galilee and its surrounding area.
  29. 29Immediately, when they had come out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
  30. 30Now Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever, and immediately they told him about her.
  31. 31He came and took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her immediately, and she served them.
  32. 32At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to him all who were sick, and those who were possessed by demons.
  33. 33All the city was gathered together at the door.
  34. 34He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. He didn’t allow the demons to speak, because they knew him.
  35. 35Early in the morning, while it was still dark, he rose up and went out, and departed into a deserted place, and prayed there.
  36. 36Simon and those who were with him searched for him.
  37. 37They found him and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.”
  38. 38He said to them, “Let’s go elsewhere into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because I came out for this reason.”
  39. 39He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out demons.
  40. 40A leper came to him, begging him, kneeling down to him, and saying to him, “If you want to, you can make me clean.”
  41. 41Being moved with compassion, he stretched out his hand, and touched him, and said to him, “I want to. Be made clean.”
  42. 42When he had said this, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean.
  43. 43He strictly warned him, and immediately sent him out,
  44. 44and said to him, “See you say nothing to anybody, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”
  45. 45But he went out, and began to proclaim it much, and to spread about the matter, so that Jesus could no more openly enter into a city, but was outside in desert places. People came to him from everywhere.

Chapter 2

  1. 1When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.
  2. 2Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them.
  3. 3Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him.
  4. 4When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.
  5. 5Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”
  6. 6But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
  7. 7“Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
  8. 8Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?
  9. 9Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’
  10. 10But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—
  11. 11“I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”
  12. 12He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”
  13. 13He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.
  14. 14As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he arose and followed him.
  15. 15He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.
  16. 16The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”
  17. 17When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
  18. 18John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”
  19. 19Jesus said to them, “Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast.
  20. 20But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in that day.
  21. 21No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made.
  22. 22No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.”
  23. 23He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain.
  24. 24The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”
  25. 25He said to them, “Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry—he, and those who were with him?
  26. 26How he entered into God’s house at the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?”
  27. 27He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
  28. 28Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”

Chapter 3

  1. 1He entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man there who had his hand withered.
  2. 2They watched him, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him.
  3. 3He said to the man who had his hand withered, “Stand up.”
  4. 4He said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?” But they were silent.
  5. 5When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other.
  6. 6The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
  7. 7Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples, and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea,
  8. 8from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.
  9. 9He spoke to his disciples that a little boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they wouldn’t press on him.
  10. 10For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him.
  11. 11The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, “You are the Son of God!”
  12. 12He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.
  13. 13He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him.
  14. 14He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach,
  15. 15and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:
  16. 16Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
  17. 17James the son of Zebedee; and John, the brother of James, (whom he called Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder);
  18. 18Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot;
  19. 19and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him. Then he came into a house.
  20. 20The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
  21. 21When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him; for they said, “He is insane.”
  22. 22The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons.”
  23. 23He summoned them, and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
  24. 24If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
  25. 25If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
  26. 26If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end.
  27. 27But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder unless he first binds the strong man; then he will plunder his house.
  28. 28Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;
  29. 29but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.”
  30. 30—because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
  31. 31His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.
  32. 32A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, “Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters are outside looking for you.”
  33. 33He answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
  34. 34Looking around at those who sat around him, he said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!
  35. 35For whoever does the will of God is my brother, my sister, and mother.”

Chapter 4

  1. 1Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.
  2. 2He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,
  3. 3“Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,
  4. 4and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds came and devoured it.
  5. 5Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.
  6. 6When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
  7. 7Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
  8. 8Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”
  9. 9He said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.”
  10. 10When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.
  11. 11He said to them, “To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,
  12. 12that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’ ”
  13. 13He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?
  14. 14The farmer sows the word.
  15. 15The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.
  16. 16These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.
  17. 17They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.
  18. 18Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,
  19. 19and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
  20. 20Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”
  21. 21He said to them, “Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand?
  22. 22For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.
  23. 23If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.”
  24. 24He said to them, “Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.
  25. 25For whoever has, to him more will be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.”
  26. 26He said, “God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,
  27. 27and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, though he doesn’t know how.
  28. 28For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.
  29. 29But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”
  30. 30He said, “How will we liken God’s Kingdom? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?
  31. 31It’s like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,
  32. 32yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow.”
  33. 33With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.
  34. 34Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.
  35. 35On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side.”
  36. 36Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.
  37. 37A big wind storm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.
  38. 38He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?”
  39. 39He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
  40. 40He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?”
  41. 41They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

Chapter 5

  1. 1They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.
  2. 2When he had come out of the boat, immediately a man with an unclean spirit met him out of the tombs.
  3. 3He lived in the tombs. Nobody could bind him any more, not even with chains,
  4. 4because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him, and the fetters broken in pieces. Nobody had the strength to tame him.
  5. 5Always, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he was crying out, and cutting himself with stones.
  6. 6When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him,
  7. 7and crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don’t torment me.”
  8. 8For he said to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”
  9. 9He asked him, “What is your name?” He said to him, “My name is Legion, for we are many.”
  10. 10He begged him much that he would not send them away out of the country.
  11. 11Now on the mountainside there was a great herd of pigs feeding.
  12. 12All the demons begged him, saying, “Send us into the pigs, that we may enter into them.”
  13. 13At once Jesus gave them permission. The unclean spirits came out and entered into the pigs. The herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and they were drowned in the sea.
  14. 14Those who fed them fled, and told it in the city and in the country. The people came to see what it was that had happened.
  15. 15They came to Jesus, and saw him who had been possessed by demons sitting, clothed, and in his right mind, even him who had the legion; and they were afraid.
  16. 16Those who saw it declared to them what happened to him who was possessed by demons, and about the pigs.
  17. 17They began to beg him to depart from their region.
  18. 18As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.
  19. 19He didn’t allow him, but said to him, “Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you.”
  20. 20He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.
  21. 21When Jesus had crossed back over in the boat to the other side, a great multitude was gathered to him; and he was by the sea.
  22. 22Behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name, came; and seeing him, he fell at his feet,
  23. 23and begged him much, saying, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Please come and lay your hands on her, that she may be made healthy, and live.”
  24. 24He went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they pressed upon him on all sides.
  25. 25A certain woman, who had a discharge of blood for twelve years,
  26. 26and had suffered many things by many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was no better, but rather grew worse,
  27. 27having heard the things concerning Jesus, came up behind him in the crowd, and touched his clothes.
  28. 28For she said, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be made well.”
  29. 29Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.
  30. 30Immediately Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd, and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
  31. 31His disciples said to him, “You see the multitude pressing against you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’ ”
  32. 32He looked around to see her who had done this thing.
  33. 33But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had been done to her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
  34. 34He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be cured of your disease.”
  35. 35While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler’s house saying, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?”
  36. 36But Jesus, when he heard the message spoken, immediately said to the ruler of the synagogue, “Don’t be afraid, only believe.”
  37. 37He allowed no one to follow him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.
  38. 38He came to the synagogue ruler’s house, and he saw an uproar, weeping, and great wailing.
  39. 39When he had entered in, he said to them, “Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep.”
  40. 40They ridiculed him. But he, having put them all out, took the father of the child, her mother, and those who were with him, and went in where the child was lying.
  41. 41Taking the child by the hand, he said to her, “Talitha cumi!” which means, being interpreted, “Girl, I tell you, get up!”
  42. 42Immediately the girl rose up and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.
  43. 43He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and commanded that something should be given to her to eat.

Chapter 6

  1. 1He went out from there. He came into his own country, and his disciples followed him.
  2. 2When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things?” and, “What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands?
  3. 3Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judah, and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” They were offended at him.
  4. 4Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and among his own relatives, and in his own house.”
  5. 5He could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people, and healed them.
  6. 6He marveled because of their unbelief. He went around the villages teaching.
  7. 7He called to himself the twelve, and began to send them out two by two; and he gave them authority over the unclean spirits.
  8. 8He commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a staff only: no bread, no wallet, no money in their purse,
  9. 9but to wear sandals, and not put on two tunics.
  10. 10He said to them, “Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.
  11. 11Whoever will not receive you nor hear you, as you depart from there, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony against them. Assuredly, I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!”
  12. 12They went out and preached that people should repent.
  13. 13They cast out many demons, and anointed many with oil who were sick, and healed them.
  14. 14King Herod heard this, for his name had become known, and he said, “John the Baptizer has risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him.”
  15. 15But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”
  16. 16But Herod, when he heard this, said, “This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead.”
  17. 17For Herod himself had sent out and arrested John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, for he had married her.
  18. 18For John said to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
  19. 19Herodias set herself against him, and desired to kill him, but she couldn’t,
  20. 20for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and kept him safe. When he heard him, he did many things, and he heard him gladly.
  21. 21Then a convenient day came, that Herod on his birthday made a supper for his nobles, the high officers, and the chief men of Galilee.
  22. 22When the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those sitting with him. The king said to the young lady, “Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you.”
  23. 23He swore to her, “Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom.”
  24. 24She went out, and said to her mother, “What shall I ask?” She said, “The head of John the Baptizer.”
  25. 25She came in immediately with haste to the king, and asked, “I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptizer on a platter.”
  26. 26The king was exceedingly sorry, but for the sake of his oaths, and of his dinner guests, he didn’t wish to refuse her.
  27. 27Immediately the king sent out a soldier of his guard, and commanded to bring John’s head, and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
  28. 28and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.
  29. 29When his disciples heard this, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
  30. 30The apostles gathered themselves together to Jesus, and they told him all things, whatever they had done, and whatever they had taught.
  31. 31He said to them, “You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile.” For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
  32. 32They went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.
  33. 33They saw them going, and many recognized him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to him.
  34. 34Jesus came out, saw a great multitude, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.
  35. 35When it was late in the day, his disciples came to him, and said, “This place is deserted, and it is late in the day.
  36. 36Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages, and buy themselves bread, for they have nothing to eat.”
  37. 37But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give them something to eat?”
  38. 38He said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go see.” When they knew, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
  39. 39He commanded them that everyone should sit down in groups on the green grass.
  40. 40They sat down in ranks, by hundreds and by fifties.
  41. 41He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves, and he gave to his disciples to set before them, and he divided the two fish among them all.
  42. 42They all ate, and were filled.
  43. 43They took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and also of the fish.
  44. 44Those who ate the loaves were five thousand men.
  45. 45Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he himself sent the multitude away.
  46. 46After he had taken leave of them, he went up the mountain to pray.
  47. 47When evening had come, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.
  48. 48Seeing them distressed in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea, and he would have passed by them,
  49. 49but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;
  50. 50for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he immediately spoke with them, and said to them, “Cheer up! It is I! Don’t be afraid.”
  51. 51He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;
  52. 52for they hadn’t understood about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
  53. 53When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.
  54. 54When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,
  55. 55and ran around that whole region, and began to bring those who were sick, on their mats, to where they heard he was.
  56. 56Wherever he entered, into villages, or into cities, or into the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might just touch the fringe of his garment; and as many as touched him were made well.

Chapter 7

  1. 1Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes gathered together to him, having come from Jerusalem.
  2. 2Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is unwashed, hands, they found fault.
  3. 3(For the Pharisees and all the Jews don’t eat unless they wash their hands and forearms, holding to the tradition of the elders.
  4. 4They don’t eat when they come from the marketplace unless they bathe themselves, and there are many other things, which they have received to hold to: washings of cups, pitchers, bronze vessels, and couches.)
  5. 5The Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why don’t your disciples walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with unwashed hands?”
