  33. 33They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”
  34. 34He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them?
  35. 35But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days.”
  36. 36He also told a parable to them. “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.
  37. 37No one puts new wine into old wine skins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.
  38. 38But new wine must be put into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.
  39. 39No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’ ”

Chapter 6

  1. 1Now on the second Sabbath after the first, he was going through the grain fields. His disciples plucked the heads of grain and ate, rubbing them in their hands.
  2. 2But some of the Pharisees said to them, “Why do you do that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath day?”
  3. 3Jesus, answering them, said, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, he, and those who were with him;
  4. 4how he entered into God’s house, and took and ate the show bread, and gave also to those who were with him, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests alone?”
  5. 5He said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”
  6. 6It also happened on another Sabbath that he entered into the synagogue and taught. There was a man there, and his right hand was withered.
  7. 7The scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see whether he would heal on the Sabbath, that they might find an accusation against him.
  8. 8But he knew their thoughts; and he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Rise up, and stand in the middle.” He arose and stood.
  9. 9Then Jesus said to them, “I will ask you something: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?”
  10. 10He looked around at them all, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his hand was restored as sound as the other.
  11. 11But they were filled with rage, and talked with one another about what they might do to Jesus.
  12. 12In these days, he went out to the mountain to pray, and he continued all night in prayer to God.
  13. 13When it was day, he called his disciples, and from them he chose twelve, whom he also named apostles:
  14. 14Simon, whom he also named Peter; Andrew, his brother; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;
  15. 15Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called the Zealot;
  16. 16Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who also became a traitor.
  17. 17He came down with them, and stood on a level place, with a crowd of his disciples, and a great number of the people from all Judea and Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases;
  18. 18as well as those who were troubled by unclean spirits, and they were being healed.
  19. 19All the multitude sought to touch him, for power came out of him and healed them all.
  20. 20He lifted up his eyes to his disciples, and said, “Blessed are you who are poor, God’s Kingdom is yours.
  21. 21Blessed are you who hunger now, for you will be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.
  22. 22Blessed are you when men hate you, and when they exclude and mock you, and throw out your name as evil, for the Son of Man’s sake.
  23. 23Rejoice in that day, and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in heaven, for their fathers did the same thing to the prophets.
  24. 24“But woe to you who are rich! For you have received your consolation.
  25. 25Woe to you, you who are full now, for you will be hungry. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.
  26. 26Woe, when men speak well of you, for their fathers did the same thing to the false prophets.
  27. 27“But I tell you who hear: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
  28. 28bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.
  29. 29To him who strikes you on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him who takes away your cloak, don’t withhold your coat also.
  30. 30Give to everyone who asks you, and don’t ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.
  31. 31“As you would like people to do to you, do exactly so to them.
  32. 32If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.
  33. 33If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.
  34. 34If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive back as much.
  35. 35But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; and your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.
  36. 36“Therefore be merciful, even as your Father is also merciful.
  37. 37Don’t judge, and you won’t be judged. Don’t condemn, and you won’t be condemned. Set free, and you will be set free.
  38. 38“Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you. For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.”
  39. 39He spoke a parable to them. “Can the blind guide the blind? Won’t they both fall into a pit?
  40. 40A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.
  41. 41Why do you see the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?
  42. 42Or how can you tell your brother, ‘Brother, let me remove the speck of chaff that is in your eye,’ when you yourself don’t see the beam that is in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye.
  43. 43For there is no good tree that produces rotten fruit; nor again a rotten tree that produces good fruit.
  44. 44For each tree is known by its own fruit. For people don’t gather figs from thorns, nor do they gather grapes from a bramble bush.
  45. 45The good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings out that which is good, and the evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart brings out that which is evil, for out of the abundance of the heart, his mouth speaks.
  46. 46“Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and don’t do the things which I say?
  47. 47Everyone who comes to me, and hears my words, and does them, I will show you who he is like.
  48. 48He is like a man building a house, who dug and went deep, and laid a foundation on the rock. When a flood arose, the stream broke against that house, and could not shake it, because it was founded on the rock.
  49. 49But he who hears, and doesn’t do, is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the stream broke, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.”

Chapter 7

  1. 1After he had finished speaking in the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.
  2. 2A certain centurion’s servant, who was dear to him, was sick and at the point of death.
  3. 3When he heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and save his servant.
  4. 4When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy for you to do this for him,
  5. 5for he loves our nation, and he built our synagogue for us.”
  6. 6Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof.
  7. 7Therefore I didn’t even think myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.
  8. 8For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
  9. 9When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel.”
  10. 10Those who were sent, returning to the house, found that the servant who had been sick was well.
  11. 11Soon afterwards, he went to a city called Nain. Many of his disciples, along with a great multitude, went with him.
  12. 12Now when he came near to the gate of the city, behold, one who was dead was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. Many people of the city were with her.
  13. 13When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, “Don’t cry.”
  14. 14He came near and touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still. He said, “Young man, I tell you, arise!”
  15. 15He who was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.
  16. 16Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited his people!”
  17. 17This report went out concerning him in the whole of Judea, and in all the surrounding region.
  18. 18The disciples of John told him about all these things.
  19. 19John, calling to himself two of his disciples, sent them to Jesus, saying, “Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?”
  20. 20When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptizer has sent us to you, saying, ‘Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?’ ”
  21. 21In that hour he cured many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits; and to many who were blind he gave sight.
  22. 22Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you have seen and heard: that the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.
  23. 23Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”
  24. 24When John’s messengers had departed, he began to tell the multitudes about John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
  25. 25But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.
  26. 26But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.
  27. 27This is he of whom it is written, ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’
  28. 28“For I tell you, among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer, yet he who is least in God’s Kingdom is greater than he.”
  29. 29When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John’s baptism.
  30. 30But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.
  31. 31“To what then should I compare the people of this generation? What are they like?
  32. 32They are like children who sit in the marketplace, and call to one another, saying, ‘We piped to you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned, and you didn’t weep.’
  33. 33For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’
  34. 34The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man, and a drunkard; a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
  35. 35Wisdom is justified by all her children.”
  36. 36One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered into the Pharisee’s house, and sat at the table.
  37. 37Behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster jar of ointment.
  38. 38Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.
  39. 39Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner.”
  40. 40Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.” He said, “Teacher, say on.”
  41. 41“A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
  42. 42When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?”
  43. 43Simon answered, “He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most.” He said to him, “You have judged correctly.”
  44. 44Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head.
  45. 45You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.
  46. 46You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.
  47. 47Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But one to whom little is forgiven, loves little.”
  48. 48He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
  49. 49Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
  50. 50He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

Chapter 8

  1. 1Soon afterwards, he went about through cities and villages, preaching and bringing the good news of God’s Kingdom. With him were the twelve,
  2. 2and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;
  3. 3and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod’s steward; Susanna; and many others; who served them from their possessions.
  4. 4When a great multitude came together, and people from every city were coming to him, he spoke by a parable.
  5. 5“The farmer went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some fell along the road, and it was trampled under foot, and the birds of the sky devoured it.
  6. 6Other seed fell on the rock, and as soon as it grew, it withered away, because it had no moisture.
  7. 7Other fell amid the thorns, and the thorns grew with it, and choked it.
  8. 8Other fell into the good ground, and grew, and produced one hundred times as much fruit.” As he said these things, he called out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”
  9. 9Then his disciples asked him, “What does this parable mean?”
  10. 10He said, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of God’s Kingdom, but to the rest in parables; that ‘seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.’
  11. 11Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
  12. 12Those along the road are those who hear, then the devil comes, and takes away the word from their heart, that they may not believe and be saved.
  13. 13Those on the rock are they who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; but these have no root, who believe for a while, then fall away in time of temptation.
  14. 14That which fell among the thorns, these are those who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with cares, riches, and pleasures of life, and bring no fruit to maturity.
  15. 15Those in the good ground, these are those who with an honest and good heart, having heard the word, hold it tightly, and produce fruit with perseverance.
  16. 16“No one, when he has lit a lamp, covers it with a container, or puts it under a bed; but puts it on a stand, that those who enter in may see the light.
  17. 17For nothing is hidden that will not be revealed; nor anything secret that will not be known and come to light.
  18. 18Be careful therefore how you hear. For whoever has, to him will be given; and whoever doesn’t have, from him will be taken away even that which he thinks he has.”
  19. 19His mother and brothers came to him, and they could not come near him for the crowd.
  20. 20Some people told him, “Your mother and your brothers stand outside, desiring to see you.”
  21. 21But he answered them, “My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God, and do it.”
  22. 22Now on one of those days, he entered into a boat, himself and his disciples, and he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side of the lake.” So they launched out.
  23. 23But as they sailed, he fell asleep. A wind storm came down on the lake, and they were taking on dangerous amounts of water.
  24. 24They came to him, and awoke him, saying, “Master, master, we are dying!” He awoke, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water, and they ceased, and it was calm.
  25. 25He said to them, “Where is your faith?” Being afraid they marveled, saying to one another, “Who is this then, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?”
  26. 26They arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is opposite Galilee.
  27. 27When Jesus stepped ashore, a certain man out of the city who had demons for a long time met him. He wore no clothes, and didn’t live in a house, but in the tombs.
  28. 28When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, “What do I have to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I beg you, don’t torment me!”
  29. 29For Jesus was commanding the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For the unclean spirit had often seized the man. He was kept under guard, and bound with chains and fetters. Breaking the bonds apart, he was driven by the demon into the desert.
  30. 30Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He said, “Legion,” for many demons had entered into him.
  31. 31They begged him that he would not command them to go into the abyss.
  32. 32Now there was there a herd of many pigs feeding on the mountain, and they begged him that he would allow them to enter into those. Then he allowed them.
  33. 33The demons came out of the man, and entered into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake, and were drowned.
  34. 34When those who fed them saw what had happened, they fled and told it in the city and in the country.
  35. 35People went out to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting at Jesus’ feet, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
  36. 36Those who saw it told them how he who had been possessed by demons was healed.
  37. 37All the people of the surrounding country of the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them, for they were very much afraid. Then he entered into the boat and returned.
  38. 38But the man from whom the demons had gone out begged him that he might go with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,
  39. 39“Return to your house, and declare what great things God has done for you.” He went his way, proclaiming throughout the whole city what great things Jesus had done for him.
  40. 40When Jesus returned, the multitude welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
  41. 41Behold, a man named Jairus came. He was a ruler of the synagogue. He fell down at Jesus’ feet, and begged him to come into his house,
  42. 42for he had an only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she was dying. But as he went, the multitudes pressed against him.
  43. 43A woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years, who had spent all her living on physicians and could not be healed by any
  44. 44came behind him, and touched the fringe of his cloak. Immediately the flow of her blood stopped.
  45. 45Jesus said, “Who touched me?” When all denied it, Peter and those with him said, “Master, the multitudes press and jostle you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’”
  46. 46But Jesus said, “Someone did touch me, for I perceived that power has gone out of me.”
  47. 47When the woman saw that she was not hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him declared to him in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.
  48. 48He said to her, “Daughter, cheer up. Your faith has made you well. Go in peace.”
  49. 49While he still spoke, one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house came, saying to him, “Your daughter is dead. Don’t trouble the Teacher.”
  50. 50But Jesus hearing it, answered him, “Don’t be afraid. Only believe, and she will be healed.”
  51. 51When he came to the house, he didn’t allow anyone to enter in, except Peter, John, James, the father of the child, and her mother.
  52. 52All were weeping and mourning her, but he said, “Don’t weep. She isn’t dead, but sleeping.”
  53. 53They were ridiculing him, knowing that she was dead.
  54. 54But he put them all outside, and taking her by the hand, he called, saying, “Child, arise!”
  55. 55Her spirit returned, and she rose up immediately. He commanded that something be given to her to eat.
  56. 56Her parents were amazed, but he commanded them to tell no one what had been done.

Chapter 9

  1. 1He called the twelve together, and gave them power and authority over all demons, and to cure diseases.
  2. 2He sent them out to preach God’s Kingdom and to heal the sick.
  3. 3He said to them, “Take nothing for your journey—no staffs, nor wallet, nor bread, nor money. Don’t have two coats each.
  4. 4Into whatever house you enter, stay there, and depart from there.
  5. 5As many as don’t receive you, when you depart from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them.”
  6. 6They departed and went throughout the villages, preaching the Good News and healing everywhere.
  7. 7Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him; and he was very perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,
  8. 8and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.
  9. 9Herod said, “I beheaded John, but who is this about whom I hear such things?” He sought to see him.
  10. 10The apostles, when they had returned, told him what things they had done. He took them and withdrew apart to a desert region of a city called Bethsaida.
  11. 11But the multitudes, perceiving it, followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them of God’s Kingdom, and he cured those who needed healing.
  12. 12The day began to wear away; and the twelve came and said to him, “Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms, and lodge, and get food, for we are here in a deserted place.”
  13. 13But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.” They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we should go and buy food for all these people.”
  14. 14For they were about five thousand men. He said to his disciples, “Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”
  15. 15They did so, and made them all sit down.
  16. 16He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to the sky, he blessed them, broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.
  17. 17They ate and were all filled. They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over.
  18. 18As he was praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he asked them, “Who do the multitudes say that I am?”
  19. 19They answered, “ ‘John the Baptizer,’ but others say, ‘Elijah,’ and others, that one of the old prophets has risen again.”
  20. 20He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”
  21. 21But he warned them, and commanded them to tell this to no one,
  22. 22saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.”
  23. 23He said to all, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
  24. 24For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake, will save it.
  25. 25For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world, and loses or forfeits his own self?
  26. 26For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of Man be ashamed, when he comes in his glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels.
  27. 27But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here who will in no way taste of death until they see God’s Kingdom.”
  28. 28About eight days after these sayings, he took with him Peter, John, and James, and went up onto the mountain to pray.
  29. 29As he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became white and dazzling.
  30. 30Behold, two men were talking with him, who were Moses and Elijah,
  31. 31who appeared in glory, and spoke of his departure, which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.
  32. 32Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep, but when they were fully awake, they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.
  33. 33As they were parting from him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah,” not knowing what he said.
  34. 34While he said these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered into the cloud.
  35. 35A voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!”
  36. 36When the voice came, Jesus was found alone. They were silent, and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.
  37. 37On the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a great multitude met him.
  38. 38Behold, a man from the crowd called out, saying, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.
  39. 39Behold, a spirit takes him, he suddenly cries out, and it convulses him so that he foams, and it hardly departs from him, bruising him severely.
  40. 40I begged your disciples to cast it out, and they couldn’t.”
  41. 41Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”
  42. 42While he was still coming, the demon threw him down and convulsed him violently. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
  43. 43They were all astonished at the majesty of God. But while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did, he said to his disciples,
  44. 44“Let these words sink into your ears, for the Son of Man will be delivered up into the hands of men.”
  45. 45But they didn’t understand this saying. It was concealed from them, that they should not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.
  46. 46An argument arose among them about which of them was the greatest.
  47. 47Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their hearts, took a little child, and set him by his side,
  48. 48and said to them, “Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me. Whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For whoever is least among you all, this one will be great.”
  49. 49John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow with us.”
  50. 50Jesus said to him, “Don’t forbid him, for he who is not against us is for us.”
  51. 51It came to pass, when the days were near that he should be taken up, he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem
  52. 52and sent messengers before his face. They went and entered into a village of the Samaritans, so as to prepare for him.
  53. 53They didn’t receive him, because he was traveling with his face set toward Jerusalem.
  54. 54When his disciples, James and John, saw this, they said, “Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from the sky, and destroy them, just as Elijah did?”
  55. 55But he turned and rebuked them, “You don’t know of what kind of spirit you are.
  56. 56For the Son of Man didn’t come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.” They went to another village.
  57. 57As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, “I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord.”
  58. 58Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”
  59. 59He said to another, “Follow me!” But he said, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”
  60. 60But Jesus said to him, “Leave the dead to bury their own dead, but you go and announce God’s Kingdom.”
  61. 61Another also said, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first allow me to say good-bye to those who are at my house.”
  62. 62But Jesus said to him, “No one, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for God’s Kingdom.”

Chapter 10

  1. 1Now after these things, the Lord also appointed seventy others, and sent them two by two ahead of him into every city and place where he was about to come.
  2. 2Then he said to them, “The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few. Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest.
  3. 3Go your ways. Behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.
  4. 4Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals. Greet no one on the way.
  5. 5Into whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house.’
  6. 6If a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
  7. 7Remain in that same house, eating and drinking the things they give, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Don’t go from house to house.
  8. 8Into whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat the things that are set before you.
  9. 9Heal the sick who are there, and tell them, ‘God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’
  10. 10But into whatever city you enter, and they don’t receive you, go out into its streets and say,
  11. 11‘Even the dust from your city that clings to us, we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’
  12. 12I tell you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.
  13. 13“Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
  14. 14But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.
  15. 15You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.
  16. 16Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me.”
  17. 17The seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!”
  18. 18He said to them, “I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven.
  19. 19Behold, I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will in any way hurt you.
  20. 20Nevertheless, don’t rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”
  21. 21In that same hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said, “I thank you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.”
  22. 22Turning to the disciples, he said, “All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him.”
  23. 23Turning to the disciples, he said privately, “Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,
  24. 24for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.”
  25. 25Behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested him, saying, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
  26. 26He said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you read it?”
  27. 27He answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.”
  28. 28He said to him, “You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live.”
  29. 29But he, desiring to justify himself, asked Jesus, “Who is my neighbor?”
  30. 30Jesus answered, “A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead.
  31. 31By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
  32. 32In the same way a Levite also, when he came to the place, and saw him, passed by on the other side.
  33. 33But a certain Samaritan, as he traveled, came where he was. When he saw him, he was moved with compassion,
  34. 34came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. He set him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
  35. 35On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii, gave them to the host, and said to him, ‘Take care of him. Whatever you spend beyond that, I will repay you when I return.’
  36. 36Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers?”
  37. 37He said, “He who showed mercy on him.” Then Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.”
  38. 38As they went on their way, he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.
  39. 39She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet, and heard his word.
  40. 40But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me.”
  41. 41Jesus answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,
  42. 42but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.”

Chapter 11

  1. 1When he finished praying in a certain place, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples.”
  2. 2He said to them, “When you pray, say, ‘Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy. May your Kingdom come. May your will be done on earth, as it is in heaven.
  3. 3Give us day by day our daily bread.
  4. 4Forgive us our sins, for we ourselves also forgive everyone who is indebted to us. Bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.’ ”
  5. 5He said to them, “Which of you, if you go to a friend at midnight, and tell him, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,
  6. 6for a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him,’
  7. 7and he from within will answer and say, ‘Don’t bother me. The door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed. I can’t get up and give it to you’?
  8. 8I tell you, although he will not rise and give it to him because he is his friend, yet because of his persistence, he will get up and give him as many as he needs.
  9. 9“I tell you, keep asking, and it will be given you. Keep seeking, and you will find. Keep knocking, and it will be opened to you.
  10. 10For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.
  11. 11“Which of you fathers, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, he won’t give him a snake instead of a fish, will he?
  12. 12Or if he asks for an egg, he won’t give him a scorpion, will he?
  13. 13If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him?”
  14. 14He was casting out a demon, and it was mute. When the demon had gone out, the mute man spoke; and the multitudes marveled.
  15. 15But some of them said, “He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”
  16. 16Others, testing him, sought from him a sign from heaven.
  17. 17But he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation. A house divided against itself falls.
  18. 18If Satan also is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? For you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.
  19. 19But if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
  20. 20But if I by God’s finger cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come to you.
  21. 21“When the strong man, fully armed, guards his own dwelling, his goods are safe.
  22. 22But when someone stronger attacks him and overcomes him, he takes from him his whole armor in which he trusted, and divides his plunder.
  23. 23“He that is not with me is against me. He who doesn’t gather with me scatters.
  24. 24The unclean spirit, when he has gone out of the man, passes through dry places, seeking rest, and finding none, he says, ‘I will turn back to my house from which I came out.’
  25. 25When he returns, he finds it swept and put in order.
  26. 26Then he goes, and takes seven other spirits more evil than himself, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first.”
  27. 27It came to pass, as he said these things, a certain woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice, and said to him, “Blessed is the womb that bore you, and the breasts which nursed you!”
  28. 28But he said, “On the contrary, blessed are those who hear the word of God, and keep it.”
  29. 29When the multitudes were gathering together to him, he began to say, “This is an evil generation. It seeks after a sign. No sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah, the prophet.
  30. 30For even as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will also be to this generation.
  31. 31The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and will condemn them: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, one greater than Solomon is here.
  32. 32The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, one greater than Jonah is here.
  33. 33“No one, when he has lit a lamp, puts it in a cellar or under a basket, but on a stand, that those who come in may see the light.
  34. 34The lamp of the body is the eye. Therefore when your eye is good, your whole body is also full of light; but when it is evil, your body also is full of darkness.
  35. 35Therefore see whether the light that is in you isn’t darkness.
  36. 36If therefore your whole body is full of light, having no part dark, it will be wholly full of light, as when the lamp with its bright shining gives you light.”
  37. 37Now as he spoke, a certain Pharisee asked him to dine with him. He went in, and sat at the table.
  38. 38When the Pharisee saw it, he marveled that he had not first washed himself before dinner.
  39. 39The Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter, but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness.
  40. 40You foolish ones, didn’t he who made the outside make the inside also?
  41. 41But give for gifts to the needy those things which are within, and behold, all things will be clean to you.
  42. 42But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and every herb, but you bypass justice and God’s love. You ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.
  43. 43Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the best seats in the synagogues, and the greetings in the marketplaces.
  44. 44Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like hidden graves, and the men who walk over them don’t know it.”
  45. 45One of the lawyers answered him, “Teacher, in saying this you insult us also.”
  46. 46He said, “Woe to you lawyers also! For you load men with burdens that are difficult to carry, and you yourselves won’t even lift one finger to help carry those burdens.
  47. 47Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.
  48. 48So you testify and consent to the works of your fathers. For they killed them, and you build their tombs.
  49. 49Therefore also the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send to them prophets and apostles; and some of them they will kill and persecute,
  50. 50that the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;
  51. 51from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary.’ Yes, I tell you, it will be required of this generation.
  52. 52Woe to you lawyers! For you took away the key of knowledge. You didn’t enter in yourselves, and those who were entering in, you hindered.”
  53. 53As he said these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to be terribly angry, and to draw many things out of him;
  54. 54lying in wait for him, and seeking to catch him in something he might say, that they might accuse him.

Chapter 12

  1. 1Meanwhile, when a multitude of many thousands had gathered together, so much so that they trampled on each other, he began to tell his disciples first of all, “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
  2. 2But there is nothing covered up that will not be revealed, nor hidden that will not be known.
  3. 3Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light. What you have spoken in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.
  4. 4“I tell you, my friends, don’t be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.
  5. 5But I will warn you whom you should fear. Fear him who after he has killed, has power to cast into Gehenna. Yes, I tell you, fear him.
  6. 6“Aren’t five sparrows sold for two assaria coins? Not one of them is forgotten by God.
  7. 7But the very hairs of your head are all counted. Therefore don’t be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows.
  8. 8“I tell you, everyone who confesses me before men, the Son of Man will also confess before the angels of God;
  9. 9but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of God’s angels.
  10. 10Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
  11. 11When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, don’t be anxious how or what you will answer, or what you will say;
  12. 12for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that same hour what you must say.”
  13. 13One of the multitude said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
  14. 14But he said to him, “Man, who made me a judge or an arbitrator over you?”
  15. 15He said to them, “Beware! Keep yourselves from covetousness, for a man’s life doesn’t consist of the abundance of the things which he possesses.”
  16. 16He spoke a parable to them, saying, “The ground of a certain rich man produced abundantly.
  17. 17He reasoned within himself, saying, ‘What will I do, because I don’t have room to store my crops?’
  18. 18He said, ‘This is what I will do. I will pull down my barns, build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
  19. 19I will tell my soul, “Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years. Take your ease, eat, drink, and be merry.” ’
  20. 20“But God said to him, ‘You foolish one, tonight your soul is required of you. The things which you have prepared—whose will they be?’
  21. 21So is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.”
  22. 22He said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, don’t be anxious for your life, what you will eat, nor yet for your body, what you will wear.
  23. 23Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing.
  24. 24Consider the ravens: they don’t sow, they don’t reap, they have no warehouse or barn, and God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!
  25. 25Which of you by being anxious can add a cubit to his height?
  26. 26If then you aren’t able to do even the least things, why are you anxious about the rest?
  27. 27Consider the lilies, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin; yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
  28. 28But if this is how God clothes the grass in the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?
  29. 29Don’t seek what you will eat or what you will drink; neither be anxious.
  30. 30For the nations of the world seek after all of these things, but your Father knows that you need these things.
  31. 31But seek God’s Kingdom, and all these things will be added to you.
  32. 32Don’t be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.
  33. 33Sell that which you have, and give gifts to the needy. Make for yourselves purses which don’t grow old, a treasure in the heavens that doesn’t fail, where no thief approaches, neither moth destroys.
  34. 34For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
  35. 35“Let your waist be dressed and your lamps burning.
  36. 36Be like men watching for their lord, when he returns from the wedding feast; that when he comes and knocks, they may immediately open to him.
  37. 37Blessed are those servants, whom the lord will find watching when he comes. Most certainly I tell you that he will dress himself, make them recline, and will come and serve them.
  38. 38They will be blessed if he comes in the second or third watch, and finds them so.
  39. 39But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not allowed his house to be broken into.
  40. 40Therefore be ready also, for the Son of Man is coming in an hour that you don’t expect him.”
  41. 41Peter said to him, “Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everybody?”
  42. 42The Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord will set over his household, to give them their portion of food at the right times?
  43. 43Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes.
  44. 44Truly I tell you, that he will set him over all that he has.
  45. 45But if that servant says in his heart, ‘My lord delays his coming,’ and begins to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken,
  46. 46then the lord of that servant will come in a day when he isn’t expecting him, and in an hour that he doesn’t know, and will cut him in two, and place his portion with the unfaithful.
  47. 47That servant, who knew his lord’s will, and didn’t prepare, nor do what he wanted, will be beaten with many stripes,
  48. 48but he who didn’t know, and did things worthy of stripes, will be beaten with few stripes. To whomever much is given, of him will much be required; and to whom much was entrusted, of him more will be asked.
  49. 49“I came to throw fire on the earth. I wish it were already kindled.
  50. 50But I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!
  51. 51Do you think that I have come to give peace in the earth? I tell you, no, but rather division.
  52. 52For from now on, there will be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.
  53. 53They will be divided, father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against her mother; mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”
  54. 54He said to the multitudes also, “When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and so it happens.
  55. 55When a south wind blows, you say, ‘There will be a scorching heat,’ and it happens.
  56. 56You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how is it that you don’t interpret this time?
  57. 57Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?
  58. 58For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
  59. 59I tell you, you will by no means get out of there, until you have paid the very last penny.”

Chapter 13

  1. 1Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.
  2. 2Jesus answered them, “Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered such things?
  3. 3I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.
  4. 4Or those eighteen, on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them; do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?
  5. 5I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.”
  6. 6He spoke this parable. “A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it, and found none.
  7. 7He said to the vine dresser, ‘Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down. Why does it waste the soil?’
  8. 8He answered, ‘Lord, leave it alone this year also, until I dig around it and fertilize it.
  9. 9If it bears fruit, fine; but if not, after that, you can cut it down.’ ”
  10. 10He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day.
  11. 11Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years. She was bent over, and could in no way straighten herself up.
  12. 12When Jesus saw her, he called her, and said to her, “Woman, you are freed from your infirmity.”
  13. 13He laid his hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight and glorified God.
  14. 14The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the multitude, “There are six days in which men ought to work. Therefore come on those days and be healed, and not on the Sabbath day!”
  15. 15Therefore the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Doesn’t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath, and lead him away to water?
  16. 16Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham whom Satan had bound eighteen long years, be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day?”
  17. 17As he said these things, all his adversaries were disappointed and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.
  18. 18He said, “What is God’s Kingdom like? To what shall I compare it?
  19. 19It is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took and put in his own garden. It grew and became a large tree, and the birds of the sky live in its branches.”
  20. 20Again he said, “To what shall I compare God’s Kingdom?
  21. 21It is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour, until it was all leavened.”
  22. 22He went on his way through cities and villages, teaching, and traveling on to Jerusalem.
  23. 23One said to him, “Lord, are they few who are saved?” He said to them,
  24. 24“Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in and will not be able.
  25. 25When once the master of the house has risen up, and has shut the door, and you begin to stand outside and to knock at the door, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’ then he will answer and tell you, ‘I don’t know you or where you come from.’
  26. 26Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’
  27. 27He will say, ‘I tell you, I don’t know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.’
  28. 28There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside.
  29. 29They will come from the east, west, north, and south, and will sit down in God’s Kingdom.
  30. 30Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.”
  31. 31On that same day, some Pharisees came, saying to him, “Get out of here, and go away, for Herod wants to kill you.”
  32. 32He said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.
  33. 33Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, for it can’t be that a prophet would perish outside of Jerusalem.’
  34. 34“Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you refused!
  35. 35Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ”

Chapter 14

  1. 1When he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him.
  2. 2Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him.
  3. 3Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”
  4. 4But they were silent. He took him, and healed him, and let him go.
  5. 5He answered them, “Which of you, if your son or an ox fell into a well, wouldn’t immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?”
  6. 6They couldn’t answer him regarding these things.
  7. 7He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,
  8. 8“When you are invited by anyone to a wedding feast, don’t sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him,
  9. 9and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.
  10. 10But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, ‘Friend, move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.
  11. 11For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.”
  12. 12He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.
  13. 13But when you make a feast, ask the poor, the maimed, the lame, or the blind;
  14. 14and you will be blessed, because they don’t have the resources to repay you. For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous.”
  15. 15When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things, he said to him, “Blessed is he who will feast in God’s Kingdom!”
  16. 16But he said to him, “A certain man made a great supper, and he invited many people.
  17. 17He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, ‘Come, for everything is ready now.’
  18. 18They all as one began to make excuses. “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’
  19. 19“Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.’
  20. 20“Another said, ‘I have married a wife, and therefore I can’t come.’
  21. 21“That servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house, being angry, said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor, maimed, blind, and lame.’
  22. 22“The servant said, ‘Lord, it is done as you commanded, and there is still room.’
  23. 23“The lord said to the servant, ‘Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.
  24. 24For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper.’ ”
  25. 25Now great multitudes were going with him. He turned and said to them,
  26. 26“If anyone comes to me, and doesn’t disregard his own father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he can’t be my disciple.
  27. 27Whoever doesn’t bear his own cross, and come after me, can’t be my disciple.
  28. 28For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doesn’t first sit down and count the cost, to see if he has enough to complete it?
  29. 29Or perhaps, when he has laid a foundation, and is not able to finish, everyone who sees begins to mock him,
  30. 30saying, ‘This man began to build, and wasn’t able to finish.’
  31. 31Or what king, as he goes to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?
  32. 32Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy, and asks for conditions of peace.
  33. 33So therefore whoever of you who doesn’t renounce all that he has, he can’t be my disciple.
  34. 34Salt is good, but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless, with what do you season it?
  35. 35It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. It is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

Chapter 15

  1. 1Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.
  2. 2The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.”
  3. 3He told them this parable.
  4. 4“Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep, and lost one of them, wouldn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?
  5. 5When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
  6. 6When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’
  7. 7I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.
  8. 8Or what woman, if she had ten drachma coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?
  9. 9When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.’
  10. 10Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”
  11. 11He said, “A certain man had two sons.
  12. 12The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of your property.’ He divided his livelihood between them.
  13. 13Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.
  14. 14When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.
  15. 15He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
  16. 16He wanted to fill his belly with the husks that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.
  17. 17But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough to spare, and I’m dying with hunger!
  18. 18I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight.
  19. 19I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.” ’
  20. 20“He arose, and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
  21. 21The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
  22. 22“But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe, and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand, and sandals on his feet.
  23. 23Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let’s eat, and celebrate;
  24. 24for this, my son, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ Then they began to celebrate.
  25. 25“Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.
  26. 26He called one of the servants to him, and asked what was going on.
  27. 27He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’
  28. 28But he was angry, and would not go in. Therefore his father came out, and begged him.
  29. 29But he answered his father, ‘Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.
  30. 30But when this your son came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’
  31. 31“He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
  32. 32But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ ”

Chapter 16

  1. 1He also said to his disciples, “There was a certain rich man who had a manager. An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions.
  2. 2He called him, and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.’
  3. 3“The manager said within himself, ‘What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don’t have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.
  4. 4I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from management, they may receive me into their houses.’
  5. 5Calling each one of his lord’s debtors to him, he said to the first, ‘How much do you owe to my lord?’
  6. 6He said, ‘A hundred batos of oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.’
  7. 7Then he said to another, ‘How much do you owe?’ He said, ‘A hundred cors of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write eighty.’
  8. 8“His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely, for the children of this world are, in their own generation, wiser than the children of the light.
  9. 9I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.
  10. 10He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
  11. 11If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
  12. 12If you have not been faithful in that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?
  13. 13No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to one, and despise the other. You aren’t able to serve God and Mammon.”
  14. 14The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, also heard all these things, and they scoffed at him.
  15. 15He said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts. For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.
  16. 16The law and the prophets were until John. From that time the Good News of God’s Kingdom is preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.
  17. 17But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall.
  18. 18Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery. He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.
  19. 19“Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.
  20. 20A certain beggar, named Lazarus, was taken to his gate, full of sores,
  21. 21and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores.
  22. 22The beggar died, and he was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom. The rich man also died, and was buried.
  23. 23In Hades, he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far off, and Lazarus at his bosom.
  24. 24He cried and said, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue! For I am in anguish in this flame.’
  25. 25“But Abraham said, ‘Son, remember that you, in your lifetime, received your good things, and Lazarus, in the same way, bad things. But here he is now comforted, and you are in anguish.
  26. 26Besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that those who want to pass from here to you are not able, and that no one may cross over from there to us.’
  27. 27“He said, ‘I ask you therefore, father, that you would send him to my father’s house;
  28. 28for I have five brothers, that he may testify to them, so they won’t also come into this place of torment.’
  29. 29“But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’
  30. 30“He said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’
  31. 31“He said to him, ‘If they don’t listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead.’ ”

Chapter 17

  1. 1He said to the disciples, “It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come, but woe to him through whom they come!
  2. 2It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.
  3. 3Be careful. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.
  4. 4If he sins against you seven times in the day, and seven times returns, saying, ‘I repent,’ you shall forgive him.”
  5. 5The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”
  6. 6The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted, and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
  7. 7But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say when he comes in from the field, ‘Come immediately and sit down at the table,’
  8. 8and will not rather tell him, ‘Prepare my supper, clothe yourself properly, and serve me, while I eat and drink. Afterward you shall eat and drink’?
  9. 9Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded? I think not.
  10. 10Even so you also, when you have done all the things that are commanded you, say, ‘We are unworthy servants. We have done our duty.’ ”
  11. 11As he was on his way to Jerusalem, he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee.
  12. 12As he entered into a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, who stood at a distance.
  13. 13They lifted up their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
  14. 14When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” As they went, they were cleansed.
  15. 15One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.
  16. 16He fell on his face at Jesus’ feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.
  17. 17Jesus answered, “Weren’t the ten cleansed? But where are the nine?
  18. 18Were there none found who returned to give glory to God, except this foreigner?”
  19. 19Then he said to him, “Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you.”
  20. 20Being asked by the Pharisees when God’s Kingdom would come, he answered them, “God’s Kingdom doesn’t come with observation;
  21. 21neither will they say, ‘Look, here!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ for behold, God’s Kingdom is within you.”
  22. 22He said to the disciples, “The days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
  23. 23They will tell you, ‘Look, here!’ or ‘Look, there!’ Don’t go away or follow after them,
  24. 24for as the lightning, when it flashes out of one part under the sky, shines to another part under the sky; so will the Son of Man be in his day.
  25. 25But first, he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
  26. 26As it was in the days of Noah, even so will it be also in the days of the Son of Man.
  27. 27They ate, they drank, they married, and they were given in marriage until the day that Noah entered into the ship, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
  28. 28Likewise, even as it was in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;
  29. 29but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from the sky and destroyed them all.
  30. 30It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
  31. 31In that day, he who will be on the housetop and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back.
  32. 32Remember Lot’s wife!
  33. 33Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it.
  34. 34I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. One will be taken and the other will be left.
  35. 35There will be two grinding grain together. One will be taken and the other will be left.”
  36. 36
  37. 37They, answering, asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the body is, there the vultures will also be gathered together.”

Chapter 18

  1. 1He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up,
  2. 2saying, “There was a judge in a certain city who didn’t fear God, and didn’t respect man.
  3. 3A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, ‘Defend me from my adversary!’
  4. 4He wouldn’t for a while, but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God, nor respect man,
  5. 5yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.’ ”
  6. 6The Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.
  7. 7Won’t God avenge his chosen ones who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?
  8. 8I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”
  9. 9He spoke also this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others.
  10. 10“Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.
  11. 11The Pharisee stood and prayed to himself like this: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like the rest of men, extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
  12. 12I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.’
  13. 13But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn’t even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’
  14. 14I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”
  15. 15They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
  16. 16Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.
  17. 17Most certainly, I tell you, whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”
  18. 18A certain ruler asked him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
  19. 19Jesus asked him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one: God.
  20. 20You know the commandments: ‘Don’t commit adultery,’ ‘Don’t murder,’ ‘Don’t steal,’ ‘Don’t give false testimony,’ ‘Honor your father and your mother.’ ”
  21. 21He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”
  22. 22When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have, and distribute it to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
  23. 23But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.
  24. 24Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!
  25. 25For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”
  26. 26Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”
  27. 27But he said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”
  28. 28Peter said, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”
  29. 29He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for God’s Kingdom’s sake,
  30. 30who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”
  31. 31He took the twelve aside, and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.
  32. 32For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.
  33. 33They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again.”
  34. 34They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn’t understand the things that were said.
  35. 35As he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.
  36. 36Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.
  37. 37They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.
  38. 38He cried out, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”
  39. 39Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”
  40. 40Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,
  41. 41“What do you want me to do?” He said, “Lord, that I may see again.”
  42. 42Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.”
  43. 43Immediately he received his sight and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

Chapter 19

  1. 1He entered and was passing through Jericho.
  2. 2There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.
  3. 3He was trying to see who Jesus was, and couldn’t because of the crowd, because he was short.
  4. 4He ran on ahead, and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, for he was going to pass that way.
  5. 5When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and saw him, and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house.”
  6. 6He hurried, came down, and received him joyfully.
  7. 7When they saw it, they all murmured, saying, “He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner.”
  8. 8Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.”
  9. 9Jesus said to him, “Today, salvation has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.
  10. 10For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.”
  11. 11As they heard these things, he went on and told a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that God’s Kingdom would be revealed immediately.
  12. 12He said therefore, “A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and to return.
  13. 13He called ten servants of his and gave them ten mina coins, and told them, ‘Conduct business until I come.’
  14. 14But his citizens hated him, and sent an envoy after him, saying, ‘We don’t want this man to reign over us.’
  15. 15“When he had come back again, having received the kingdom, he commanded these servants, to whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by conducting business.
  16. 16The first came before him, saying, ‘Lord, your mina has made ten more minas.’
  17. 17“He said to him, ‘Well done, you good servant! Because you were found faithful with very little, you shall have authority over ten cities.’
  18. 18“The second came, saying, ‘Your mina, Lord, has made five minas.’
  19. 19“So he said to him, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’
  20. 20Another came, saying, ‘Lord, behold, your mina, which I kept laid away in a handkerchief,
  21. 21for I feared you, because you are an exacting man. You take up that which you didn’t lay down, and reap that which you didn’t sow.’
  22. 22“He said to him, ‘Out of your own mouth I will judge you, you wicked servant! You knew that I am an exacting man, taking up that which I didn’t lay down, and reaping that which I didn’t sow.
  23. 23Then why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?’
  24. 24He said to those who stood by, ‘Take the mina away from him and give it to him who has the ten minas.’
  25. 25“They said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten minas!’
  26. 26‘For I tell you that to everyone who has, will more be given; but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.
  27. 27But bring those enemies of mine who didn’t want me to reign over them here, and kill them before me.’ ”
  28. 28Having said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
  29. 29When he came near to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,
  30. 30saying, “Go your way into the village on the other side, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, which no man had ever sat upon. Untie it and bring it.
  31. 31If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ say to him: ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
  32. 32Those who were sent went away, and found things just as he had told them.
  33. 33As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying the colt?”
  34. 34They said, “The Lord needs it.”
  35. 35Then they brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt, and sat Jesus on them.
  36. 36As he went, they spread their cloaks on the road.
  37. 37As he was now getting near, at the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen,
  38. 38saying, “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest!”
  39. 39Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples!”
  40. 40He answered them, “I tell you that if these were silent, the stones would cry out.”
  41. 41When he came near, he saw the city and wept over it,
  42. 42saying, “If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to your peace! But now, they are hidden from your eyes.
  43. 43For the days will come on you, when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,
  44. 44and will dash you and your children within you to the ground. They will not leave in you one stone on another, because you didn’t know the time of your visitation.”
  45. 45He entered into the temple, and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it,
  46. 46saying to them, “It is written, ‘My house is a house of prayer,’ but you have made it a ‘den of robbers’!”
  47. 47He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests, the scribes, and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him.
  48. 48They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.

Chapter 20

  1. 1On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the priests and scribes came to him with the elders.
  2. 2They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”
  3. 3He answered them, “I also will ask you one question. Tell me:
  4. 4the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?”
