  5. 5They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’
  6. 6But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”
  7. 7They answered that they didn’t know where it was from.
  8. 8Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
  9. 9He began to tell the people this parable. “A man planted a vineyard, and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.
  10. 10At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him, and sent him away empty.
  11. 11He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him, and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.
  12. 12He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him, and threw him out.
  13. 13The lord of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.’
  14. 14“But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.’
  15. 15They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?
  16. 16He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.” When they heard that, they said, “May that never be!”
  17. 17But he looked at them and said, “Then what is this that is written, ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the chief cornerstone?’
  18. 18Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but it will crush whomever it falls on to dust.”
  19. 19The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour, but they feared the people—for they knew he had spoken this parable against them.
  20. 20They watched him and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.
  21. 21They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.
  22. 22Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”
  23. 23But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?
  24. 24Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?” They answered, “Caesar’s.”
  25. 25He said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
  26. 26They weren’t able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer and were silent.
  27. 27Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.
  28. 28They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up children for his brother.
  29. 29There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.
  30. 30The second took her as wife, and he died childless.
  31. 31The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.
  32. 32Afterward the woman also died.
  33. 33Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”
  34. 34Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry, and are given in marriage.
  35. 35But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
  36. 36For they can’t die any more, for they are like the angels, and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.
  37. 37But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’
  38. 38Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.”
  39. 39Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you speak well.”
  40. 40They didn’t dare to ask him any more questions.
  41. 41He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?
  42. 42David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
  43. 43until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.” ’
  44. 44“David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”
  45. 45In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,
  46. 46“Beware of those scribes who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;
  47. 47who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers: these will receive greater condemnation.”

Chapter 21

  1. 1He looked up and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.
  2. 2He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.
  3. 3He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,
  4. 4for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on.”
  5. 5As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,
  6. 6“As for these things which you see, the days will come, in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down.”
  7. 7They asked him, “Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?”
  8. 8He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them.
  9. 9When you hear of wars and disturbances, don’t be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won’t come immediately.”
  10. 10Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
  11. 11There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.
  12. 12But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.
  13. 13It will turn out as a testimony for you.
  14. 14Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,
  15. 15for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.
  16. 16You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will cause some of you to be put to death.
  17. 17You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake.
  18. 18And not a hair of your head will perish.
  19. 19“By your endurance you will win your lives.
  20. 20“But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.
  21. 21Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the middle of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.
  22. 22For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.
  23. 23Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.
  24. 24They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
  25. 25There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;
  26. 26men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
  27. 27Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
  28. 28But when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”
  29. 29He told them a parable. “See the fig tree and all the trees.
  30. 30When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.
  31. 31Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that God’s Kingdom is near.
  32. 32Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.
  33. 33Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.
  34. 34“So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.
  35. 35For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.
  36. 36Therefore be watchful all the time, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
  37. 37Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.
  38. 38All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

Chapter 22

  1. 1Now the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.
  2. 2The chief priests and the scribes sought how they might put him to death, for they feared the people.
  3. 3Satan entered into Judas, who was also called Iscariot, who was counted with the twelve.
  4. 4He went away, and talked with the chief priests and captains about how he might deliver him to them.
  5. 5They were glad, and agreed to give him money.
  6. 6He consented, and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the multitude.
  7. 7The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.
  8. 8Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat.”
  9. 9They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare?”
  10. 10He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house which he enters.
  11. 11Tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?” ’
  12. 12He will show you a large, furnished upper room. Make preparations there.”
  13. 13They went, found things as Jesus had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
  14. 14When the hour had come, he sat down with the twelve apostles.
  15. 15He said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,
  16. 16for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in God’s Kingdom.”
  17. 17He received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, “Take this, and share it among yourselves,
  18. 18for I tell you, I will not drink at all again from the fruit of the vine, until God’s Kingdom comes.”
  19. 19He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke, and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me.”
  20. 20Likewise, he took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.
  21. 21But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.
  22. 22The Son of Man indeed goes, as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whom he is betrayed!”
  23. 23They began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.
  24. 24A dispute also arose among them, which of them was considered to be greatest.
  25. 25He said to them, “The kings of the nations lord it over them, and those who have authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’
  26. 26But not so with you. But one who is the greater among you, let him become as the younger, and one who is governing, as one who serves.
  27. 27For who is greater, one who sits at the table, or one who serves? Isn’t it he who sits at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
  28. 28But you are those who have continued with me in my trials.
  29. 29I confer on you a kingdom, even as my Father conferred on me,
  30. 30that you may eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”
  31. 31The Lord said, “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have all of you, that he might sift you as wheat,
  32. 32but I prayed for you, that your faith wouldn’t fail. You, when once you have turned again, establish your brothers.”
  33. 33He said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”
  34. 34He said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will by no means crow today until you deny that you know me three times.”
  35. 35He said to them, “When I sent you out without purse, wallet, and sandals, did you lack anything?” They said, “Nothing.”
  36. 36Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a wallet. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.
  37. 37For I tell you that this which is written must still be fulfilled in me: ‘He was counted with transgressors.’ For that which concerns me has an end.”
  38. 38They said, “Lord, behold, here are two swords.” He said to them, “That is enough.”
  39. 39He came out and went, as his custom was, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples also followed him.
  40. 40When he was at the place, he said to them, “Pray that you don’t enter into temptation.”
  41. 41He was withdrawn from them about a stone’s throw, and he knelt down and prayed,
  42. 42saying, “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”
  43. 43An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.
  44. 44Being in agony he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.
  45. 45When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples, and found them sleeping because of grief,
  46. 46and said to them, “Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”
  47. 47While he was still speaking, behold, a multitude, and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He came near to Jesus to kiss him.
  48. 48But Jesus said to him, “Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”
  49. 49When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said to him, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”
  50. 50A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
  51. 51But Jesus answered, “Let me at least do this”—and he touched his ear, and healed him.
  52. 52Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and elders, who had come against him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?
  53. 53When I was with you in the temple daily, you didn’t stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”
  54. 54They seized him, and led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter followed from a distance.
  55. 55When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard, and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.
  56. 56A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, “This man also was with him.”
  57. 57He denied Jesus, saying, “Woman, I don’t know him.”
  58. 58After a little while someone else saw him, and said, “You also are one of them!” But Peter answered, “Man, I am not!”
  59. 59After about one hour passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, “Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean!”
  60. 60But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
  61. 61The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the Lord’s word, how he said to him, “Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.”
  62. 62He went out, and wept bitterly.
  63. 63The men who held Jesus mocked him and beat him.
  64. 64Having blindfolded him, they struck him on the face and asked him, “Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?”
  65. 65They spoke many other things against him, insulting him.
  66. 66As soon as it was day, the assembly of the elders of the people were gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,
  67. 67“If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you won’t believe,
  68. 68and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.
  69. 69From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
  70. 70They all said, “Are you then the Son of God?” He said to them, “You say it, because I am.”
  71. 71They said, “Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!”

Chapter 23

  1. 1The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.
  2. 2They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”
  3. 3Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “So you say.”
  4. 4Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”
  5. 5But they insisted, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place.”
  6. 6But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.
  7. 7When he found out that he was in Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.
  8. 8Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.
  9. 9He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.
  10. 10The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.
  11. 11Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.
  12. 12Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.
  13. 13Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,
  14. 14and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and behold, having examined him before you, I found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.
  15. 15Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.
  16. 16I will therefore chastise him and release him.”
  17. 17Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.
  18. 18But they all cried out together, saying, “Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!”—
  19. 19one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.
  20. 20Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,
  21. 21but they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify him!”
  22. 22He said to them the third time, “Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him.”
  23. 23But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.
  24. 24Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.
  25. 25He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.
  26. 26When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it after Jesus.
  27. 27A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.
  28. 28But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
  29. 29For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’
  30. 30Then they will begin to tell the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and tell the hills, ‘Cover us.’
  31. 31For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?”
  32. 32There were also others, two criminals, led with him to be put to death.
  33. 33When they came to the place that is called “The Skull”, they crucified him there with the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.
  34. 34Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.
  35. 35The people stood watching. The rulers with them also scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others. Let him save himself, if this is the Christ of God, his chosen one!”
  36. 36The soldiers also mocked him, coming to him and offering him vinegar,
  37. 37and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”
  38. 38An inscription was also written over him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: “THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
  39. 39One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, “If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!”
  40. 40But the other answered, and rebuking him said, “Don’t you even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?
  41. 41And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”
  42. 42He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”
  43. 43Jesus said to him, “Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”
  44. 44It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.
  45. 45The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
  46. 46Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” Having said this, he breathed his last.
  47. 47When the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, “Certainly this was a righteous man.”
  48. 48All the multitudes that came together to see this, when they saw the things that were done, returned home beating their breasts.
  49. 49All his acquaintances and the women who followed with him from Galilee stood at a distance, watching these things.
  50. 50Behold, a man named Joseph, who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man
  51. 51(he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for God’s Kingdom:
  52. 52this man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.
  53. 53He took it down, and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb that was cut in stone, where no one had ever been laid.
  54. 54It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.
  55. 55The women, who had come with him out of Galilee, followed after, and saw the tomb, and how his body was laid.
  56. 56They returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

Chapter 24

  1. 1But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they and some others came to the tomb, bringing the spices which they had prepared.
  2. 2They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
  3. 3They entered in, and didn’t find the Lord Jesus’ body.
  4. 4While they were greatly perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.
  5. 5Becoming terrified, they bowed their faces down to the earth. They said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?
  6. 6He isn’t here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,
  7. 7saying that the Son of Man must be delivered up into the hands of sinful men and be crucified, and the third day rise again?”
  8. 8They remembered his words,
  9. 9returned from the tomb, and told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
  10. 10Now they were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Mary the mother of James. The other women with them told these things to the apostles.
  11. 11These words seemed to them to be nonsense, and they didn’t believe them.
  12. 12But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Stooping and looking in, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he departed to his home, wondering what had happened.
  13. 13Behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was sixty stadia from Jerusalem.
  14. 14They talked with each other about all of these things which had happened.
  15. 15While they talked and questioned together, Jesus himself came near, and went with them.
  16. 16But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.
  17. 17He said to them, “What are you talking about as you walk, and are sad?”
  18. 18One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things which have happened there in these days?”
  19. 19He said to them, “What things?” They said to him, “The things concerning Jesus, the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people;
  20. 20and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.
  21. 21But we were hoping that it was he who would redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.
  22. 22Also, certain women of our company amazed us, having arrived early at the tomb;
  23. 23and when they didn’t find his body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.
  24. 24Some of us went to the tomb, and found it just like the women had said, but they didn’t see him.”
  25. 25He said to them, “Foolish men, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!
  26. 26Didn’t the Christ have to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?”
  27. 27Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.
  28. 28They came near to the village where they were going, and he acted like he would go further.
  29. 29They urged him, saying, “Stay with us, for it is almost evening, and the day is almost over.” He went in to stay with them.
  30. 30When he had sat down at the table with them, he took the bread and gave thanks. Breaking it, he gave it to them.
  31. 31Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, then he vanished out of their sight.
  32. 32They said to one another, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us, while he spoke to us along the way, and while he opened the Scriptures to us?”
  33. 33They rose up that very hour, returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,
  34. 34saying, “The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”
  35. 35They related the things that happened along the way, and how he was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.
  36. 36As they said these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”
  37. 37But they were terrified and filled with fear, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
  38. 38He said to them, “Why are you troubled? Why do doubts arise in your hearts?
  39. 39See my hands and my feet, that it is truly me. Touch me and see, for a spirit doesn’t have flesh and bones, as you see that I have.”
  40. 40When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
  41. 41While they still didn’t believe for joy, and wondered, he said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
  42. 42They gave him a piece of a broiled fish and some honeycomb.
  43. 43He took them, and ate in front of them.
  44. 44He said to them, “This is what I told you, while I was still with you, that all things which are written in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms, concerning me must be fulfilled.”
  45. 45Then he opened their minds, that they might understand the Scriptures.
  46. 46He said to them, “Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,
  47. 47and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
  48. 48You are witnesses of these things.
  49. 49Behold, I send out the promise of my Father on you. But wait in the city of Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high.”
  50. 50He led them out as far as Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
  51. 51While he blessed them, he withdrew from them, and was carried up into heaven.
  52. 52They worshiped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
  53. 53and were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
4

John

Gospel

Chapter 1

  1. 1In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
  2. 2The same was in the beginning with God.
  3. 3All things were made through him. Without him, nothing was made that has been made.
  4. 4In him was life, and the life was the light of men.
  5. 5The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome it.
  6. 6There came a man, sent from God, whose name was John.
  7. 7The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him.
  8. 8He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light.
  9. 9The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world.
  10. 10He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him.
  11. 11He came to his own, and those who were his own didn’t receive him.
  12. 12But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name:
  13. 13who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
  14. 14The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.
  15. 15John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’ ”
  16. 16From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.
  17. 17For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.
  18. 18No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, has declared him.
  19. 19This is John’s testimony, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
  20. 20He declared, and didn’t deny, but he declared, “I am not the Christ.”
  21. 21They asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the prophet?” He answered, “No.”
  22. 22They said therefore to him, “Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
  23. 23He said, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as Isaiah the prophet said.”
  24. 24The ones who had been sent were from the Pharisees.
  25. 25They asked him, “Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”
  26. 26John answered them, “I baptize in water, but among you stands one whom you don’t know.
  27. 27He is the one who comes after me, who is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to loosen.”
  28. 28These things were done in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
  29. 29The next day, he saw Jesus coming to him, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
  30. 30This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who is preferred before me, for he was before me.’
  31. 31I didn’t know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water: that he would be revealed to Israel.”
  32. 32John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.
  33. 33I didn’t recognize him, but he who sent me to baptize in water said to me, ‘On whomever you will see the Spirit descending and remaining on him is he who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.’
  34. 34I have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.”
  35. 35Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples,
  36. 36and he looked at Jesus as he walked, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”
  37. 37The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
  38. 38Jesus turned and saw them following, and said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), “where are you staying?”
  39. 39He said to them, “Come, and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.
  40. 40One of the two who heard John and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
  41. 41He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is, being interpreted, Christ).
  42. 42He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him, and said, “You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas” (which is by interpretation, Peter).
  43. 43On the next day, he was determined to go out into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
  44. 44Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
  45. 45Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, “We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
  46. 46Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
  47. 47Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and said about him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!”
  48. 48Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
  49. 49Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!”
  50. 50Jesus answered him, “Because I told you, ‘I saw you underneath the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these!”
  51. 51He said to him, “Most certainly, I tell you all, hereafter you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

Chapter 2

  1. 1The third day, there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there.
  2. 2Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the wedding.
  3. 3When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine.”
  4. 4Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”
  5. 5His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.”
  6. 6Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews’ way of purifying, containing two or three metretes apiece.
  7. 7Jesus said to them, “Fill the water pots with water.” So they filled them up to the brim.
  8. 8He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.
  9. 9When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn’t know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom
  10. 10and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!”
  11. 11This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.
  12. 12After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.
  13. 13The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
  14. 14He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting.
  15. 15He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers’ money and overthrew their tables.
  16. 16To those who sold the doves, he said, “Take these things out of here! Don’t make my Father’s house a marketplace!”
  17. 17His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will eat me up.”
  18. 18The Jews therefore answered him, “What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?”
  19. 19Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
  20. 20The Jews therefore said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple! Will you raise it up in three days?”
  21. 21But he spoke of the temple of his body.
  22. 22When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.
  23. 23Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did.
  24. 24But Jesus didn’t entrust himself to them, because he knew everyone,
  25. 25and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

Chapter 3

  1. 1Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.
  2. 2The same came to him by night, and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him.”
  3. 3Jesus answered him, “Most certainly, I tell you, unless one is born anew, he can’t see God’s Kingdom.”
  4. 4Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”
  5. 5Jesus answered, “Most certainly I tell you, unless one is born of water and spirit, he can’t enter into God’s Kingdom.
  6. 6That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
  7. 7Don’t marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born anew.’
  8. 8The wind blows where it wants to, and you hear its sound, but don’t know where it comes from and where it is going. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
  9. 9Nicodemus answered him, “How can these things be?”
  10. 10Jesus answered him, “Are you the teacher of Israel, and don’t understand these things?
  11. 11Most certainly I tell you, we speak that which we know, and testify of that which we have seen, and you don’t receive our witness.
  12. 12If I told you earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
  13. 13No one has ascended into heaven but he who descended out of heaven, the Son of Man, who is in heaven.
  14. 14As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
  15. 15that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
  16. 16For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
  17. 17For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.
  18. 18He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.
  19. 19This is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil.
  20. 20For everyone who does evil hates the light, and doesn’t come to the light, lest his works would be exposed.
  21. 21But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his works may be revealed, that they have been done in God.”
  22. 22After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them and baptized.
  23. 23John also was baptizing in Enon near Salim, because there was much water there. They came, and were baptized;
  24. 24for John was not yet thrown into prison.
  25. 25Therefore a dispute arose on the part of John’s disciples with some Jews about purification.
  26. 26They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, he baptizes, and everyone is coming to him.”
  27. 27John answered, “A man can receive nothing unless it has been given him from heaven.
  28. 28You yourselves testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before him.’
  29. 29He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. This, my joy, therefore is made full.
  30. 30He must increase, but I must decrease.
  31. 31He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
  32. 32What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.
  33. 33He who has received his witness has set his seal to this, that God is true.
  34. 34For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for God gives the Spirit without measure.
  35. 35The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand.
  36. 36One who believes in the Son has eternal life, but one who disobeys the Son won’t see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.”

Chapter 4

  1. 1Therefore when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John
  2. 2(although Jesus himself didn’t baptize, but his disciples),
  3. 3he left Judea and departed into Galilee.
  4. 4He needed to pass through Samaria.
  5. 5So he came to a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son, Joseph.
  6. 6Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being tired from his journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.
  7. 7A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”
  8. 8For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.
  9. 9The Samaritan woman therefore said to him, “How is it that you, being a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)
  10. 10Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”
  11. 11The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. So where do you get that living water?
  12. 12Are you greater than our father, Jacob, who gave us the well and drank from it himself, as did his children and his livestock?”
  13. 13Jesus answered her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again,
  14. 14but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never thirst again; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life.”
  15. 15The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I don’t get thirsty, neither come all the way here to draw.”
  16. 16Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”
  17. 17The woman answered, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You said well, ‘I have no husband,’
  18. 18for you have had five husbands; and he whom you now have is not your husband. This you have said truly.”
  19. 19The woman said to him, “Sir, I perceive that you are a prophet.
  20. 20Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where people ought to worship.”
  21. 21Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe me, the hour comes, when neither in this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, will you worship the Father.
  22. 22You worship that which you don’t know. We worship that which we know; for salvation is from the Jews.
  23. 23But the hour comes, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such to be his worshipers.
  24. 24God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
  25. 25The woman said to him, “I know that Messiah comes, he who is called Christ. When he has come, he will declare to us all things.”
  26. 26Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who speaks to you.”
  27. 27At this, his disciples came. They marveled that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, “What are you looking for?” or, “Why do you speak with her?”
  28. 28So the woman left her water pot, went away into the city, and said to the people,
  29. 29“Come, see a man who told me everything that I did. Can this be the Christ?”
  30. 30They went out of the city, and were coming to him.
  31. 31In the meanwhile, the disciples urged him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”
  32. 32But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you don’t know about.”
  33. 33The disciples therefore said to one another, “Has anyone brought him something to eat?”
  34. 34Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to accomplish his work.
  35. 35Don’t you say, ‘There are yet four months until the harvest?’ Behold, I tell you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, that they are white for harvest already.
  36. 36He who reaps receives wages and gathers fruit to eternal life; that both he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.
  37. 37For in this the saying is true, ‘One sows, and another reaps.’
  38. 38I sent you to reap that for which you haven’t labored. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”
  39. 39From that city many of the Samaritans believed in him because of the word of the woman, who testified, “He told me everything that I did.”
  40. 40So when the Samaritans came to him, they begged him to stay with them. He stayed there two days.
  41. 41Many more believed because of his word.
  42. 42They said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of your speaking; for we have heard for ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world.”
  43. 43After the two days he went out from there and went into Galilee.
  44. 44For Jesus himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.
  45. 45So when he came into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all the things that he did in Jerusalem at the feast, for they also went to the feast.
  46. 46Jesus came therefore again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water into wine. There was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.
  47. 47When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to him, and begged him that he would come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
  48. 48Jesus therefore said to him, “Unless you see signs and wonders, you will in no way believe.”
  49. 49The nobleman said to him, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”
  50. 50Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your son lives.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way.
  51. 51As he was now going down, his servants met him and reported, saying “Your child lives!”
  52. 52So he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. They said therefore to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour, the fever left him.”
  53. 53So the father knew that it was at that hour in which Jesus said to him, “Your son lives.” He believed, as did his whole house.
  54. 54This is again the second sign that Jesus did, having come out of Judea into Galilee.

Chapter 5

  1. 1After these things, there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
  2. 2Now in Jerusalem by the sheep gate, there is a pool, which is called in Hebrew, “Bethesda”, having five porches.
  3. 3In these lay a great multitude of those who were sick, blind, lame, or paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water;
  4. 4for an angel went down at certain times into the pool and stirred up the water. Whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was healed of whatever disease he had.
  5. 5A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
  6. 6When Jesus saw him lying there, and knew that he had been sick for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be made well?”
  7. 7The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I’m coming, another steps down before me.”
  8. 8Jesus said to him, “Arise, take up your mat, and walk.”
  9. 9Immediately, the man was made well, and took up his mat and walked. Now it was the Sabbath on that day.
  10. 10So the Jews said to him who was cured, “It is the Sabbath. It is not lawful for you to carry the mat.”
  11. 11He answered them, “He who made me well said to me, ‘Take up your mat and walk.’”
  12. 12Then they asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take up your mat and walk’?”
  13. 13But he who was healed didn’t know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, a crowd being in the place.
  14. 14Afterward Jesus found him in the temple, and said to him, “Behold, you are made well. Sin no more, so that nothing worse happens to you.”
  15. 15The man went away, and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
  16. 16For this cause the Jews persecuted Jesus, and sought to kill him, because he did these things on the Sabbath.
  17. 17But Jesus answered them, “My Father is still working, so I am working, too.”
  18. 18For this cause therefore the Jews sought all the more to kill him, because he not only broke the Sabbath, but also called God his own Father, making himself equal with God.
  19. 19Jesus therefore answered them, “Most certainly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing of himself, but what he sees the Father doing. For whatever things he does, these the Son also does likewise.
  20. 20For the Father has affection for the Son, and shows him all things that he himself does. He will show him greater works than these, that you may marvel.
  21. 21For as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son also gives life to whom he desires.
  22. 22For the Father judges no one, but he has given all judgment to the Son,
  23. 23that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father. He who doesn’t honor the Son doesn’t honor the Father who sent him.
  24. 24“Most certainly I tell you, he who hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life, and doesn’t come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.
  25. 25Most certainly I tell you, the hour comes, and now is, when the dead will hear the Son of God’s voice; and those who hear will live.
  26. 26For as the Father has life in himself, even so he gave to the Son also to have life in himself.
  27. 27He also gave him authority to execute judgment, because he is a son of man.
  28. 28Don’t marvel at this, for the hour comes in which all who are in the tombs will hear his voice,
  29. 29and will come out; those who have done good, to the resurrection of life; and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of judgment.
  30. 30I can of myself do nothing. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is righteous; because I don’t seek my own will, but the will of my Father who sent me.
  31. 31“If I testify about myself, my witness is not valid.
  32. 32It is another who testifies about me. I know that the testimony which he testifies about me is true.
  33. 33You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.
  34. 34But the testimony which I receive is not from man. However, I say these things that you may be saved.
  35. 35He was the burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
  36. 36But the testimony which I have is greater than that of John, for the works which the Father gave me to accomplish, the very works that I do, testify about me, that the Father has sent me.
  37. 37The Father himself, who sent me, has testified about me. You have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his form.
  38. 38You don’t have his word living in you, because you don’t believe him whom he sent.
  39. 39“You search the Scriptures, because you think that in them you have eternal life; and these are they which testify about me.
  40. 40Yet you will not come to me, that you may have life.
  41. 41I don’t receive glory from men.
  42. 42But I know you, that you don’t have God’s love in yourselves.
  43. 43I have come in my Father’s name, and you don’t receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.
  44. 44How can you believe, who receive glory from one another, and you don’t seek the glory that comes from the only God?
  45. 45“Don’t think that I will accuse you to the Father. There is one who accuses you, even Moses, on whom you have set your hope.
  46. 46For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote about me.
  47. 47But if you don’t believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”

Chapter 6

  1. 1After these things, Jesus went away to the other side of the sea of Galilee, which is also called the Sea of Tiberias.
  2. 2A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.
  3. 3Jesus went up into the mountain, and he sat there with his disciples.
  4. 4Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.
  5. 5Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude was coming to him, said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, that these may eat?”
  6. 6He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.
  7. 7Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may receive a little.”
  8. 8One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
  9. 9“There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?”
  10. 10Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in that place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
  11. 11Jesus took the loaves; and having given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to those who were sitting down; likewise also of the fish as much as they desired.
  12. 12When they were filled, he said to his disciples, “Gather up the broken pieces which are left over, that nothing be lost.”
  13. 13So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves, which were left over by those who had eaten.
  14. 14When therefore the people saw the sign which Jesus did, they said, “This is truly the prophet who comes into the world.”
  15. 15Jesus therefore, perceiving that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
  16. 16When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea.
  17. 17They entered into the boat, and were going over the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.
  18. 18The sea was tossed by a great wind blowing.
  19. 19When therefore they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia, they saw Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing near to the boat; and they were afraid.
  20. 20But he said to them, “It is I. Don’t be afraid.”
  21. 21They were willing therefore to receive him into the boat. Immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.
  22. 22On the next day, the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except the one in which his disciples had embarked, and that Jesus hadn’t entered with his disciples into the boat, but his disciples had gone away alone.
  23. 23However boats from Tiberias came near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
  24. 24When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn’t there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
  25. 25When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
  26. 26Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves, and were filled.
  27. 27Don’t work for the food which perishes, but for the food which remains to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has sealed him.”
  28. 28They said therefore to him, “What must we do, that we may work the works of God?”
  29. 29Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
  30. 30They said therefore to him, “What then do you do for a sign, that we may see and believe you? What work do you do?
  31. 31Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness. As it is written, ‘He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.’ ”
  32. 32Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, it wasn’t Moses who gave you the bread out of heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.
  33. 33For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.”
  34. 34They said therefore to him, “Lord, always give us this bread.”
  35. 35Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will not be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
  36. 36But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don’t believe.
  37. 37All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.
  38. 38For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
  39. 39This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.
  40. 40This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”
  41. 41The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, “I am the bread which came down out of heaven.”
  42. 42They said, “Isn’t this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then does he say, ‘I have come down out of heaven?’ ”
  43. 43Therefore Jesus answered them, “Don’t murmur among yourselves.
  44. 44No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up in the last day.
  45. 45It is written in the prophets, ‘They will all be taught by God.’ Therefore everyone who hears from the Father and has learned, comes to me.
  46. 46Not that anyone has seen the Father, except he who is from God. He has seen the Father.
  47. 47Most certainly, I tell you, he who believes in me has eternal life.
  48. 48I am the bread of life.
  49. 49Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness and they died.
  50. 50This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that anyone may eat of it and not die.
  51. 51I am the living bread which came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. Yes, the bread which I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
  52. 52The Jews therefore contended with one another, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
  53. 53Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don’t have life in yourselves.
  54. 54He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
  55. 55For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
  56. 56He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.
  57. 57As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he who feeds on me, he will also live because of me.
  58. 58This is the bread which came down out of heaven—not as our fathers ate the manna, and died. He who eats this bread will live forever.”
  59. 59He said these things in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
  60. 60Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?”
  61. 61But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said to them, “Does this cause you to stumble?
  62. 62Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
  63. 63It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.
  64. 64But there are some of you who don’t believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn’t believe, and who it was who would betray him.
  65. 65He said, “For this cause I have said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father.”
  66. 66At this, many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
  67. 67Jesus said therefore to the twelve, “You don’t also want to go away, do you?”
  68. 68Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
  69. 69We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
  70. 70Jesus answered them, “Didn’t I choose you, the twelve, and one of you is a devil?”
  71. 71Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.

Chapter 7

  1. 1After these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee, for he wouldn’t walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill him.
  2. 2Now the feast of the Jews, the Feast of Booths, was at hand.
  3. 3His brothers therefore said to him, “Depart from here and go into Judea, that your disciples also may see your works which you do.
  4. 4For no one does anything in secret while he seeks to be known openly. If you do these things, reveal yourself to the world.”
  5. 5For even his brothers didn’t believe in him.
  6. 6Jesus therefore said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.
  7. 7The world can’t hate you, but it hates me, because I testify about it, that its works are evil.
  8. 8You go up to the feast. I am not yet going up to this feast, because my time is not yet fulfilled.”
  9. 9Having said these things to them, he stayed in Galilee.
  10. 10But when his brothers had gone up to the feast, then he also went up, not publicly, but as it were in secret.
  11. 11The Jews therefore sought him at the feast, and said, “Where is he?”
  12. 12There was much murmuring among the multitudes concerning him. Some said, “He is a good man.” Others said, “Not so, but he leads the multitude astray.”
  13. 13Yet no one spoke openly of him for fear of the Jews.
  14. 14But when it was now the middle of the feast, Jesus went up into the temple and taught.
  15. 15The Jews therefore marveled, saying, “How does this man know letters, having never been educated?”
  16. 16Jesus therefore answered them, “My teaching is not mine, but his who sent me.
  17. 17If anyone desires to do his will, he will know about the teaching, whether it is from God, or if I am speaking from myself.
  18. 18He who speaks from himself seeks his own glory, but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.
  19. 19Didn’t Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keeps the law? Why do you seek to kill me?”
  20. 20The multitude answered, “You have a demon! Who seeks to kill you?”
  21. 21Jesus answered them, “I did one work and you all marvel because of it.
  22. 22Moses has given you circumcision (not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers), and on the Sabbath you circumcise a boy.
  23. 23If a boy receives circumcision on the Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me, because I made a man completely healthy on the Sabbath?
  24. 24Don’t judge according to appearance, but judge righteous judgment.”
  25. 25Therefore some of them of Jerusalem said, “Isn’t this he whom they seek to kill?
  26. 26Behold, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Can it be that the rulers indeed know that this is truly the Christ?
  27. 27However we know where this man comes from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he comes from.”
  28. 28Jesus therefore cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, “You both know me, and know where I am from. I have not come of myself, but he who sent me is true, whom you don’t know.
  29. 29I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”
  30. 30They sought therefore to take him; but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.
  31. 31But of the multitude, many believed in him. They said, “When the Christ comes, he won’t do more signs than those which this man has done, will he?”
  32. 32The Pharisees heard the multitude murmuring these things concerning him, and the chief priests and the Pharisees sent officers to arrest him.
  33. 33Then Jesus said, “I will be with you a little while longer, then I go to him who sent me.
  34. 34You will seek me, and won’t find me. You can’t come where I am.”
  35. 35The Jews therefore said among themselves, “Where will this man go that we won’t find him? Will he go to the Dispersion among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?
  36. 36What is this word that he said, ‘You will seek me, and won’t find me;’ and ‘Where I am, you can’t come’?”
  37. 37Now on the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink!
  38. 38He who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, from within him will flow rivers of living water.”
  39. 39But he said this about the Spirit, which those believing in him were to receive. For the Holy Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus wasn’t yet glorified.
  40. 40Many of the multitude therefore, when they heard these words, said, “This is truly the prophet.”
  41. 41Others said, “This is the Christ.” But some said, “What, does the Christ come out of Galilee?
  42. 42Hasn’t the Scripture said that the Christ comes of the offspring of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was?”
  43. 43So a division arose in the multitude because of him.
  44. 44Some of them would have arrested him, but no one laid hands on him.
  45. 45The officers therefore came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and they said to them, “Why didn’t you bring him?”
  46. 46The officers answered, “No man ever spoke like this man!”
  47. 47The Pharisees therefore answered them, “You aren’t also led astray, are you?
  48. 48Have any of the rulers believed in him, or of the Pharisees?
  49. 49But this multitude that doesn’t know the law is cursed.”
  50. 50Nicodemus (he who came to him by night, being one of them) said to them,
  51. 51“Does our law judge a man, unless it first hears from him personally and knows what he does?”
  52. 52They answered him, “Are you also from Galilee? Search, and see that no prophet has arisen out of Galilee.”
  53. 53Everyone went to his own house,

Chapter 8

  1. 1but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
  2. 2Now very early in the morning, he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him. He sat down and taught them.
  3. 3The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman taken in adultery. Having set her in the middle,
  4. 4they told him, “Teacher, we found this woman in adultery, in the very act.
  5. 5Now in our law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. What then do you say about her?”
  6. 6They said this testing him, that they might have something to accuse him of. But Jesus stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
  7. 7But when they continued asking him, he looked up and said to them, “He who is without sin among you, let him throw the first stone at her.”
  8. 8Again he stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
  9. 9They, when they heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning from the oldest, even to the last. Jesus was left alone with the woman where she was, in the middle.
  10. 10Jesus, standing up, saw her and said, “Woman, where are your accusers? Did no one condemn you?”
  11. 11She said, “No one, Lord.” Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way. From now on, sin no more.”
  12. 12Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”
  13. 13The Pharisees therefore said to him, “You testify about yourself. Your testimony is not valid.”
  14. 14Jesus answered them, “Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from, and where I am going; but you don’t know where I came from, or where I am going.
  15. 15You judge according to the flesh. I judge no one.
  16. 16Even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.
  17. 17It’s also written in your law that the testimony of two people is valid.
  18. 18I am one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me.”
  19. 19They said therefore to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
  20. 20Jesus spoke these words in the treasury, as he taught in the temple. Yet no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
  21. 21Jesus said therefore again to them, “I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins. Where I go, you can’t come.”
  22. 22The Jews therefore said, “Will he kill himself, because he says, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come’?”
  23. 23He said to them, “You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world.
  24. 24I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.”
  25. 25They said therefore to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been saying to you from the beginning.
  26. 26I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you. However he who sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these I say to the world.”
  27. 27They didn’t understand that he spoke to them about the Father.
  28. 28Jesus therefore said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.
  29. 29He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn’t left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”
  30. 30As he spoke these things, many believed in him.
  31. 31Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, “If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.
  32. 32You will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”
  33. 33They answered him, “We are Abraham’s offspring, and have never been in bondage to anyone. How do you say, ‘You will be made free’?”
  34. 34Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.
  35. 35A bondservant doesn’t live in the house forever. A son remains forever.
  36. 36If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
  37. 37I know that you are Abraham’s offspring, yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.
  38. 38I say the things which I have seen with my Father; and you also do the things which you have seen with your father.”
  39. 39They answered him, “Our father is Abraham.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.
  40. 40But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth which I heard from God. Abraham didn’t do this.
  41. 41You do the works of your father.” They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father, God.”
  42. 42Therefore Jesus said to them, “If God were your father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from God. For I haven’t come of myself, but he sent me.
  43. 43Why don’t you understand my speech? Because you can’t hear my word.
  44. 44You are of your father, the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and doesn’t stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks on his own; for he is a liar, and the father of lies.
  45. 45But because I tell the truth, you don’t believe me.
  46. 46Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
  47. 47He who is of God hears the words of God. For this cause you don’t hear, because you are not of God.”
  48. 48Then the Jews answered him, “Don’t we say well that you are a Samaritan, and have a demon?”
  49. 49Jesus answered, “I don’t have a demon, but I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
  50. 50But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.
  51. 51Most certainly, I tell you, if a person keeps my word, he will never see death.”
  52. 52Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, as did the prophets; and you say, ‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’
  53. 53Are you greater than our father, Abraham, who died? The prophets died. Who do you make yourself out to be?”
  54. 54Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say that he is our God.
  55. 55You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, ‘I don’t know him,’ I would be like you, a liar. But I know him and keep his word.
  56. 56Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day. He saw it, and was glad.”
  57. 57The Jews therefore said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old! Have you seen Abraham?”
  58. 58Jesus said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, before Abraham came into existence, I AM.”
  59. 59Therefore they took up stones to throw at him, but Jesus was hidden, and went out of the temple, having gone through the middle of them, and so passed by.

Chapter 9

  1. 1As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.
  2. 2His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
  3. 3Jesus answered, “This man didn’t sin, nor did his parents; but, that the works of God might be revealed in him.
  4. 4I must work the works of him who sent me while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work.
  5. 5While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
  6. 6When he had said this, he spat on the ground, made mud with the saliva, anointed the blind man’s eyes with the mud,
  7. 7and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means “Sent”). So he went away, washed, and came back seeing.
  8. 8The neighbors therefore, and those who saw that he was blind before, said, “Isn’t this he who sat and begged?”
  9. 9Others were saying, “It is he.” Still others were saying, “He looks like him.” He said, “I am he.”
  10. 10They therefore were asking him, “How were your eyes opened?”
  11. 11He answered, “A man called Jesus made mud, anointed my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to the pool of Siloam and wash.’ So I went away and washed, and I received sight.”
  12. 12Then they asked him, “Where is he?” He said, “I don’t know.”
  13. 13They brought him who had been blind to the Pharisees.
  14. 14It was a Sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
  15. 15Again therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and I see.”
  16. 16Some therefore of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, because he doesn’t keep the Sabbath.” Others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” There was division among them.
  17. 17Therefore they asked the blind man again, “What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”
  18. 18The Jews therefore didn’t believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and had received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight,
  19. 19and asked them, “Is this your son, whom you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
  20. 20His parents answered them, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
  21. 21but how he now sees, we don’t know; or who opened his eyes, we don’t know. He is of age. Ask him. He will speak for himself.”
  22. 22His parents said these things because they feared the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if any man would confess him as Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.
  23. 23Therefore his parents said, “He is of age. Ask him.”
  24. 24So they called the man who was blind a second time, and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.”
  25. 25He therefore answered, “I don’t know if he is a sinner. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see.”
  26. 26They said to him again, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
  27. 27He answered them, “I told you already, and you didn’t listen. Why do you want to hear it again? You don’t also want to become his disciples, do you?”
  28. 28They insulted him and said, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
  29. 29We know that God has spoken to Moses. But as for this man, we don’t know where he comes from.”
  30. 30The man answered them, “How amazing! You don’t know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.
  31. 31We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God, and does his will, he listens to him.
  32. 32Since the world began it has never been heard of that anyone opened the eyes of someone born blind.
  33. 33If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
  34. 34They answered him, “You were altogether born in sins, and do you teach us?” Then they threw him out.
  35. 35Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and finding him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of God?”
  36. 36He answered, “Who is he, Lord, that I may believe in him?”
  37. 37Jesus said to him, “You have both seen him, and it is he who speaks with you.”
  38. 38He said, “Lord, I believe!” and he worshiped him.
  39. 39Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment, that those who don’t see may see; and that those who see may become blind.”
  40. 40Those of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things, and said to him, “Are we also blind?”
  41. 41Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, ‘We see.’ Therefore your sin remains.

Chapter 10

  1. 1“Most certainly, I tell you, one who doesn’t enter by the door into the sheep fold, but climbs up some other way, is a thief and a robber.
  2. 2But one who enters in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
  3. 3The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name, and leads them out.
  4. 4Whenever he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
  5. 5They will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him; for they don’t know the voice of strangers.”
  6. 6Jesus spoke this parable to them, but they didn’t understand what he was telling them.
  7. 7Jesus therefore said to them again, “Most certainly, I tell you, I am the sheep’s door.
  8. 8All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn’t listen to them.
  9. 9I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out, and will find pasture.
  10. 10The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.
  11. 11I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
  12. 12He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn’t own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep, and scatters them.
  13. 13The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand, and doesn’t care for the sheep.
  14. 14I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I’m known by my own;
  15. 15even as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.
  16. 16I have other sheep, which are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice. They will become one flock with one shepherd.
  17. 17Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life, that I may take it again.
  18. 18No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father.”
  19. 19Therefore a division arose again among the Jews because of these words.
  20. 20Many of them said, “He has a demon, and is insane! Why do you listen to him?”
  21. 21Others said, “These are not the sayings of one possessed by a demon. It isn’t possible for a demon to open the eyes of the blind, is it?”
  22. 22It was the Feast of the Dedication at Jerusalem.
  23. 23It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.
