  12. 12Now I mean this, that each one of you says, “I follow Paul,” “I follow Apollos,” “I follow Cephas,” and, “I follow Christ.”
  13. 13Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul?
  14. 14I thank God that I baptized none of you, except Crispus and Gaius,
  15. 15so that no one should say that I had baptized you into my own name.
  16. 16(I also baptized the household of Stephanas; besides them, I don’t know whether I baptized any other.)
  17. 17For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Good News—not in wisdom of words, so that the cross of Christ wouldn’t be made void.
  18. 18For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
  19. 19For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise. I will bring the discernment of the discerning to nothing.”
  20. 20Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the lawyer of this world? Hasn’t God made foolish the wisdom of this world?
  21. 21For seeing that in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom didn’t know God, it was God’s good pleasure through the foolishness of the preaching to save those who believe.
  22. 22For Jews ask for signs, Greeks seek after wisdom,
  23. 23but we preach Christ crucified: a stumbling block to Jews, and foolishness to Greeks,
  24. 24but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God;
  25. 25because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.
  26. 26For you see your calling, brothers, that not many are wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, and not many noble;
  27. 27but God chose the foolish things of the world that he might put to shame those who are wise. God chose the weak things of the world that he might put to shame the things that are strong.
  28. 28God chose the lowly things of the world, and the things that are despised, and the things that don’t exist, that he might bring to nothing the things that exist,
  29. 29that no flesh should boast before God.
  30. 30Because of him, you are in Christ Jesus, who was made to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption:
  31. 31that, as it is written, “He who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”

Chapter 2

  1. 1When I came to you, brothers, I didn’t come with excellence of speech or of wisdom, proclaiming to you the testimony of God.
  2. 2For I determined not to know anything among you except Jesus Christ and him crucified.
  3. 3I was with you in weakness, in fear, and in much trembling.
  4. 4My speech and my preaching were not in persuasive words of human wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power,
  5. 5that your faith wouldn’t stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.
  6. 6We speak wisdom, however, among those who are full grown, yet a wisdom not of this world nor of the rulers of this world who are coming to nothing.
  7. 7But we speak God’s wisdom in a mystery, the wisdom that has been hidden, which God foreordained before the worlds for our glory,
  8. 8which none of the rulers of this world has known. For had they known it, they wouldn’t have crucified the Lord of glory.
  9. 9But as it is written, “Things which an eye didn’t see, and an ear didn’t hear, which didn’t enter into the heart of man, these God has prepared for those who love him.”
  10. 10But to us, God revealed them through the Spirit. For the Spirit searches all things, yes, the deep things of God.
  11. 11For who among men knows the things of a man, except the spirit of the man, which is in him? Even so, no one knows the things of God, except God’s Spirit.
  12. 12But we received not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit which is from God, that we might know the things that were freely given to us by God.
  13. 13We also speak these things, not in words which man’s wisdom teaches, but which the Holy Spirit teaches, comparing spiritual things with spiritual things.
  14. 14Now the natural man doesn’t receive the things of God’s Spirit, for they are foolishness to him, and he can’t know them, because they are spiritually discerned.
  15. 15But he who is spiritual discerns all things, and he himself is judged by no one.
  16. 16“For who has known the mind of the Lord, that he should instruct him?” But we have Christ’s mind.

Chapter 3

  1. 1Brothers, I couldn’t speak to you as to spiritual, but as to fleshly, as to babies in Christ.
  2. 2I fed you with milk, not with meat; for you weren’t yet ready. Indeed, you aren’t ready even now,
  3. 3for you are still fleshly. For insofar as there is jealousy, strife, and factions among you, aren’t you fleshly, and don’t you walk in the ways of men?
  4. 4For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” aren’t you fleshly?
  5. 5Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants through whom you believed, and each as the Lord gave to him?
  6. 6I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase.
  7. 7So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.
  8. 8Now he who plants and he who waters are the same, but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
  9. 9For we are God’s fellow workers. You are God’s farming, God’s building.
  10. 10According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another builds on it. But let each man be careful how he builds on it.
  11. 11For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.
  12. 12But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or stubble,
  13. 13each man’s work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man’s work is.
  14. 14If any man’s work remains which he built on it, he will receive a reward.
  15. 15If any man’s work is burned, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, but as through fire.
  16. 16Don’t you know that you are a temple of God, and that God’s Spirit lives in you?
  17. 17If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; for God’s temple is holy, which you are.
  18. 18Let no one deceive himself. If anyone thinks that he is wise among you in this world, let him become a fool, that he may become wise.
  19. 19For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He has taken the wise in their craftiness.”
  20. 20And again, “The Lord knows the reasoning of the wise, that it is worthless.”
  21. 21Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours,
  22. 22whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come. All are yours,
  23. 23and you are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.

Chapter 4

  1. 1So let a man think of us as Christ’s servants, and stewards of God’s mysteries.
  2. 2Here, moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful.
  3. 3But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you, or by man’s judgment. Yes, I don’t judge my own self.
  4. 4For I know nothing against myself. Yet I am not justified by this, but he who judges me is the Lord.
  5. 5Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and reveal the counsels of the hearts. Then each man will get his praise from God.
  6. 6Now these things, brothers, I have in a figure transferred to myself and Apollos for your sakes, that in us you might learn not to think beyond the things which are written, that none of you be puffed up against one another.
  7. 7For who makes you different? And what do you have that you didn’t receive? But if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it?
  8. 8You are already filled. You have already become rich. You have come to reign without us. Yes, and I wish that you did reign, that we also might reign with you.
  9. 9For, I think that God has displayed us, the apostles, last of all, like men sentenced to death. For we are made a spectacle to the world, both to angels and men.
  10. 10We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You have honor, but we have dishonor.
  11. 11Even to this present hour we hunger, thirst, are naked, are beaten, and have no certain dwelling place.
  12. 12We toil, working with our own hands. When people curse us, we bless. Being persecuted, we endure.
  13. 13Being defamed, we entreat. We are made as the filth of the world, the dirt wiped off by all, even until now.
  14. 14I don’t write these things to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children.
  15. 15For though you have ten thousand tutors in Christ, you don’t have many fathers. For in Christ Jesus, I became your father through the Good News.
  16. 16I beg you therefore, be imitators of me.
  17. 17Because of this I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways which are in Christ, even as I teach everywhere in every assembly.
  18. 18Now some are puffed up, as though I were not coming to you.
  19. 19But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord is willing. And I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power.
  20. 20For God’s Kingdom is not in word, but in power.
  21. 21What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or in love and a spirit of gentleness?

Chapter 5

  1. 1It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one has his father’s wife.
  2. 2You are arrogant, and didn’t mourn instead, that he who had done this deed might be removed from among you.
  3. 3For I most certainly, as being absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as though I were present, judged him who has done this thing.
  4. 4In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, you being gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,
  5. 5are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
  6. 6Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?
  7. 7Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place.
  8. 8Therefore let’s keep the feast, not with old yeast, neither with the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
  9. 9I wrote to you in my letter to have no company with sexual sinners;
  10. 10yet not at all meaning with the sexual sinners of this world, or with the covetous and extortionists, or with idolaters; for then you would have to leave the world.
  11. 11But as it is, I wrote to you not to associate with anyone who is called a brother who is a sexual sinner, or covetous, or an idolater, or a slanderer, or a drunkard, or an extortionist. Don’t even eat with such a person.
  12. 12For what do I have to do with also judging those who are outside? Don’t you judge those who are within?
  13. 13But those who are outside, God judges. “Put away the wicked man from among yourselves.”

Chapter 6

  1. 1Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?
  2. 2Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters?
  3. 3Don’t you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life?
  4. 4If then you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?
  5. 5I say this to move you to shame. Isn’t there even one wise man among you who would be able to decide between his brothers?
  6. 6But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers!
  7. 7Therefore it is already altogether a defect in you, that you have lawsuits one with another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
  8. 8No, but you yourselves do wrong and defraud, and that against your brothers.
  9. 9Or don’t you know that the unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom? Don’t be deceived. Neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor male prostitutes, nor homosexuals,
  10. 10nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor slanderers, nor extortionists, will inherit God’s Kingdom.
  11. 11Some of you were such, but you were washed. But you were sanctified. But you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and in the Spirit of our God.
  12. 12“All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are expedient. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be brought under the power of anything.
  13. 13“Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods,” but God will bring to nothing both it and them. But the body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.
  14. 14Now God raised up the Lord, and will also raise us up by his power.
  15. 15Don’t you know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be!
  16. 16Or don’t you know that he who is joined to a prostitute is one body? For, “The two”, he says, “will become one flesh.”
  17. 17But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit.
  18. 18Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin that a man does is outside the body,” but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body.
  19. 19Or don’t you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,
  20. 20for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God’s.

Chapter 7

  1. 1Now concerning the things about which you wrote to me: it is good for a man not to touch a woman.
  2. 2But, because of sexual immoralities, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband.
  3. 3Let the husband give his wife the affection owed her, and likewise also the wife her husband.
  4. 4The wife doesn’t have authority over her own body, but the husband. Likewise also the husband doesn’t have authority over his own body, but the wife.
  5. 5Don’t deprive one another, unless it is by consent for a season, that you may give yourselves to fasting and prayer, and may be together again, that Satan doesn’t tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
  6. 6But this I say by way of concession, not of commandment.
  7. 7Yet I wish that all men were like me. However each man has his own gift from God, one of this kind, and another of that kind.
  8. 8But I say to the unmarried and to widows, it is good for them if they remain even as I am.
  9. 9But if they don’t have self-control, let them marry. For it’s better to marry than to burn.
  10. 10But to the married I command—not I, but the Lord—that the wife not leave her husband
  11. 11(but if she departs, let her remain unmarried, or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband not leave his wife.
  12. 12But to the rest I—not the Lord—say, if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live with him, let him not leave her.
  13. 13The woman who has an unbelieving husband, and he is content to live with her, let her not leave her husband.
  14. 14For the unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified in the husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but now they are holy.
  15. 15Yet if the unbeliever departs, let there be separation. The brother or the sister is not under bondage in such cases, but God has called us in peace.
  16. 16For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?
  17. 17Only, as the Lord has distributed to each man, as God has called each, so let him walk. So I command in all the assemblies.
  18. 18Was anyone called having been circumcised? Let him not become uncircumcised. Has anyone been called in uncircumcision? Let him not be circumcised.
  19. 19Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God.
  20. 20Let each man stay in that calling in which he was called.
  21. 21Were you called being a bondservant? Don’t let that bother you, but if you get an opportunity to become free, use it.
  22. 22For he who was called in the Lord being a bondservant is the Lord’s free man. Likewise he who was called being free is Christ’s bondservant.
  23. 23You were bought with a price. Don’t become bondservants of men.
  24. 24Brothers, let each man, in whatever condition he was called, stay in that condition with God.
  25. 25Now concerning virgins, I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my judgment as one who has obtained mercy from the Lord to be trustworthy.
  26. 26Therefore I think that because of the distress that is on us, that it’s good for a man to remain as he is.
  27. 27Are you bound to a wife? Don’t seek to be freed. Are you free from a wife? Don’t seek a wife.
  28. 28But if you marry, you have not sinned. If a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet such will have oppression in the flesh, and I want to spare you.
  29. 29But I say this, brothers: the time is short, that from now on, both those who have wives may be as though they had none;
  30. 30and those who weep, as though they didn’t weep; and those who rejoice, as though they didn’t rejoice; and those who buy, as though they didn’t possess;
  31. 31and those who use the world, as not using it to the fullest. For the mode of this world passes away.
  32. 32But I desire to have you to be free from cares. He who is unmarried is concerned for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;
  33. 33but he who is married is concerned about the things of the world, how he may please his wife.
  34. 34There is also a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares about the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit. But she who is married cares about the things of the world—how she may please her husband.
  35. 35This I say for your own profit; not that I may ensnare you, but for that which is appropriate, and that you may attend to the Lord without distraction.
  36. 36But if any man thinks that he is behaving inappropriately toward his virgin, if she is past the flower of her age, and if need so requires, let him do what he desires. He doesn’t sin. Let them marry.
  37. 37But he who stands steadfast in his heart, having no urgency, but has power over his own will, and has determined in his own heart to keep his own virgin, does well.
  38. 38So then both he who gives his own virgin in marriage does well, and he who doesn’t give her in marriage does better.
  39. 39A wife is bound by law for as long as her husband lives; but if the husband is dead, she is free to be married to whomever she desires, only in the Lord.
  40. 40But she is happier if she stays as she is, in my judgment, and I think that I also have God’s Spirit.

Chapter 8

  1. 1Now concerning things sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.
  2. 2But if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he doesn’t yet know as he ought to know.
  3. 3But if anyone loves God, the same is known by him.
  4. 4Therefore concerning the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that no idol is anything in the world, and that there is no other God but one.
  5. 5For though there are things that are called “gods”, whether in the heavens or on earth; as there are many “gods” and many “lords”;
  6. 6yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we live through him.
  7. 7However, that knowledge isn’t in all men. But some, with consciousness of the idol until now, eat as of a thing sacrificed to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
  8. 8But food will not commend us to God. For neither, if we don’t eat, are we the worse; nor, if we eat, are we the better.
  9. 9But be careful that by no means does this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak.
  10. 10For if a man sees you who have knowledge sitting in an idol’s temple, won’t his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat things sacrificed to idols?
  11. 11And through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes, the brother for whose sake Christ died.
  12. 12Thus, sinning against the brothers, and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.
  13. 13Therefore if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forever more, that I don’t cause my brother to stumble.

Chapter 9

  1. 1Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven’t I seen Jesus Christ, our Lord? Aren’t you my work in the Lord?
  2. 2If to others I am not an apostle, yet at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
  3. 3My defense to those who examine me is this:
  4. 4Have we no right to eat and to drink?
  5. 5Have we no right to take along a wife who is a believer, even as the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?
  6. 6Or have only Barnabas and I no right to not work?
  7. 7What soldier ever serves at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard, and doesn’t eat of its fruit? Or who feeds a flock, and doesn’t drink from the flock’s milk?
  8. 8Do I speak these things according to the ways of men? Or doesn’t the law also say the same thing?
  9. 9For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.” Is it for the oxen that God cares,
  10. 10or does he say it assuredly for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake, because he who plows ought to plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should partake of his hope.
  11. 11If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things?
  12. 12If others partake of this right over you, don’t we yet more? Nevertheless we didn’t use this right, but we bear all things, that we may cause no hindrance to the Good News of Christ.
  13. 13Don’t you know that those who serve around sacred things eat from the things of the temple, and those who wait on the altar have their portion with the altar?
  14. 14Even so the Lord ordained that those who proclaim the Good News should live from the Good News.
  15. 15But I have used none of these things, and I don’t write these things that it may be done so in my case; for I would rather die, than that anyone should make my boasting void.
  16. 16For if I preach the Good News, I have nothing to boast about; for necessity is laid on me; but woe is to me if I don’t preach the Good News.
  17. 17For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward. But if not of my own will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me.
  18. 18What then is my reward? That when I preach the Good News, I may present the Good News of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the Good News.
  19. 19For though I was free from all, I brought myself under bondage to all, that I might gain the more.
  20. 20To the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain those who are under the law;
  21. 21to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law.
  22. 22To the weak I became as weak, that I might gain the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I may by all means save some.
  23. 23Now I do this for the sake of the Good News, that I may be a joint partaker of it.
  24. 24Don’t you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, that you may win.
  25. 25Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.
  26. 26I therefore run like that, not aimlessly. I fight like that, not beating the air,
  27. 27but I beat my body and bring it into submission, lest by any means, after I have preached to others, I myself should be rejected.

Chapter 10

  1. 1Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;
  2. 2and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
  3. 3and all ate the same spiritual food;
  4. 4and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
  5. 5However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
  6. 6Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.
  7. 7Don’t be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.”
  8. 8Let’s not commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.
  9. 9Let’s not test Christ, as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents.
  10. 10Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.
  11. 11Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
  12. 12Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn’t fall.
  13. 13No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
  14. 14Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
  15. 15I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say.
  16. 16The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn’t it a sharing of the body of Christ?
  17. 17Because there is one loaf of bread, we, who are many, are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf of bread.
  18. 18Consider Israel according to the flesh. Don’t those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?
  19. 19What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
  20. 20But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, and not to God, and I don’t desire that you would have fellowship with demons.
  21. 21You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.
  22. 22Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
  23. 23“All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are profitable. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things build up.
  24. 24Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor’s good.
  25. 25Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience,
  26. 26for “the earth is the Lord’s, and its fullness.”
  27. 27But if one of those who don’t believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience.
  28. 28But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” don’t eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For “the earth is the Lord’s, with all its fullness.”
  29. 29Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other’s conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience?
  30. 30If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for something I give thanks for?
  31. 31Whether therefore you eat, or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
  32. 32Give no occasion for stumbling, whether to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;
  33. 33even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.

Chapter 11

  1. 1Be imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ.
  2. 2Now I praise you, brothers, that you remember me in all things, and hold firm the traditions, even as I delivered them to you.
  3. 3But I would have you know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.
  4. 4Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.
  5. 5But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered dishonors her head. For it is one and the same thing as if she were shaved.
  6. 6For if a woman is not covered, let her hair also be cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or be shaved, let her be covered.
  7. 7For a man indeed ought not to have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God, but the woman is the glory of the man.
  8. 8For man is not from woman, but woman from man;
  9. 9for man wasn’t created for the woman, but woman for the man.
  10. 10For this cause the woman ought to have authority over her own head, because of the angels.
  11. 11Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord.
  12. 12For as woman came from man, so a man also comes through a woman; but all things are from God.
  13. 13Judge for yourselves. Is it appropriate that a woman pray to God unveiled?
  14. 14Doesn’t even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?
  15. 15But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her, for her hair is given to her for a covering.
  16. 16But if any man seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither do God’s assemblies.
  17. 17But in giving you this command, I don’t praise you, that you come together not for the better but for the worse.
  18. 18For first of all, when you come together in the assembly, I hear that divisions exist among you, and I partly believe it.
  19. 19For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you.
  20. 20When therefore you assemble yourselves together, it is not the Lord’s supper that you eat.
  21. 21For in your eating each one takes his own supper first. One is hungry, and another is drunken.
  22. 22What, don’t you have houses to eat and to drink in? Or do you despise God’s assembly and put them to shame who don’t have enough? What shall I tell you? Shall I praise you? In this I don’t praise you.
  23. 23For I received from the Lord that which also I delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.
  24. 24When he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “Take, eat. This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me.”
  25. 25In the same way he also took the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink, in memory of me.”
  26. 26For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
  27. 27Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord’s cup in a way unworthy of the Lord will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.
  28. 28But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread, and drink of the cup.
  29. 29For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself if he doesn’t discern the Lord’s body.
  30. 30For this cause many among you are weak and sickly, and not a few sleep.
  31. 31For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged.
  32. 32But when we are judged, we are punished by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
  33. 33Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
  34. 34But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.

Chapter 12

  1. 1Now concerning spiritual things, brothers, I don’t want you to be ignorant.
  2. 2You know that when you were heathen, you were led away to those mute idols, however you might be led.
  3. 3Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God’s Spirit says, “Jesus is accursed.” No one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” but by the Holy Spirit.
  4. 4Now there are various kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.
  5. 5There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord.
  6. 6There are various kinds of workings, but the same God, who works all things in all.
  7. 7But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the profit of all.
  8. 8For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;
  9. 9to another faith, by the same Spirit; and to another gifts of healings, by the same Spirit;
  10. 10and to another workings of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discerning of spirits; to another different kinds of languages; and to another the interpretation of languages.
  11. 11But the one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing to each one separately as he desires.
  12. 12For as the body is one, and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.
  13. 13For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free; and were all given to drink into one Spirit.
  14. 14For the body is not one member, but many.
  15. 15If the foot would say, “Because I’m not the hand, I’m not part of the body,” it is not therefore not part of the body.
  16. 16If the ear would say, “Because I’m not the eye, I’m not part of the body,” it’s not therefore not part of the body.
  17. 17If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be?
  18. 18But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body, just as he desired.
  19. 19If they were all one member, where would the body be?
  20. 20But now they are many members, but one body.
  21. 21The eye can’t tell the hand, “I have no need for you,” or again the head to the feet, “I have no need for you.”
  22. 22No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.
  23. 23Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant propriety;
  24. 24whereas our presentable parts have no such need. But God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part,
  25. 25that there should be no division in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.
  26. 26When one member suffers, all the members suffer with it. When one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
  27. 27Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
  28. 28God has set some in the assembly: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, and various kinds of languages.
  29. 29Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all miracle workers?
  30. 30Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with various languages? Do all interpret?
  31. 31But earnestly desire the best gifts. Moreover, I show a most excellent way to you.

Chapter 13

  1. 1If I speak with the languages of men and of angels, but don’t have love, I have become sounding brass, or a clanging cymbal.
  2. 2If I have the gift of prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge; and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but don’t have love, I am nothing.
  3. 3If I give away all my goods to feed the poor, and if I give my body to be burned, but don’t have love, it profits me nothing.
  4. 4Love is patient and is kind. Love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud,
  5. 5doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil;
  6. 6doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth;
  7. 7bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, and endures all things.
  8. 8Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will be done away with. Where there are various languages, they will cease. Where there is knowledge, it will be done away with.
  9. 9For we know in part and we prophesy in part;
  10. 10but when that which is complete has come, then that which is partial will be done away with.
  11. 11When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I felt as a child, I thought as a child. Now that I have become a man, I have put away childish things.
  12. 12For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, even as I was also fully known.
  13. 13But now faith, hope, and love remain—these three. The greatest of these is love.

Chapter 14

  1. 1Follow after love and earnestly desire spiritual gifts, but especially that you may prophesy.
  2. 2For he who speaks in another language speaks not to men, but to God; for no one understands; but in the Spirit he speaks mysteries.
  3. 3But he who prophesies speaks to men for their edification, exhortation, and consolation.
  4. 4He who speaks in another language edifies himself, but he who prophesies edifies the assembly.
  5. 5Now I desire to have you all speak with other languages, but rather that you would prophesy. For he is greater who prophesies than he who speaks with other languages, unless he interprets, that the assembly may be built up.
  6. 6But now, brothers, if I come to you speaking with other languages, what would I profit you, unless I speak to you either by way of revelation, or of knowledge, or of prophesying, or of teaching?
  7. 7Even things without life, giving a voice, whether pipe or harp, if they didn’t give a distinction in the sounds, how would it be known what is piped or harped?
  8. 8For if the trumpet gave an uncertain sound, who would prepare himself for war?
  9. 9So also you, unless you uttered by the tongue words easy to understand, how would it be known what is spoken? For you would be speaking into the air.
  10. 10There are, it may be, so many kinds of sounds in the world, and none of them is without meaning.
  11. 11If then I don’t know the meaning of the sound, I would be to him who speaks a foreigner, and he who speaks would be a foreigner to me.
  12. 12So also you, since you are zealous for spiritual gifts, seek that you may abound to the building up of the assembly.
  13. 13Therefore let him who speaks in another language pray that he may interpret.
  14. 14For if I pray in another language, my spirit prays, but my understanding is unfruitful.
  15. 15What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also. I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.
  16. 16Otherwise if you bless with the spirit, how will he who fills the place of the unlearned say the “Amen” at your giving of thanks, seeing he doesn’t know what you say?
  17. 17For you most certainly give thanks well, but the other person is not built up.
  18. 18I thank my God, I speak with other languages more than you all.
  19. 19However in the assembly I would rather speak five words with my understanding, that I might instruct others also, than ten thousand words in another language.
  20. 20Brothers, don’t be children in thoughts, yet in malice be babies, but in thoughts be mature.
  21. 21In the law it is written, “By men of strange languages and by the lips of strangers I will speak to this people. They won’t even hear me that way, says the Lord.”
  22. 22Therefore other languages are for a sign, not to those who believe, but to the unbelieving; but prophesying is for a sign, not to the unbelieving, but to those who believe.
  23. 23If therefore the whole assembly is assembled together and all speak with other languages, and unlearned or unbelieving people come in, won’t they say that you are crazy?
  24. 24But if all prophesy, and someone unbelieving or unlearned comes in, he is reproved by all, and he is judged by all.
  25. 25And thus the secrets of his heart are revealed. So he will fall down on his face and worship God, declaring that God is among you indeed.
  26. 26What is it then, brothers? When you come together, each one of you has a psalm, has a teaching, has a revelation, has another language, or has an interpretation. Let all things be done to build each other up.
  27. 27If any man speaks in another language, let it be two, or at the most three, and in turn; and let one interpret.
  28. 28But if there is no interpreter, let him keep silent in the assembly, and let him speak to himself, and to God.
  29. 29Let the prophets speak, two or three, and let the others discern.
  30. 30But if a revelation is made to another sitting by, let the first keep silent.
  31. 31For you all can prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be exhorted.
  32. 32The spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets,
  33. 33for God is not a God of confusion, but of peace, as in all the assemblies of the saints.
  34. 34Let the wives be quiet in the assemblies, for it has not been permitted for them to be talking except in submission, as the law also says,
  35. 35if they desire to learn anything. “Let them ask their own husbands at home, for it is shameful for a wife to be talking in the assembly.”
  36. 36What!? Was it from you that the word of God went out? Or did it come to you alone?
  37. 37If any man thinks himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him recognize the things which I write to you, that they are the commandment of the Lord.
  38. 38But if anyone is ignorant, let him be ignorant.
  39. 39Therefore, brothers, desire earnestly to prophesy, and don’t forbid speaking with other languages.
  40. 40Let all things be done decently and in order.

Chapter 15

  1. 1Now I declare to you, brothers, the Good News which I preached to you, which also you received, in which you also stand,
  2. 2by which also you are saved, if you hold firmly the word which I preached to you—unless you believed in vain.
  3. 3For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,
  4. 4that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
  5. 5and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
  6. 6Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep.
  7. 7Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles,
  8. 8and last of all, as to the child born at the wrong time, he appeared to me also.
  9. 9For I am the least of the apostles, who is not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.
  10. 10But by the grace of God I am what I am. His grace which was given to me was not futile, but I worked more than all of them; yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
  11. 11Whether then it is I or they, so we preach, and so you believed.
  12. 12Now if Christ is preached, that he has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
  13. 13But if there is no resurrection of the dead, neither has Christ been raised.
  14. 14If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain, and your faith also is in vain.
  15. 15Yes, we are also found false witnesses of God, because we testified about God that he raised up Christ, whom he didn’t raise up, if it is so that the dead are not raised.
  16. 16For if the dead aren’t raised, neither has Christ been raised.
  17. 17If Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins.
  18. 18Then they also who are fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
  19. 19If we have only hoped in Christ in this life, we are of all men most pitiable.
  20. 20But now Christ has been raised from the dead. He became the first fruits of those who are asleep.
  21. 21For since death came by man, the resurrection of the dead also came by man.
  22. 22For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.
  23. 23But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then those who are Christ’s, at his coming.
  24. 24Then the end comes, when he will deliver up the Kingdom to God, even the Father, when he will have abolished all rule and all authority and power.
  25. 25For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
  26. 26The last enemy that will be abolished is death.
  27. 27For, “He put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when he says, “All things are put in subjection”, it is evident that he is excepted who subjected all things to him.
  28. 28When all things have been subjected to him, then the Son will also himself be subjected to him who subjected all things to him, that God may be all in all.
  29. 29Or else what will they do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead aren’t raised at all, why then are they baptized for the dead?
  30. 30Why do we also stand in jeopardy every hour?
  31. 31I affirm, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
  32. 32If I fought with animals at Ephesus for human purposes, what does it profit me? If the dead are not raised, then “let’s eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
  33. 33Don’t be deceived! “Evil companionships corrupt good morals.”
  34. 34Wake up righteously, and don’t sin, for some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
  35. 35But someone will say, “How are the dead raised?” and, “With what kind of body do they come?”
  36. 36You foolish one, that which you yourself sow is not made alive unless it dies.
  37. 37That which you sow, you don’t sow the body that will be, but a bare grain, maybe of wheat, or of some other kind.
  38. 38But God gives it a body even as it pleased him, and to each seed a body of its own.
  39. 39All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds.
  40. 40There are also celestial bodies and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial differs from that of the terrestrial.
  41. 41There is one glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differs from another star in glory.
  42. 42So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body is sown perishable; it is raised imperishable.
  43. 43It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
  44. 44It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body and there is also a spiritual body.
  45. 45So also it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living soul.” The last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
  46. 46However that which is spiritual isn’t first, but that which is natural, then that which is spiritual.
  47. 47The first man is of the earth, made of dust. The second man is the Lord from heaven.
  48. 48As is the one made of dust, such are those who are also made of dust; and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
  49. 49As we have borne the image of those made of dust, let’s also bear the image of the heavenly.
  50. 50Now I say this, brothers, that flesh and blood can’t inherit God’s Kingdom; neither does the perishable inherit imperishable.
  51. 51Behold, I tell you a mystery. We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,
  52. 52in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.
  53. 53For this perishable body must become imperishable, and this mortal must put on immortality.
  54. 54But when this perishable body will have become imperishable, and this mortal will have put on immortality, then what is written will happen: “Death is swallowed up in victory.”
  55. 55“Death, where is your sting? Hades, where is your victory?”
  56. 56The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
  57. 57But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
  58. 58Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the Lord’s work, because you know that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.

Chapter 16

  1. 1Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I commanded the assemblies of Galatia, you do likewise.
  2. 2On the first day of every week, let each one of you save, as he may prosper, that no collections are made when I come.
  3. 3When I arrive, I will send whoever you approve with letters to carry your gracious gift to Jerusalem.
  4. 4If it is appropriate for me to go also, they will go with me.
  5. 5But I will come to you when I have passed through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia.
  6. 6But with you it may be that I will stay, or even winter, that you may send me on my journey wherever I go.
  7. 7For I do not wish to see you now in passing, but I hope to stay a while with you, if the Lord permits.
  8. 8But I will stay at Ephesus until Pentecost,
  9. 9for a great and effective door has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
  10. 10Now if Timothy comes, see that he is with you without fear, for he does the work of the Lord, as I also do.
  11. 11Therefore let no one despise him. But set him forward on his journey in peace, that he may come to me; for I expect him with the brothers.
  12. 12Now concerning Apollos, the brother, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers; and it was not at all his desire to come now; but he will come when he has an opportunity.
  13. 13Watch! Stand firm in the faith! Be courageous! Be strong!
  14. 14Let all that you do be done in love.
  15. 15Now I beg you, brothers—you know the house of Stephanas, that it is the first fruits of Achaia, and that they have set themselves to serve the saints—
  16. 16that you also be in subjection to such, and to everyone who helps in the work and labors.
  17. 17I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus; for that which was lacking on your part, they supplied.
  18. 18For they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge those who are like that.
  19. 19The assemblies of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you much in the Lord, together with the assembly that is in their house.
  20. 20All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
  21. 21This greeting is by me, Paul, with my own hand.
  22. 22If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be cursed. Come, Lord!
  23. 23The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
  24. 24My love to all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.
8

2 Corinthians

Epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the assembly of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia:
  2. 2Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
  3. 3Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort;
  4. 4who comforts us in all our affliction, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
  5. 5For as the sufferings of Christ abound to us, even so our comfort also abounds through Christ.
  6. 6But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you the patient enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer.
  7. 7Our hope for you is steadfast, knowing that, since you are partakers of the sufferings, so you are also of the comfort.
  8. 8For we don’t desire to have you uninformed, brothers, concerning our affliction which happened to us in Asia, that we were weighed down exceedingly, beyond our power, so much that we despaired even of life.
  9. 9Yes, we ourselves have had the sentence of death within ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead,
  10. 10who delivered us out of so great a death, and does deliver; on whom we have set our hope that he will also still deliver us;
  11. 11you also helping together on our behalf by your supplication; that, for the gift given to us by means of many, thanks may be given by many persons on your behalf.
  12. 12For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God we behaved ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.
  13. 13For we write no other things to you than what you read or even acknowledge, and I hope you will acknowledge to the end,
  14. 14as also you acknowledged us in part, that we are your boasting, even as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.
  15. 15In this confidence, I was determined to come first to you, that you might have a second benefit,
  16. 16and by you to pass into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and to be sent forward by you on my journey to Judea.
  17. 17When I therefore was thus determined, did I show fickleness? Or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be the “Yes, yes” and the “No, no?”
  18. 18But as God is faithful, our word toward you was not “Yes and no.”
  19. 19For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, by me, Silvanus, and Timothy, was not “Yes and no,” but in him is “Yes.”
  20. 20For however many are the promises of God, in him is the “Yes.” Therefore also through him is the “Amen”, to the glory of God through us.
  21. 21Now he who establishes us with you in Christ and anointed us is God,
  22. 22who also sealed us, and gave us the down payment of the Spirit in our hearts.
  23. 23But I call God for a witness to my soul, that I didn’t come to Corinth to spare you.
  24. 24We don’t control your faith, but are fellow workers with you for your joy. For you stand firm in faith.

Chapter 2

  1. 1But I determined this for myself, that I would not come to you again in sorrow.
  2. 2For if I make you grieve, then who will make me glad but he who is made to grieve by me?
  3. 3And I wrote this very thing to you, so that, when I came, I wouldn’t have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy would be shared by all of you.
  4. 4For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote to you with many tears, not that you should be made to grieve, but that you might know the love that I have so abundantly for you.
  5. 5But if any has caused sorrow, he has caused sorrow, not to me, but in part (that I not press too heavily) to you all.
  6. 6This punishment which was inflicted by the many is sufficient for such a one;
  7. 7so that on the contrary you should rather forgive him and comfort him, lest by any means such a one should be swallowed up with his excessive sorrow.
  8. 8Therefore I beg you to confirm your love toward him.
  9. 9For to this end I also wrote, that I might know the proof of you, whether you are obedient in all things.
  10. 10Now I also forgive whomever you forgive anything. For if indeed I have forgiven anything, I have forgiven that one for your sakes in the presence of Christ,
  11. 11that no advantage may be gained over us by Satan, for we are not ignorant of his schemes.
  12. 12Now when I came to Troas for the Good News of Christ, and when a door was opened to me in the Lord,
  13. 13I had no relief for my spirit, because I didn’t find Titus, my brother, but taking my leave of them, I went out into Macedonia.
  14. 14Now thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph in Christ, and reveals through us the sweet aroma of his knowledge in every place.
  15. 15For we are a sweet aroma of Christ to God, in those who are saved and in those who perish:
  16. 16to the one a stench from death to death, to the other a sweet aroma from life to life. Who is sufficient for these things?
  17. 17For we are not as so many, peddling the word of God. But as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God, we speak in Christ.

Chapter 3

  1. 1Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as do some, letters of commendation to you or from you?
  2. 2You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men,
  3. 3being revealed that you are a letter of Christ, served by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tablets of stone, but in tablets that are hearts of flesh.
  4. 4Such confidence we have through Christ toward God,
  5. 5not that we are sufficient of ourselves, to account anything as from ourselves; but our sufficiency is from God,
  6. 6who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant, not of the letter, but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
  7. 7But if the service of death, written engraved on stones, came with glory, so that the children of Israel could not look steadfastly on the face of Moses for the glory of his face, which was passing away,
  8. 8won’t service of the Spirit be with much more glory?
  9. 9For if the service of condemnation has glory, the service of righteousness exceeds much more in glory.
  10. 10For most certainly that which has been made glorious has not been made glorious in this respect, by reason of the glory that surpasses.
  11. 11For if that which passes away was with glory, much more that which remains is in glory.
  12. 12Having therefore such a hope, we use great boldness of speech,
  13. 13and not as Moses, who put a veil on his face, that the children of Israel wouldn’t look steadfastly on the end of that which was passing away.
  14. 14But their minds were hardened, for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains, because in Christ it passes away.
  15. 15But to this day, when Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.
  16. 16But whenever someone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
  17. 17Now the Lord is the Spirit and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.
  18. 18But we all, with unveiled face seeing the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit.

Chapter 4

  1. 1Therefore seeing we have this ministry, even as we obtained mercy, we don’t faint.
  2. 2But we have renounced the hidden things of shame, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully, but by the manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.
  3. 3Even if our Good News is veiled, it is veiled in those who are dying,
  4. 4in whom the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelieving, that the light of the Good News of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God, should not dawn on them.
  5. 5For we don’t preach ourselves, but Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for Jesus’ sake,
  6. 6seeing it is God who said, “Light will shine out of darkness,” who has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
  7. 7But we have this treasure in clay vessels, that the exceeding greatness of the power may be of God, and not from ourselves.
  8. 8We are pressed on every side, yet not crushed; perplexed, yet not to despair;
  9. 9pursued, yet not forsaken; struck down, yet not destroyed;
  10. 10always carrying in the body the putting to death of the Lord Jesus, that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.
  11. 11For we who live are always delivered to death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus may be revealed in our mortal flesh.
  12. 12So then death works in us, but life in you.
  13. 13But having the same spirit of faith, according to that which is written, “I believed, and therefore I spoke.” We also believe, and therefore we also speak;
  14. 14knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus, and will present us with you.
  15. 15For all things are for your sakes, that the grace, being multiplied through the many, may cause the thanksgiving to abound to the glory of God.
  16. 16Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day.
  17. 17For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory,
  18. 18while we don’t look at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.

Chapter 5

  1. 1For we know that if the earthly house of our tent is dissolved, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal, in the heavens.
  2. 2For most certainly in this we groan, longing to be clothed with our habitation which is from heaven,
  3. 3if indeed being clothed, we will not be found naked.
  4. 4For indeed we who are in this tent do groan, being burdened, not that we desire to be unclothed, but that we desire to be clothed, that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
  5. 5Now he who made us for this very thing is God, who also gave to us the down payment of the Spirit.
  6. 6Therefore we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord;
  7. 7for we walk by faith, not by sight.
  8. 8We are courageous, I say, and are willing rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.
  9. 9Therefore also we make it our aim, whether at home or absent, to be well pleasing to him.
  10. 10For we must all be revealed before the judgment seat of Christ that each one may receive the things in the body according to what he has done, whether good or bad.
  11. 11Knowing therefore the fear of the Lord, we persuade men, but we are revealed to God, and I hope that we are revealed also in your consciences.
  12. 12For we are not commending ourselves to you again, but speak as giving you occasion of boasting on our behalf, that you may have something to answer those who boast in appearance, and not in heart.
  13. 13For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God. Or if we are of sober mind, it is for you.
  14. 14For the love of Christ constrains us; because we judge thus, that one died for all, therefore all died.
  15. 15He died for all, that those who live should no longer live to themselves, but to him who for their sakes died and rose again.
  16. 16Therefore we know no one after the flesh from now on. Even though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now we know him so no more.
  17. 17Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old things have passed away. Behold, all things have become new.
  18. 18But all things are of God, who reconciled us to himself through Jesus Christ, and gave to us the ministry of reconciliation;
  19. 19namely, that God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning to them their trespasses, and having committed to us the word of reconciliation.
  20. 20We are therefore ambassadors on behalf of Christ, as though God were entreating by us: we beg you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.
  21. 21For him who knew no sin he made to be sin on our behalf; so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.

Chapter 6

  1. 1Working together, we entreat also that you do not receive the grace of God in vain,
  2. 2for he says, “At an acceptable time I listened to you. In a day of salvation I helped you.” Behold, now is the acceptable time. Behold, now is the day of salvation.
  3. 3We give no occasion of stumbling in anything, that our service may not be blamed,
  4. 4but in everything commending ourselves, as servants of God, in great endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,
  5. 5in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in watchings, in fastings;
  6. 6in pureness, in knowledge, in perseverance, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in sincere love,
  7. 7in the word of truth, in the power of God; by the armor of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,
  8. 8by glory and dishonor, by evil report and good report; as deceivers, and yet true;
  9. 9as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as punished, and not killed;
  10. 10as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.
  11. 11Our mouth is open to you, Corinthians. Our heart is enlarged.
  12. 12You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted by your own affections.
  13. 13Now in return, I speak as to my children: you also open your hearts.
  14. 14Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers, for what fellowship do righteousness and iniquity have? Or what fellowship does light have with darkness?
  15. 15What agreement does Christ have with Belial? Or what portion does a believer have with an unbeliever?
  16. 16What agreement does a temple of God have with idols? For you are a temple of the living God. Even as God said, “I will dwell in them and walk in them. I will be their God and they will be my people.”
  17. 17Therefore “ ‘Come out from among them, and be separate,’ says the Lord. ‘Touch no unclean thing. I will receive you.
  18. 18I will be to you a Father. You will be to me sons and daughters,’ says the Lord Almighty.”

Chapter 7

  1. 1Having therefore these promises, beloved, let’s cleanse ourselves from all defilement of flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God.
  2. 2Open your hearts to us. We wronged no one. We corrupted no one. We took advantage of no one.
  3. 3I say this not to condemn you, for I have said before that you are in our hearts to die together and live together.
  4. 4Great is my boldness of speech toward you. Great is my boasting on your behalf. I am filled with comfort. I overflow with joy in all our affliction.
  5. 5For even when we had come into Macedonia, our flesh had no relief, but we were afflicted on every side. Fightings were outside. Fear was inside.
  6. 6Nevertheless, he who comforts the lowly, God, comforted us by the coming of Titus,
  7. 7and not by his coming only, but also by the comfort with which he was comforted in you while he told us of your longing, your mourning, and your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.
  8. 8For though I grieved you with my letter, I do not regret it, though I did regret it. For I see that my letter made you grieve, though just for a while.
  9. 9I now rejoice, not that you were grieved, but that you were grieved to repentance. For you were grieved in a godly way, that you might suffer loss by us in nothing.
  10. 10For godly sorrow produces repentance to salvation, which brings no regret. But the sorrow of the world produces death.
  11. 11For behold, this same thing, that you were grieved in a godly way, what earnest care it worked in you. Yes, what defense, indignation, fear, longing, zeal, and vengeance! In everything you demonstrated yourselves to be pure in the matter.
  12. 12So although I wrote to you, I wrote not for his cause that did the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered the wrong, but that your earnest care for us might be revealed in you in the sight of God.
  13. 13Therefore we have been comforted. In our comfort we rejoiced the more exceedingly for the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.
  14. 14For if in anything I have boasted to him on your behalf, I was not disappointed. But as we spoke all things to you in truth, so our glorying also which I made before Titus was found to be truth.
  15. 15His affection is more abundantly toward you, while he remembers all of your obedience, how with fear and trembling you received him.
  16. 16I rejoice that in everything I am confident concerning you.

Chapter 8

  1. 1Moreover, brothers, we make known to you the grace of God which has been given in the assemblies of Macedonia,
  2. 2how in much proof of affliction, the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded to the riches of their generosity.
  3. 3For according to their power, I testify, yes and beyond their power, they gave of their own accord,
  4. 4begging us with much entreaty to receive this grace and the fellowship in the service to the saints.
  5. 5This was not as we had expected, but first they gave their own selves to the Lord, and to us through the will of God.
  6. 6So we urged Titus, that as he had made a beginning before, so he would also complete in you this grace.
  7. 7But as you abound in everything, in faith, utterance, knowledge, all earnestness, and in your love to us, see that you also abound in this grace.
  8. 8I speak not by way of commandment, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity also of your love.
  9. 9For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich.
  10. 10I give a judgment in this: for this is expedient for you who were the first to start a year ago, not only to do, but also to be willing.
  11. 11But now complete the doing also, that as there was the readiness to be willing, so there may be the completion also out of your ability.
  12. 12For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what you have, not according to what you don’t have.
  13. 13For this is not that others may be eased and you distressed,
  14. 14but for equality. Your abundance at this present time supplies their lack, that their abundance also may become a supply for your lack; that there may be equality.
  15. 15As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.”
  16. 16But thanks be to God, who puts the same earnest care for you into the heart of Titus.
  17. 17For he indeed accepted our exhortation, but being himself very earnest, he went out to you of his own accord.
  18. 18We have sent together with him the brother whose praise in the Good News is known throughout all the assemblies.
  19. 19Not only so, but he was also appointed by the assemblies to travel with us in this grace, which is served by us to the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our readiness.
  20. 20We are avoiding this, that any man should blame us concerning this abundance which is administered by us.
  21. 21Having regard for honorable things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
  22. 22We have sent with them our brother, whom we have many times proved earnest in many things, but now much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence which he has in you.
  23. 23As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for you. As for our brothers, they are the apostles of the assemblies, the glory of Christ.
  24. 24Therefore show the proof of your love to them before the assemblies, and of our boasting on your behalf.

Chapter 9

  1. 1It is indeed unnecessary for me to write to you concerning the service to the saints,
  2. 2for I know your readiness, of which I boast on your behalf to those of Macedonia, that Achaia has been prepared for the past year. Your zeal has stirred up very many of them.
  3. 3But I have sent the brothers that our boasting on your behalf may not be in vain in this respect, that, just as I said, you may be prepared,
  4. 4lest by any means, if anyone from Macedonia comes there with me and finds you unprepared, we (to say nothing of you) would be disappointed in this confident boasting.
  5. 5I thought it necessary therefore to entreat the brothers that they would go before to you and arrange ahead of time the generous gift that you promised before, that the same might be ready as a matter of generosity, and not of greediness.
  6. 6Remember this: he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly. He who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
  7. 7Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
  8. 8And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that you, always having all sufficiency in everything, may abound to every good work.
  9. 9As it is written, “He has scattered abroad. He has given to the poor. His righteousness remains forever.”
  10. 10Now may he who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food, supply and multiply your seed for sowing, and increase the fruits of your righteousness,
  11. 11you being enriched in everything to all generosity, which produces thanksgiving to God through us.
  12. 12For this service of giving that you perform not only makes up for lack among the saints, but abounds also through much giving of thanks to God,
  13. 13seeing that through the proof given by this service, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the Good News of Christ and for the generosity of your contribution to them and to all,
  14. 14while they themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, yearn for you by reason of the exceeding grace of God in you.
  15. 15Now thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift!

Chapter 10

  1. 1Now I Paul, myself, entreat you by the humility and gentleness of Christ, I who in your presence am lowly among you, but being absent am bold toward you.
  2. 2Yes, I beg you that I may not, when present, show courage with the confidence with which I intend to be bold against some, who consider us to be walking according to the flesh.
  3. 3For though we walk in the flesh, we don’t wage war according to the flesh;
  4. 4for the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh, but mighty before God to the throwing down of strongholds,
  5. 5throwing down imaginations and every high thing that is exalted against the knowledge of God and bringing every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ,
  6. 6and being in readiness to avenge all disobedience when your obedience is made full.
  7. 7Do you look at things only as they appear in front of your face? If anyone trusts in himself that he is Christ’s, let him consider this again with himself, that even as he is Christ’s, so we also are Christ’s.
  8. 8For even if I boast somewhat abundantly concerning our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up, and not for casting you down, I will not be ashamed,
  9. 9that I may not seem as if I desire to terrify you by my letters.
  10. 10For, “His letters”, they say, “are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech is despised.”
  11. 11Let such a person consider this, that what we are in word by letters when we are absent, such are we also in deed when we are present.
  12. 12For we are not bold to number or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But they themselves, measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are without understanding.
  13. 13But we will not boast beyond proper limits, but within the boundaries with which God appointed to us, which reach even to you.
  14. 14For we don’t stretch ourselves too much, as though we didn’t reach to you. For we came even as far as to you with the Good News of Christ,
  15. 15not boasting beyond proper limits in other men’s labors, but having hope that as your faith grows, we will be abundantly enlarged by you in our sphere of influence,
  16. 16so as to preach the Good News even to the parts beyond you, not to boast in what someone else has already done.
  17. 17But “he who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”
  18. 18For it isn’t he who commends himself who is approved, but whom the Lord commends.

Chapter 11

  1. 1I wish that you would bear with me in a little foolishness, but indeed you do bear with me.
  2. 2For I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy. For I married you to one husband, that I might present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
  3. 3But I am afraid that somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve in his craftiness, so your minds might be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.
  4. 4For if he who comes preaches another Jesus, whom we didn’t preach, or if you receive a different spirit, which you didn’t receive, or a different “good news”, which you didn’t accept, you put up with that well enough.
  5. 5For I reckon that I am not at all behind the very best apostles.
  6. 6But though I am unskilled in speech, yet I am not unskilled in knowledge. No, in every way we have been revealed to you in all things.
  7. 7Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached to you God’s Good News free of charge?
  8. 8I robbed other assemblies, taking wages from them that I might serve you.
  9. 9When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so.
  10. 10As the truth of Christ is in me, no one will stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
  11. 11Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows.
  12. 12But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from those who desire an occasion, that in which they boast, they may be found even as we.
  13. 13For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, masquerading as Christ’s apostles.
  14. 14And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light.
  15. 15It is no great thing therefore if his servants also masquerade as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.
  16. 16I say again, let no one think me foolish. But if so, yet receive me as foolish, that I also may boast a little.
  17. 17That which I speak, I don’t speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting.
  18. 18Seeing that many boast after the flesh, I will also boast.
  19. 19For you bear with the foolish gladly, being wise.
  20. 20For you bear with a man if he brings you into bondage, if he devours you, if he takes you captive, if he exalts himself, or if he strikes you on the face.
  21. 21I speak by way of disparagement, as though we had been weak. Yet in whatever way anyone is bold (I speak in foolishness), I am bold also.
  22. 22Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the offspring of Abraham? So am I.
  23. 23Are they servants of Christ? (I speak as one beside himself.) I am more so: in labors more abundantly, in prisons more abundantly, in stripes above measure, and in deaths often.
  24. 24Five times I received forty stripes minus one from the Jews.
  25. 25Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I suffered shipwreck. I have been a night and a day in the deep.
  26. 26I have been in travels often, perils of rivers, perils of robbers, perils from my countrymen, perils from the Gentiles, perils in the city, perils in the wilderness, perils in the sea, perils among false brothers;
  27. 27in labor and travail, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, and in cold and nakedness.
  28. 28Besides those things that are outside, there is that which presses on me daily: anxiety for all the assemblies.
  29. 29Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and I don’t burn with indignation?
  30. 30If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness.
  31. 31The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ, he who is blessed forever more, knows that I don’t lie.
  32. 32In Damascus the governor under King Aretas guarded the Damascenes’ city, desiring to arrest me.
  33. 33I was let down in a basket through a window by the wall, and escaped his hands.

Chapter 12

  1. 1It is doubtless not profitable for me to boast. For I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.
