  15. 15Don’t count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
  16. 16Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.
  17. 17The greeting of me, Paul, with my own hand, which is the sign in every letter: this is how I write.
  18. 18The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
15

1 Timothy

Pastoral epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ according to the commandment of God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ our hope;
  2. 2to Timothy, my true child in faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
  3. 3As I urged you when I was going into Macedonia, stay at Ephesus that you might command certain men not to teach a different doctrine,
  4. 4and not to pay attention to myths and endless genealogies, which cause disputes, rather than God’s stewardship, which is in faith—
  5. 5but the goal of this command is love, out of a pure heart and a good conscience and sincere faith,
  6. 6from which things some, having missed the mark, have turned away to vain talking,
  7. 7desiring to be teachers of the law, though they understand neither what they say, nor about what they strongly affirm.
  8. 8But we know that the law is good, if a person uses it lawfully,
  9. 9as knowing this, that law is not made for a righteous person, but for the lawless and insubordinate, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,
  10. 10for the sexually immoral, for homosexuals, for slave-traders, for liars, for perjurers, and for any other thing contrary to the sound doctrine,
  11. 11according to the Good News of the glory of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.
  12. 12I thank him who enabled me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he counted me faithful, appointing me to service;
  13. 13although I used to be a blasphemer, a persecutor, and insolent. However, I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.
  14. 14The grace of our Lord abounded exceedingly with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
  15. 15The saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am chief.
  16. 16However, for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first, Jesus Christ might display all his patience for an example of those who were going to believe in him for eternal life.
  17. 17Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, to God who alone is wise, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
  18. 18I commit this instruction to you, my child Timothy, according to the prophecies which were given to you before, that by them you may wage the good warfare,
  19. 19holding faith and a good conscience, which some having thrust away made a shipwreck concerning the faith,
  20. 20of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I delivered to Satan, that they might be taught not to blaspheme.

Chapter 2

  1. 1I exhort therefore, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and givings of thanks be made for all men:
  2. 2for kings and all who are in high places, that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and reverence.
  3. 3For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
  4. 4who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.
  5. 5For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,
  6. 6who gave himself as a ransom for all, the testimony in its own times,
  7. 7to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am telling the truth in Christ, not lying—a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
  8. 8I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands without anger and doubting.
  9. 9In the same way, that women also adorn themselves in decent clothing, with modesty and propriety, not just with braided hair, gold, pearls, or expensive clothing,
  10. 10but with good works, which is appropriate for women professing godliness.
  11. 11Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission.
  12. 12But I don’t permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in quietness.
  13. 13For Adam was formed first, then Eve.
  14. 14Adam wasn’t deceived, but the woman, being deceived, has fallen into disobedience;
  15. 15but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and sanctification with sobriety.

Chapter 3

  1. 1This is a faithful saying: someone who seeks to be an overseer desires a good work.
  2. 2The overseer therefore must be without reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, sensible, modest, hospitable, good at teaching;
  3. 3not a drinker, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous;
  4. 4one who rules his own house well, having children in subjection with all reverence;
  5. 5(but how could someone who doesn’t know how to rule one’s own house take care of God’s assembly?)
  6. 6not a new convert, lest being puffed up he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.
  7. 7Moreover he must have good testimony from those who are outside, to avoid falling into reproach and the snare of the devil.
  8. 8Servants, in the same way, must be reverent, not double-tongued, not addicted to much wine, not greedy for money,
  9. 9holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
  10. 10Let them also first be tested; then let them serve if they are blameless.
  11. 11Their wives in the same way must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, and faithful in all things.
  12. 12Let servants be husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
  13. 13For those who have served well gain for themselves a good standing, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
  14. 14These things I write to you, hoping to come to you shortly;
  15. 15but if I wait long, that you may know how men ought to behave themselves in God’s house, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
  16. 16Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God was revealed in the flesh, justified in the spirit, seen by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, and received up in glory.

Chapter 4

  1. 1But the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to seducing spirits and doctrines of demons,
  2. 2through the hypocrisy of men who speak lies, branded in their own conscience as with a hot iron,
  3. 3forbidding marriage and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
  4. 4For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, if it is received with thanksgiving.
  5. 5For it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer.
  6. 6If you instruct the brothers of these things, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished in the words of the faith, and of the good doctrine which you have followed.
  7. 7But refuse profane and old wives’ fables. Exercise yourself toward godliness.
  8. 8For bodily exercise has some value, but godliness has value in all things, having the promise of the life which is now, and of that which is to come.
  9. 9This saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance.
  10. 10For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we have set our trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.
  11. 11Command and teach these things.
  12. 12Let no man despise your youth; but be an example to those who believe, in word, in your way of life, in love, in spirit, in faith, and in purity.
  13. 13Until I come, pay attention to reading, to exhortation, and to teaching.
  14. 14Don’t neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the elders.
  15. 15Be diligent in these things. Give yourself wholly to them, that your progress may be revealed to all.
  16. 16Pay attention to yourself and to your teaching. Continue in these things, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

Chapter 5

  1. 1Don’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father; the younger men as brothers;
  2. 2the elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, in all purity.
  3. 3Honor widows who are widows indeed.
  4. 4But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn first to show piety toward their own family and to repay their parents, for this is acceptable in the sight of God.
  5. 5Now she who is a widow indeed and desolate, has her hope set on God, and continues in petitions and prayers night and day.
  6. 6But she who gives herself to pleasure is dead while she lives.
  7. 7Also command these things, that they may be without reproach.
  8. 8But if anyone doesn’t provide for his own, and especially his own household, he has denied the faith, and is worse than an unbeliever.
  9. 9Let no one be enrolled as a widow under sixty years old, having been the wife of one man,
  10. 10being approved by good works, if she has brought up children, if she has been hospitable to strangers, if she has washed the saints’ feet, if she has relieved the afflicted, and if she has diligently followed every good work.
  11. 11But refuse younger widows, for when they have grown wanton against Christ, they desire to marry,
  12. 12having condemnation, because they have rejected their first pledge.
  13. 13Besides, they also learn to be idle, going about from house to house. Not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things which they ought not.
  14. 14I desire therefore that the younger widows marry, bear children, rule the household, and give no occasion to the adversary for insulting.
  15. 15For already some have turned away after Satan.
  16. 16If any man or woman who believes has widows, let them relieve them, and don’t let the assembly be burdened, that it might relieve those who are widows indeed.
  17. 17Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and in teaching.
  18. 18For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle the ox when it treads out the grain.” And, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”
  19. 19Don’t receive an accusation against an elder, except at the word of two or three witnesses.
  20. 20Those who sin, reprove in the sight of all, that the rest also may be in fear.
  21. 21I command you in the sight of God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the chosen angels, that you observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing by partiality.
  22. 22Lay hands hastily on no one. Don’t be a participant in other people’s sins. Keep yourself pure.
  23. 23Be no longer a drinker of water only, but use a little wine for your stomach’s sake and your frequent infirmities.
  24. 24Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment, and some also follow later.
  25. 25In the same way also there are good works that are obvious, and those that are otherwise can’t be hidden.

Chapter 6

  1. 1Let as many as are bondservants under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, that the name of God and the doctrine not be blasphemed.
  2. 2Those who have believing masters, let them not despise them because they are brothers, but rather let them serve them, because those who partake of the benefit are believing and beloved. Teach and exhort these things.
  3. 3If anyone teaches a different doctrine, and doesn’t consent to sound words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness,
  4. 4he is conceited, knowing nothing, but obsessed with arguments, disputes, and word battles, from which come envy, strife, insulting, evil suspicions,
  5. 5constant friction of people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. Withdraw yourself from such.
  6. 6But godliness with contentment is great gain.
  7. 7For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out.
  8. 8But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.
  9. 9But those who are determined to be rich fall into a temptation, a snare, and many foolish and harmful lusts, such as drown men in ruin and destruction.
  10. 10For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some have been led astray from the faith in their greed, and have pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
  11. 11But you, man of God, flee these things, and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.
  12. 12Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you confessed the good confession in the sight of many witnesses.
  13. 13I command you before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate testified the good confession,
  14. 14that you keep the commandment without spot, blameless, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
  15. 15which in its own times he will show, who is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings, and Lord of lords.
  16. 16He alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no man has seen, nor can see: to whom be honor and eternal power. Amen.
  17. 17Charge those who are rich in this present world that they not be arrogant, nor have their hope set on the uncertainty of riches, but on the living God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy;
  18. 18that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to share;
  19. 19laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold of eternal life.
  20. 20Timothy, guard that which is committed to you, turning away from the empty chatter and oppositions of what is falsely called knowledge,
  21. 21which some profess, and thus have wandered from the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.
16

2 Timothy

Pastoral epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, according to the promise of the life which is in Christ Jesus,
  2. 2to Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
  3. 3I thank God, whom I serve as my forefathers did, with a pure conscience. How unceasing is my memory of you in my petitions, night and day
  4. 4longing to see you, remembering your tears, that I may be filled with joy;
  5. 5having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you, which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also.
  6. 6For this cause, I remind you that you should stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
  7. 7For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.
  8. 8Therefore don’t be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner; but endure hardship for the Good News according to the power of God,
  9. 9who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal,
  10. 10but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who abolished death, and brought life and immortality to light through the Good News.
  11. 11For this I was appointed as a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.
  12. 12For this cause I also suffer these things. Yet I am not ashamed, for I know him whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to guard that which I have committed to him against that day.
  13. 13Hold the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
  14. 14That good thing which was committed to you, guard through the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.
  15. 15This you know, that all who are in Asia turned away from me, of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
  16. 16May the Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain,
  17. 17but when he was in Rome, he sought me diligently and found me
  18. 18(the Lord grant to him to find the Lord’s mercy in that day); and in how many things he served at Ephesus, you know very well.

Chapter 2

  1. 1You therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
  2. 2The things which you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit the same things to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also.
  3. 3You therefore must endure hardship as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
  4. 4No soldier on duty entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier.
  5. 5Also, if anyone competes in athletics, he isn’t crowned unless he has competed by the rules.
  6. 6The farmer who labors must be the first to get a share of the crops.
  7. 7Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in all things.
  8. 8Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, of the offspring of David, according to my Good News,
  9. 9in which I suffer hardship to the point of chains as a criminal. But God’s word isn’t chained.
  10. 10Therefore I endure all things for the chosen ones’ sake, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
  11. 11This saying is trustworthy: “For if we died with him, we will also live with him.
  12. 12If we endure, we will also reign with him. If we deny him, he also will deny us.
  13. 13If we are faithless, he remains faithful; for he can’t deny himself.”
  14. 14Remind them of these things, charging them in the sight of the Lord, that they don’t argue about words, to no profit, to the subverting of those who hear.
  15. 15Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth.
  16. 16But shun empty chatter, for it will go further in ungodliness,
  17. 17and those words will consume like gangrene, of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus:
  18. 18men who have erred concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past, and overthrowing the faith of some.
  19. 19However God’s firm foundation stands, having this seal, “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let every one who names the name of the Lord depart from unrighteousness.”
  20. 20Now in a large house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of clay. Some are for honor, and some for dishonor.
  21. 21If anyone therefore purges himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, and suitable for the master’s use, prepared for every good work.
  22. 22Flee from youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
  23. 23But refuse foolish and ignorant questionings, knowing that they generate strife.
  24. 24The Lord’s servant must not quarrel, but be gentle toward all, able to teach, patient,
  25. 25in gentleness correcting those who oppose him: perhaps God may give them repentance leading to a full knowledge of the truth,
  26. 26and they may recover themselves out of the devil’s snare, having been taken captive by him to his will.

Chapter 3

  1. 1But know this: that in the last days, grievous times will come.
  2. 2For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
  3. 3without natural affection, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, fierce, not lovers of good,
  4. 4traitors, headstrong, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
  5. 5holding a form of godliness, but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also.
  6. 6For some of these are people who creep into houses and take captive gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,
  7. 7always learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
  8. 8Even as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these also oppose the truth, men corrupted in mind, who concerning the faith are rejected.
  9. 9But they will proceed no further. For their folly will be evident to all men, as theirs also came to be.
  10. 10But you followed my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness,
  11. 11persecutions, and sufferings: those things that happened to me at Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I endured those persecutions. The Lord delivered me out of them all.
  12. 12Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
  13. 13But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.
  14. 14But you remain in the things which you have learned and have been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them.
  15. 15From infancy, you have known the holy Scriptures which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith, which is in Christ Jesus.
  16. 16Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness,
  17. 17that each person who belongs to God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.

Chapter 4

  1. 1I command you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at his appearing and his Kingdom:
  2. 2preach the word; be urgent in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort with all patience and teaching.
  3. 3For the time will come when they will not listen to the sound doctrine, but having itching ears, will heap up for themselves teachers after their own lusts,
  4. 4and will turn away their ears from the truth, and turn away to fables.
  5. 5But you be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do the work of an evangelist, and fulfill your ministry.
  6. 6For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure has come.
  7. 7I have fought the good fight. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith.
  8. 8From now on, the crown of righteousness is stored up for me, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day; and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved his appearing.
  9. 9Be diligent to come to me soon,
  10. 10for Demas left me, having loved this present world, and went to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia; and Titus to Dalmatia.
  11. 11Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.
  12. 12But I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
  13. 13Bring the cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus when you come, and the books, especially the parchments.
  14. 14Alexander, the coppersmith, did much evil to me. The Lord will repay him according to his deeds,
  15. 15of whom you also must beware; for he greatly opposed our words.
  16. 16At my first defense, no one came to help me, but all left me. May it not be held against them.
  17. 17But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me, that through me the message might be fully proclaimed, and that all the Gentiles might hear. So I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
  18. 18And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me for his heavenly Kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
  19. 19Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus.
  20. 20Erastus remained at Corinth, but I left Trophimus at Miletus sick.
  21. 21Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus salutes you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers.
  22. 22The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
17

Titus

Pastoral epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s chosen ones, and the knowledge of the truth which is according to godliness,
  2. 2in hope of eternal life, which God, who can’t lie, promised before time began;
  3. 3but in his own time revealed his word in the message with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior;
  4. 4to Titus, my true child according to a common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.
  5. 5I left you in Crete for this reason, that you would set in order the things that were lacking and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you,
  6. 6if anyone is blameless, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, who are not accused of loose or unruly behavior.
  7. 7For the overseer must be blameless, as God’s steward, not self-pleasing, not easily angered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain;
  8. 8but given to hospitality, a lover of good, sober minded, fair, holy, self-controlled,
  9. 9holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching, that he may be able to exhort in the sound doctrine, and to convict those who contradict him.
  10. 10For there are also many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,
  11. 11whose mouths must be stopped: men who overthrow whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for dishonest gain’s sake.
  12. 12One of them, a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, and idle gluttons.”
  13. 13This testimony is true. For this cause, reprove them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,
  14. 14not paying attention to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.
  15. 15To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.
  16. 16They profess that they know God, but by their deeds they deny him, being abominable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.

Chapter 2

  1. 1But say the things which fit sound doctrine,
  2. 2that older men should be temperate, sensible, sober minded, sound in faith, in love, and in perseverance:
  3. 3and that older women likewise be reverent in behavior, not slanderers nor enslaved to much wine, teachers of that which is good,
  4. 4that they may train the young wives to love their husbands, to love their children,
  5. 5to be sober minded, chaste, workers at home, kind, being in subjection to their own husbands, that God’s word may not be blasphemed.
  6. 6Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober minded.
  7. 7In all things be showing yourself an example of good works. In your teaching, be showing integrity, seriousness, incorruptibility,
  8. 8and soundness of speech that can’t be condemned, that he who opposes you may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us.
  9. 9Exhort servants to be in subjection to their own masters and to be well-pleasing in all things, not contradicting,
  10. 10not stealing, but showing all good fidelity, that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things.
  11. 11For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,
  12. 12instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present age;
  13. 13looking for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ,
  14. 14who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify for himself a people for his own possession, zealous for good works.
  15. 15Say these things and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one despise you.

Chapter 3

  1. 1Remind them to be in subjection to rulers and to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,
  2. 2to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men.
  3. 3For we were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
  4. 4But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love toward mankind appeared,
  5. 5not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,
  6. 6whom he poured out on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Savior;
  7. 7that being justified by his grace, we might be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
  8. 8This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you affirm confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;
  9. 9but shun foolish questionings, genealogies, strife, and disputes about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
  10. 10Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning,
  11. 11knowing that such a one is perverted and sins, being self-condemned.
  12. 12When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me to Nicopolis, for I have determined to winter there.
  13. 13Send Zenas, the lawyer, and Apollos on their journey speedily, that nothing may be lacking for them.
  14. 14Let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.
  15. 15All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
18

Philemon

Epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved fellow worker,
  2. 2to the beloved Apphia, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the assembly in your house:
  3. 3Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
  4. 4I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,
  5. 5hearing of your love and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all the saints,
  6. 6that the fellowship of your faith may become effective in the knowledge of every good thing which is in us in Christ Jesus.
  7. 7For we have much joy and comfort in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.
  8. 8Therefore though I have all boldness in Christ to command you that which is appropriate,
  9. 9yet for love’s sake I rather beg, being such a one as Paul, the aged, but also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.
  10. 10I beg you for my child, whom I have become the father of in my chains, Onesimus,
  11. 11who once was useless to you, but now is useful to you and to me.
  12. 12I am sending him back. Therefore receive him, that is, my own heart,
  13. 13whom I desired to keep with me, that on your behalf he might serve me in my chains for the Good News.
  14. 14But I was willing to do nothing without your consent, that your goodness would not be as of necessity, but of free will.
  15. 15For perhaps he was therefore separated from you for a while, that you would have him forever,
  16. 16no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much rather to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
  17. 17If then you count me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.
  18. 18But if he has wronged you at all or owes you anything, put that to my account.
  19. 19I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self besides).
  20. 20Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in the Lord.
  21. 21Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even beyond what I say.
  22. 22Also, prepare a guest room for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.
  23. 23Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
  24. 24as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
  25. 25The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
Section

Hebrews

19

Hebrews

Epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,
  2. 2has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds.
  3. 3His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, who, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high,
  4. 4having become as much better than the angels as the more excellent name he has inherited is better than theirs.
  5. 5For to which of the angels did he say at any time, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father?” and again, “I will be to him a Father, and he will be to me a Son?”
  6. 6When he again brings in the firstborn into the world he says, “Let all the angels of God worship him.”
  7. 7Of the angels he says, “He makes his angels winds, and his servants a flame of fire.”
  8. 8But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever. The scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your Kingdom.
  9. 9You have loved righteousness and hated iniquity; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.”
  10. 10And, “You, Lord, in the beginning, laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the works of your hands.
  11. 11They will perish, but you continue. They all will grow old like a garment does.
  12. 12You will roll them up like a mantle, and they will be changed; but you are the same. Your years won’t fail.”
  13. 13But which of the angels has he told at any time, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet?”
  14. 14Aren’t they all serving spirits, sent out to do service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?

Chapter 2

  1. 1Therefore we ought to pay greater attention to the things that were heard, lest perhaps we drift away.
  2. 2For if the word spoken through angels proved steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just penalty,
  3. 3how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation—which at the first having been spoken through the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard,
  4. 4God also testifying with them, both by signs and wonders, by various works of power and by gifts of the Holy Spirit, according to his own will?
  5. 5For he didn’t subject the world to come, of which we speak, to angels.
  6. 6But one has somewhere testified, saying, “What is man, that you think of him? Or the son of man, that you care for him?
  7. 7You made him a little lower than the angels. You crowned him with glory and honor.
  8. 8You have put all things in subjection under his feet.” For in that he subjected all things to him, he left nothing that is not subject to him. But now we don’t see all things subjected to him, yet.
  9. 9But we see him who has been made a little lower than the angels, Jesus, because of the suffering of death crowned with glory and honor, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for everyone.
  10. 10For it became him, for whom are all things, and through whom are all things, in bringing many children to glory, to make the author of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
  11. 11For both he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all from one, for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brothers,
  12. 12saying, “I will declare your name to my brothers. Among the congregation I will sing your praise.”
  13. 13Again, “I will put my trust in him.” Again, “Behold, here I am with the children whom God has given me.”
  14. 14Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in the same way partook of the same, that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil,
  15. 15and might deliver all of them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.
  16. 16For most certainly, he doesn’t give help to angels, but he gives help to the offspring of Abraham.
  17. 17Therefore he was obligated in all things to be made like his brothers, that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people.
  18. 18For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted.

Chapter 3

  1. 1Therefore, holy brothers, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession: Jesus,
  2. 2who was faithful to him who appointed him, as also Moses was in all his house.
  3. 3For he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, because he who built the house has more honor than the house.
  4. 4For every house is built by someone; but he who built all things is God.
  5. 5Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were afterward to be spoken,
  6. 6but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house. We are his house, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end.
  7. 7Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says, “Today if you will hear his voice,
  8. 8don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion, like as in the day of the trial in the wilderness,
  9. 9where your fathers tested me and tried me, and saw my deeds for forty years.
  10. 10Therefore I was displeased with that generation, and said, ‘They always err in their heart, but they didn’t know my ways.’
  11. 11As I swore in my wrath, ‘They will not enter into my rest.’ ”
  12. 12Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there might be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God;
  13. 13but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called “today”, lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
  14. 14For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence firm to the end,
  15. 15while it is said, “Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion.”
  16. 16For who, when they heard, rebelled? Wasn’t it all those who came out of Egypt led by Moses?
  17. 17With whom was he displeased forty years? Wasn’t it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
  18. 18To whom did he swear that they wouldn’t enter into his rest, but to those who were disobedient?
  19. 19We see that they weren’t able to enter in because of unbelief.

Chapter 4

  1. 1Let’s fear therefore, lest perhaps anyone of you should seem to have come short of a promise of entering into his rest.
  2. 2For indeed we have had good news preached to us, even as they also did, but the word they heard didn’t profit them, because it wasn’t mixed with faith by those who heard.
  3. 3For we who have believed do enter into that rest, even as he has said, “As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter into my rest;” although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.
  4. 4For he has said this somewhere about the seventh day, “God rested on the seventh day from all his works;”
  5. 5and in this place again, “They will not enter into my rest.”
  6. 6Seeing therefore it remains that some should enter into it, and they to whom the good news was preached before failed to enter in because of disobedience,
  7. 7he again defines a certain day, today, saying through David so long a time afterward (just as has been said), “Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts.”
  8. 8For if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterward of another day.
  9. 9There remains therefore a Sabbath rest for the people of God.
  10. 10For he who has entered into his rest has himself also rested from his works, as God did from his.
  11. 11Let’s therefore give diligence to enter into that rest, lest anyone fall after the same example of disobedience.
  12. 12For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit, of both joints and marrow, and is able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
  13. 13There is no creature that is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him to whom we must give an account.
  14. 14Having then a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let’s hold tightly to our confession.
  15. 15For we don’t have a high priest who can’t be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin.
  16. 16Let’s therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and may find grace for help in time of need.

Chapter 5

  1. 1For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
  2. 2The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.
  3. 3Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself.
  4. 4Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.
  5. 5So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father.”
  6. 6As he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”
  7. 7He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,
  8. 8though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.
  9. 9Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation,
  10. 10named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
  11. 11About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing.
  12. 12For although by this time you should be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the revelations of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.
  13. 13For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby.
  14. 14But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.

Chapter 6

  1. 1Therefore leaving the teaching of the first principles of Christ, let’s press on to perfection—not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, of faith toward God,
  2. 2of the teaching of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.
  3. 3This will we do, if God permits.
  4. 4For concerning those who were once enlightened and tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Spirit,
  5. 5and tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come,
  6. 6and then fell away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance; seeing they crucify the Son of God for themselves again, and put him to open shame.
  7. 7For the land which has drunk the rain that comes often on it and produces a crop suitable for them for whose sake it is also tilled, receives blessing from God;
  8. 8but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is rejected and near being cursed, whose end is to be burned.
  9. 9But, beloved, we are persuaded of better things for you, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak like this.
  10. 10For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work and the labor of love which you showed toward his name, in that you served the saints, and still do serve them.
  11. 11We desire that each one of you may show the same diligence to the fullness of hope even to the end,
  12. 12that you won’t be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and perseverance inherited the promises.
  13. 13For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself,
  14. 14saying, “Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.”
  15. 15Thus, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
  16. 16For men indeed swear by a greater one, and in every dispute of theirs the oath is final for confirmation.
  17. 17In this way God, being determined to show more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the immutability of his counsel, interposed with an oath,
  18. 18that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to take hold of the hope set before us.
  19. 19This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil;
  20. 20where as a forerunner Jesus entered for us, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

Chapter 7

  1. 1For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,
  2. 2to whom also Abraham divided a tenth part of all (being first, by interpretation, “king of righteousness”, and then also “king of Salem”, which means “king of peace”,
  3. 3without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God), remains a priest continually.
  4. 4Now consider how great this man was, to whom even Abraham, the patriarch, gave a tenth out of the best plunder.
  5. 5They indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest’s office have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brothers, though these have come out of the body of Abraham,
  6. 6but he whose genealogy is not counted from them has accepted tithes from Abraham, and has blessed him who has the promises.
  7. 7But without any dispute the lesser is blessed by the greater.
  8. 8Here people who die receive tithes, but there one receives tithes of whom it is testified that he lives.
  9. 9We can say that through Abraham even Levi, who receives tithes, has paid tithes,
  10. 10for he was yet in the body of his father when Melchizedek met him.
  11. 11Now if perfection was through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people have received the law), what further need was there for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, and not be called after the order of Aaron?
  12. 12For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change made also in the law.
  13. 13For he of whom these things are said belongs to another tribe, from which no one has officiated at the altar.
  14. 14For it is evident that our Lord has sprung out of Judah, about which tribe Moses spoke nothing concerning priesthood.
  15. 15This is yet more abundantly evident, if after the likeness of Melchizedek there arises another priest,
  16. 16who has been made, not after the law of a fleshly commandment, but after the power of an endless life;
  17. 17for it is testified, “You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.”
  18. 18For there is an annulling of a foregoing commandment because of its weakness and uselessness
  19. 19(for the law made nothing perfect), and a bringing in of a better hope, through which we draw near to God.
  20. 20Inasmuch as he was not made priest without the taking of an oath
  21. 21(for they indeed have been made priests without an oath), but he with an oath by him that says of him, “The Lord swore and will not change his mind, ‘You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.’ ”
  22. 22By so much, Jesus has become the collateral of a better covenant.
  23. 23Many, indeed, have been made priests, because they are hindered from continuing by death.
  24. 24But he, because he lives forever, has his priesthood unchangeable.
  25. 25Therefore he is also able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, seeing that he lives forever to make intercession for them.
  26. 26For such a high priest was fitting for us: holy, guiltless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;
  27. 27who doesn’t need, like those high priests, to offer up sacrifices daily, first for his own sins, and then for the sins of the people. For he did this once for all, when he offered up himself.
  28. 28For the law appoints men as high priests who have weakness, but the word of the oath which came after the law appoints a Son forever who has been perfected.

Chapter 8

  1. 1Now in the things which we are saying, the main point is this. We have such a high priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens,
  2. 2a servant of the sanctuary and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, not man.
  3. 3For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices. Therefore it is necessary that this high priest also have something to offer.
  4. 4For if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, seeing there are priests who offer the gifts according to the law,
  5. 5who serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things, even as Moses was warned by God when he was about to make the tabernacle, for he said, “See, you shall make everything according to the pattern that was shown to you on the mountain.”
  6. 6But now he has obtained a more excellent ministry, by so much as he is also the mediator of a better covenant, which on better promises has been given as law.
  7. 7For if that first covenant had been faultless, then no place would have been sought for a second.
  8. 8For finding fault with them, he said, “Behold, the days come”, says the Lord, “that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah;
  9. 9not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers, in the day that I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; for they didn’t continue in my covenant, and I disregarded them,” says the Lord.
  10. 10“For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel. After those days,” says the Lord; “I will put my laws into their mind, I will also write them on their heart. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
  11. 11They will not teach every man his fellow citizen, and every man his brother, saying, ‘Know the Lord,’ for all will know me, from their least to their greatest.
  12. 12For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness. I will remember their sins and lawless deeds no more.”
  13. 13In that he says, “A new covenant”, he has made the first old. But that which is becoming old and grows aged is near to vanishing away.

Chapter 9

  1. 1Now indeed even the first covenant had ordinances of divine service and an earthly sanctuary.
  2. 2For a tabernacle was prepared. In the first part were the lamp stand, the table, and the show bread; which is called the Holy Place.
  3. 3After the second veil was the tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies,
  4. 4having a golden altar of incense, and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold, in which was a golden pot holding the manna, Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tablets of the covenant;
  5. 5and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat, of which things we can’t speak now in detail.
  6. 6Now these things having been thus prepared, the priests go in continually into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the services,
  7. 7but into the second the high priest alone, once in the year, not without blood, which he offers for himself, and for the errors of the people.
  8. 8The Holy Spirit is indicating this, that the way into the Holy Place wasn’t yet revealed while the first tabernacle was still standing.
  9. 9This is a symbol of the present age, where gifts and sacrifices are offered that are incapable, concerning the conscience, of making the worshiper perfect,
  10. 10being only (with meats and drinks and various washings) fleshly ordinances, imposed until a time of reformation.
  11. 11But Christ having come as a high priest of the coming good things, through the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this creation,
  12. 12nor yet through the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, entered in once for all into the Holy Place, having obtained eternal redemption.
  13. 13For if the blood of goats and bulls, and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who have been defiled, sanctify to the cleanness of the flesh,
  14. 14how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without defect to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?
  15. 15For this reason he is the mediator of a new covenant, since a death has occurred for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first covenant, that those who have been called may receive the promise of the eternal inheritance.
  16. 16For where a last will and testament is, there must of necessity be the death of him who made it.
  17. 17For a will is in force where there has been death, for it is never in force while he who made it lives.
  18. 18Therefore even the first covenant has not been dedicated without blood.
  19. 19For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of the calves and the goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,
  20. 20saying, “This is the blood of the covenant which God has commanded you.”
  21. 21Moreover he sprinkled the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry in the same way with the blood.
  22. 22According to the law, nearly everything is cleansed with blood, and apart from shedding of blood there is no remission.
  23. 23It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
  24. 24For Christ hasn’t entered into holy places made with hands, which are representations of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us;
  25. 25nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest enters into the holy place year by year with blood not his own,
  26. 26or else he must have suffered often since the foundation of the world. But now once at the end of the ages, he has been revealed to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
  27. 27Inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once, and after this, judgment,
  28. 28so Christ also, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, without sin, to those who are eagerly waiting for him for salvation.

Chapter 10

  1. 1For the law, having a shadow of the good to come, not the very image of the things, can never with the same sacrifices year by year, which they offer continually, make perfect those who draw near.
  2. 2Or else wouldn’t they have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having been once cleansed, would have had no more consciousness of sins?
  3. 3But in those sacrifices there is a yearly reminder of sins.
  4. 4For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.
  5. 5Therefore when he comes into the world, he says, “You didn’t desire sacrifice and offering, but you prepared a body for me.
  6. 6You had no pleasure in whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin.
  7. 7Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come (in the scroll of the book it is written of me) to do your will, O God.’ ”
  8. 8Previously saying, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn’t desire, neither had pleasure in them” (those which are offered according to the law),
  9. 9then he has said, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He takes away the first, that he may establish the second,
  10. 10by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
  11. 11Every priest indeed stands day by day serving and often offering the same sacrifices which can never take away sins,
  12. 12but he, when he had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down on the right hand of God,
  13. 13from that time waiting until his enemies are made the footstool of his feet.
  14. 14For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.
  15. 15The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying,
  16. 16“This is the covenant that I will make with them: ‘After those days,’ says the Lord, ‘I will put my laws on their heart, I will also write them on their mind;’ ” then he says,
  17. 17“I will remember their sins and their iniquities no more.”
  18. 18Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.
  19. 19Having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus,
  20. 20by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh,
  21. 21and having a great priest over God’s house,
  22. 22let’s draw near with a true heart in fullness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and having our body washed with pure water,
  23. 23let’s hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering; for he who promised is faithful.
  24. 24Let’s consider how to provoke one another to love and good works,
  25. 25not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another, and so much the more as you see the Day approaching.
  26. 26For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins,
  27. 27but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and a fierceness of fire which will devour the adversaries.
  28. 28A man who disregards Moses’ law dies without compassion on the word of two or three witnesses.
  29. 29How much worse punishment do you think he will be judged worthy of who has trodden under foot the Son of God, and has counted the blood of the covenant with which he was sanctified an unholy thing, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?
  30. 30For we know him who said, “Vengeance belongs to me;” says the Lord, “I will repay.” Again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
  31. 31It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
  32. 32But remember the former days, in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings;
  33. 33partly, being exposed to both reproaches and oppressions; and partly, becoming partakers with those who were treated so.
  34. 34For you both had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and an enduring one in the heavens.
  35. 35Therefore don’t throw away your boldness, which has a great reward.
  36. 36For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.
  37. 37“In a very little while, he who comes will come, and will not wait.
  38. 38But the righteous will live by faith. If he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.”
  39. 39But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the saving of the soul.

Chapter 11

  1. 1Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.
  2. 2For by this, the elders obtained testimony.
  3. 3By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.
  4. 4By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, through which he had testimony given to him that he was righteous, God testifying with respect to his gifts; and through it he, being dead, still speaks.
  5. 5By faith, Enoch was taken away, so that he wouldn’t see death, and he was not found, because God translated him. For he has had testimony given to him that before his translation he had been well pleasing to God.
  6. 6Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him.
  7. 7By faith, Noah, being warned about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear, prepared a ship for the saving of his house, through which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is according to faith.
  8. 8By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed to go out to the place which he was to receive for an inheritance. He went out, not knowing where he went.
  9. 9By faith, he lived as an alien in the land of promise, as in a land not his own, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise.
  10. 10For he looked for the city which has the foundations, whose builder and maker is God.
  11. 11By faith, even Sarah herself received power to conceive, and she bore a child when she was past age, since she counted him faithful who had promised.
  12. 12Therefore as many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as innumerable as the sand which is by the sea shore, were fathered by one man, and him as good as dead.
  13. 13These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and having confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.
  14. 14For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.
  15. 15If indeed they had been thinking of that country from which they went out, they would have had enough time to return.
  16. 16But now they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.
  17. 17By faith, Abraham, being tested, offered up Isaac. Yes, he who had gladly received the promises was offering up his one and only son,
  18. 18to whom it was said, “Your offspring will be accounted as from Isaac,”
  19. 19concluding that God is able to raise up even from the dead. Figuratively speaking, he also did receive him back from the dead.
  20. 20By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even concerning things to come.
  21. 21By faith, Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.
  22. 22By faith, Joseph, when his end was near, made mention of the departure of the children of Israel, and gave instructions concerning his bones.
  23. 23By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.
  24. 24By faith, Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter,
  25. 25choosing rather to share ill treatment with God’s people than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time,
  26. 26considering the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he looked to the reward.
  27. 27By faith, he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.
  28. 28By faith, he kept the Passover, and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn should not touch them.
  29. 29By faith, they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up.
  30. 30By faith, the walls of Jericho fell down, after they had been encircled for seven days.
  31. 31By faith, Rahab the prostitute didn’t perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace.
  32. 32What more shall I say? For the time would fail me if I told of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets,
  33. 33who through faith subdued kingdoms, worked out righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
  34. 34quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, from weakness were made strong, grew mighty in war, and caused foreign armies to flee.
  35. 35Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection.
  36. 36Others were tried by mocking and scourging, yes, moreover by bonds and imprisonment.
  37. 37They were stoned. They were sawn apart. They were tempted. They were slain with the sword. They went around in sheep skins and in goat skins; being destitute, afflicted, ill-treated—
  38. 38of whom the world was not worthy—wandering in deserts, mountains, caves, and the holes of the earth.
  39. 39These all, having had testimony given to them through their faith, didn’t receive the promise,
  40. 40God having provided some better thing concerning us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.

Chapter 12

  1. 1Therefore let’s also, seeing we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, lay aside every weight and the sin which so easily entangles us, and let’s run with perseverance the race that is set before us,
  2. 2looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.
  3. 3For consider him who has endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, that you don’t grow weary, fainting in your souls.
  4. 4You have not yet resisted to blood, striving against sin.
  5. 5You have forgotten the exhortation which reasons with you as with children, “My son, don’t take lightly the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when you are reproved by him;
  6. 6for whom the Lord loves, he disciplines, and chastises every son whom he receives.”
  7. 7It is for discipline that you endure. God deals with you as with children, for what son is there whom his father doesn’t discipline?
  8. 8But if you are without discipline, of which all have been made partakers, then you are illegitimate, and not children.
  9. 9Furthermore, we had the fathers of our flesh to chasten us, and we paid them respect. Shall we not much rather be in subjection to the Father of spirits, and live?
  10. 10For they indeed, for a few days, punished us as seemed good to them; but he for our profit, that we may be partakers of his holiness.
  11. 11All chastening seems for the present to be not joyous but grievous; yet afterward it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
  12. 12Therefore lift up the hands that hang down and the feeble knees,
  13. 13and make straight paths for your feet, so what is lame may not be dislocated, but rather be healed.
  14. 14Follow after peace with all men, and the sanctification without which no man will see the Lord,
  15. 15looking carefully lest there be any man who falls short of the grace of God, lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and many be defiled by it,
  16. 16lest there be any sexually immoral person, or profane person, like Esau, who sold his birthright for one meal.
  17. 17For you know that even when he afterward desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for a change of mind though he sought it diligently with tears.
  18. 18For you have not come to a mountain that might be touched, and that burned with fire, and to blackness, darkness, storm,
  19. 19the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which those who heard it begged that not one more word should be spoken to them,
  20. 20for they could not stand that which was commanded, “If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned”.
  21. 21So fearful was the appearance that Moses said, “I am terrified and trembling.”
  22. 22But you have come to Mount Zion, and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable multitudes of angels,
  23. 23to the festal gathering and assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, to God the Judge of all, to the spirits of just men made perfect,
  24. 24to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling that speaks better than that of Abel.
  25. 25See that you don’t refuse him who speaks. For if they didn’t escape when they refused him who warned on the earth, how much more will we not escape who turn away from him who warns from heaven,
  26. 26whose voice shook the earth then, but now he has promised, saying, “Yet once more I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.”
  27. 27This phrase, “Yet once more” signifies the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that have been made, that those things which are not shaken may remain.
  28. 28Therefore, receiving a Kingdom that can’t be shaken, let’s have grace, through which we serve God acceptably, with reverence and awe,
  29. 29for our God is a consuming fire.

Chapter 13

  1. 1Let brotherly love continue.
  2. 2Don’t forget to show hospitality to strangers, for in doing so, some have entertained angels without knowing it.
  3. 3Remember those who are in bonds, as bound with them, and those who are ill-treated, since you are also in the body.
  4. 4Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the bed be undefiled; but God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers.
  5. 5Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, “I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.”
  6. 6So that with good courage we say, “The Lord is my helper. I will not fear. What can man do to me?”
  7. 7Remember your leaders, men who spoke to you the word of God, and considering the results of their conduct, imitate their faith.
  8. 8Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
  9. 9Don’t be carried away by various and strange teachings, for it is good that the heart be established by grace, not by food, through which those who were so occupied were not benefited.
  10. 10We have an altar from which those who serve the holy tabernacle have no right to eat.
  11. 11For the bodies of those animals, whose blood is brought into the holy place by the high priest as an offering for sin, are burned outside of the camp.
  12. 12Therefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood, suffered outside of the gate.
  13. 13Let’s therefore go out to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach.
  14. 14For we don’t have here an enduring city, but we seek that which is to come.
  15. 15Through him, then, let’s offer up a sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of lips which proclaim allegiance to his name.
  16. 16But don’t forget to be doing good and sharing, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
  17. 17Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they watch on behalf of your souls, as those who will give account, that they may do this with joy, and not with groaning, for that would be unprofitable for you.
  18. 18Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience, desiring to live honorably in all things.
  19. 19I strongly urge you to do this, that I may be restored to you sooner.
  20. 20Now may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, our Lord Jesus,
  21. 21make you complete in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
  22. 22But I exhort you, brothers, endure the word of exhortation; for I have written to you in few words.
  23. 23Know that our brother Timothy has been freed, with whom, if he comes shortly, I will see you.
  24. 24Greet all of your leaders and all the saints. The Italians greet you.
  25. 25Grace be with you all. Amen.
Section

General Epistles

20

James

General epistle

Chapter 1

  1. 1James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are in the Dispersion: Greetings.
  2. 2Count it all joy, my brothers, when you fall into various temptations,
  3. 3knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
  4. 4Let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.
  5. 5But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach, and it will be given to him.
  6. 6But let him ask in faith, without any doubting, for he who doubts is like a wave of the sea, driven by the wind and tossed.
  7. 7For that man shouldn’t think that he will receive anything from the Lord.
  8. 8He is a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.
  9. 9But let the brother in humble circumstances glory in his high position;
  10. 10and the rich, in that he is made humble, because like the flower in the grass, he will pass away.
  11. 11For the sun arises with the scorching wind and withers the grass, and the flower in it falls, and the beauty of its appearance perishes. So the rich man will also fade away in his pursuits.
  12. 12Blessed is a person who endures temptation, for when he has been approved, he will receive the crown of life, which the Lord promised to those who love him.
  13. 13Let no man say when he is tempted, “I am tempted by God,” for God can’t be tempted by evil, and he himself tempts no one.
  14. 14But each one is tempted when he is drawn away by his own lust and enticed.
  15. 15Then the lust, when it has conceived, bears sin. The sin, when it is full grown, produces death.
  16. 16Don’t be deceived, my beloved brothers.
  17. 17Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom can be no variation, nor turning shadow.
  18. 18Of his own will he gave birth to us by the word of truth, that we should be a kind of first fruits of his creatures.
  19. 19So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;
  20. 20for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.
  21. 21Therefore, putting away all filthiness and overflowing of wickedness, receive with humility the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.
  22. 22But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves.
  23. 23For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man looking at his natural face in a mirror;
  24. 24for he sees himself, and goes away, and immediately forgets what kind of man he was.
  25. 25But he who looks into the perfect law of freedom and continues, not being a hearer who forgets, but a doer of the work, this man will be blessed in what he does.
  26. 26If anyone among you thinks himself to be religious while he doesn’t bridle his tongue, but deceives his heart, this man’s religion is worthless.
  27. 27Pure religion and undefiled before our God and Father is this: to visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained by the world.

Chapter 2

  1. 1My brothers, don’t hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory with partiality.
  2. 2For if a man with a gold ring, in fine clothing, comes into your synagogue, and a poor man in filthy clothing also comes in,
  3. 3and you pay special attention to him who wears the fine clothing and say, “Sit here in a good place;” and you tell the poor man, “Stand there,” or “Sit by my footstool”
  4. 4haven’t you shown partiality among yourselves, and become judges with evil thoughts?
  5. 5Listen, my beloved brothers. Didn’t God choose those who are poor in this world to be rich in faith, and heirs of the Kingdom which he promised to those who love him?
  6. 6But you have dishonored the poor man. Don’t the rich oppress you, and personally drag you before the courts?
  7. 7Don’t they blaspheme the honorable name by which you are called?
  8. 8However, if you fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,” you do well.
  9. 9But if you show partiality, you commit sin, being convicted by the law as transgressors.
  10. 10For whoever keeps the whole law, and yet stumbles in one point, he has become guilty of all.
  11. 11For he who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not commit murder.” Now if you do not commit adultery, but murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.
  12. 12So speak and so do, as men who are to be judged by a law of freedom.
  13. 13For judgment is without mercy to him who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
  14. 14What good is it, my brothers, if a man says he has faith, but has no works? Can faith save him?
  15. 15And if a brother or sister is naked and in lack of daily food,
  16. 16and one of you tells them, “Go in peace. Be warmed and filled;” yet you didn’t give them the things the body needs, what good is it?
  17. 17Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself.
  18. 18Yes, a man will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I will show you my faith by my works.
  19. 19You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe, and shudder.
  20. 20But do you want to know, vain man, that faith apart from works is dead?
  21. 21Wasn’t Abraham our father justified by works, in that he offered up Isaac his son on the altar?
  22. 22You see that faith worked with his works, and by works faith was perfected.
  23. 23So the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him as righteousness,” and he was called the friend of God.
  24. 24You see then that by works, a man is justified, and not only by faith.
  25. 25In the same way, wasn’t Rahab the prostitute also justified by works, in that she received the messengers and sent them out another way?
  26. 26For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.

Chapter 3

  1. 1Let not many of you be teachers, my brothers, knowing that we will receive heavier judgment.
  2. 2For we all stumble in many things. Anyone who doesn’t stumble in word is a perfect person, able to bridle the whole body also.
  3. 3Indeed, we put bits into the horses’ mouths so that they may obey us, and we guide their whole body.
  4. 4Behold, the ships also, though they are so big and are driven by fierce winds, are yet guided by a very small rudder, wherever the pilot desires.
  5. 5So the tongue is also a little member, and boasts great things. See how a small fire can spread to a large forest!
  6. 6And the tongue is a fire. The world of iniquity among our members is the tongue, which defiles the whole body, and sets on fire the course of nature, and is set on fire by Gehenna.
  7. 7For every kind of animal, bird, creeping thing, and sea creature, is tamed, and has been tamed by mankind;
  8. 8but nobody can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
  9. 9With it we bless our God and Father, and with it we curse men who are made in the image of God.
  10. 10Out of the same mouth comes blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to be so.
  11. 11Does a spring send out from the same opening fresh and bitter water?
  12. 12Can a fig tree, my brothers, yield olives, or a vine figs? Thus no spring yields both salt water and fresh water.
  13. 13Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by his good conduct that his deeds are done in gentleness of wisdom.
  14. 14But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, don’t boast and don’t lie against the truth.
  15. 15This wisdom is not that which comes down from above, but is earthly, sensual, and demonic.
  16. 16For where jealousy and selfish ambition are, there is confusion and every evil deed.
  17. 17But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.
  18. 18Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.

Chapter 4

  1. 1Where do wars and fightings among you come from? Don’t they come from your pleasures that war in your members?
  2. 2You lust, and don’t have. You murder and covet, and can’t obtain. You fight and make war. You don’t have, because you don’t ask.
  3. 3You ask, and don’t receive, because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend it on your pleasures.
